question_text: 4/10 @ A-45. Midway through the 4th quarter, Team A lines up in a punt formation and they have five interior linemen numbered 50 - 79. They initially line up in a scrimmage kick formation, but before the snap, back A44 shifts to a position five yards behind the snapper in the clear path from the snapper to the potential kicker. The ball is snapped to A44 who throws a legal forward pass to end A88 who catches the ball at the B-45 and runs out of bounds at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. Because A44 is in the clear path between the snapper and potential kicker, Team A is not in a scrimmage kick formation at the snap. But since Team A already has the required 5 players numbered 50-79, there is no numbering-exception issue, so the formation remains legal.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. Team A is down by 4 points and snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 initially can find no receiver open, but scrambles outside the tackle box and throws complete to A88 at the B-20 and he is downed at the B-10. The play ends with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field.
choice_a: A, 1/Goal, B-10, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-20, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal, B-10, Ready – reset the clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-10, Snap – reset the clock to 0:12. This is a foul by Team B for a substitution infraction. At the option of Team A, the game clock is reset to 0:12 and starts on the snap. Team A can decline the substitution penalty yardage but still accept the clock reset.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-30. Team A lines up to kick a field goal midway through the 4th quarter. The holder and kicker are in place and the ball is snapped to holder A12. A12 hands the ball forward to wingback A44 swinging back around at the B-37. A44 runs to the B-23 where he is downed.
choice_a: Dead ball. Re-kick the field goal attempt.
choice_d: Illegal forward hand. A, 1/15, B-42, Ready.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-23, Running. Under a new 2025 editorial change, the ball remains alive when an offensive player has simulated a kick or is in position to kick a ball held for a place kick by a teammate. The ball may legally be kicked, passed, handed, or advanced.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. Midway through the 1st quarter, A1's pass to A80 is ruled incomplete at the 50 near the sideline. The FJ has a flag down for defensive pass interference on B2 at the same spot. Replays show A80 had control of the ball with his right foot in bounds and maintained control as he went to the ground out of bounds.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Ready - accept penalty for DPI.
choice_c: A, 1/10, 50, Ready - decline penalty for DPI.
choice_d: A, 1/10, 50, Snap - decline penalty for DPI.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Ready - decline penalty for DPI. Whether the pass was complete or incomplete is reviewable. The catch is confirmed, and since the yardage gained on the catch exceeds the flat 10-yard DPI enforcement, Team A takes the catch result and declines the DPI penalty.

question_text: 4/1 @ B-31. Early in the 3rd quarter, Team A had just completed a pass in the field of play and did not substitute before rushing to the line. They snap the ball and QB A1 sneaks to the B-27. There are no flags on the play. B77, who was the 12th player, was trying to get off the field, but was not out of bounds when the snap occurred.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Running. Whether Team B had 12 players on the field at the snap is reviewable, but the one-yard difference the foul would create is not significant in this situation, so replay would not stop the game.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A98 kicks the ball high and B21 gives the "T" signal while the ball is in the air standing one yard in the endzone. The ball lands at the B-5, bounces and then lands in B's end zone where A44 recovers with his knee down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The "T" signal given during a free kick is an invalid signal under the 2025 rule. The ball is dead when the untouched kick touches the ground on or behind Team B's goal line, resulting in a touchback.

question_text: 4/1 @ B-40. Midway through the 4th quarter with the clock running, the offense and defense are set at the line and QB A12 is under center. A12, as a part of his cadence, yells "move" without any action that simulates action at the snap. B75 jumps into the neutral zone causing A77 to react and jump back.
choice_d: B, 1/5, B-45, Ready. Encroachment on B75.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-45, Ready. Under a new 2025 rule, the terms "move" and "stem" are reserved for defensive cadence and may not be used by the offense. A12's cadence including "move" is a dead ball foul for delay of game - disconcerting signals.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the B-47 and runs down to the B-37 and is tackled inbounds. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-35. The clock is stopped at 1:02 for the injury and Team B is out of timeouts. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-32, Ready – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-32, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-32, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 2/5, B-37, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-32, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 40. Team B is out of timeouts, so they are charged with a delay penalty. Since the play did not make the line to gain and the injury timeout is the only reason for stopping the clock, the 10-second runoff option applies. The play clock is set to 40 seconds because of the Team B injury.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the B-47 and runs down to the B-37 and is tackled inbounds. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-35. The clock is stopped at 1:02 for the injury and Team B has one timeout remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-37, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-37, Ready – Play Clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-32, Snap – Play Clock set to 25.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-37, Snap – Play Clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-37, Snap – Play Clock set to 25. Since Team B has a timeout remaining, they are charged with a timeout instead of a delay penalty. Even though the injury was the only reason for stopping the clock, the 10-second runoff option does not apply due to the charged timeout. The clock starts on the snap, and the play clock is set to 25 seconds.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-45. Team B leads 24 -21. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, back A21 catches a swing pass at the B-47 and runs down to the B-33 and is tackled inbounds. After the ball is spotted but not yet ready for play, B55 is moving back to the line of scrimmage, looks to the sideline and then goes down injured at the B-35. The clock was stopped at 1:02 for the 1st down and Team B is out of timeouts. Play Clock?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-28, Snap – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-28, 10-Second Runoff – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-28, Ready – Play Clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-28, Snap – Play Clock set to 25.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-28, Snap – Play Clock set to 40. Team B is charged with a delay penalty since they are out of timeouts. Since the clock was already stopped for the 1st down, the injury was not the sole reason to stop the clock, so no 10-second runoff applies. After the 5-yard penalty, the clock starts on the snap at Team A's option, and the play clock is set to 40 seconds because of the Team B injury.

tags: injury timeout;2025 rule change;10-second runoff;end of game;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v3;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, neither team has a timeout remaining and Team B leads by 3 points. Team A completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. A88 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:05 on the game clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, 10-Second Runoff – Game Over. Team B has the option of a 10-second runoff, and the game is over when the option is exercised. This exception, introduced in 2024 and now codified for 2025, applies when a player injury occurs on a play with a temporary clock stoppage for a first down, and the clock is stopped with 10 seconds or less remaining in the 2nd or 4th quarters.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, neither team has a timeout remaining and Team B leads by 3 points. Team A completes a pass over the middle and A88 is tackled inbounds at the B-15 for a first down. A88 remains on the ground and the Referee declares an official's timeout for injury. There is 0:15 on the game clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff since the clock was stopped for the 1st down and the injury timeout was not the only reason to stop the clock. This play also does not meet the codified exception since more than 10 seconds remain on the game clock. The clock starts on the Referee's signal following the injury timeout.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. During the second possession series of the second extra period with the score tied, B25 intercepts A12's pass and carries the ball across Team A's goal line. During the run, B79 blocks below the waist at midfield.
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B stands. Third extra period begins with a kickoff.
choice_b: No Touchdown. Penalty carries to the third extra period.
choice_c: No Touchdown, the second extra period is over and the third extra period will begin with a Try @ 3-yard line (1-point only).
choice_d: No Touchdown, the second extra period is over and the third extra period will begin with a 2-Point Try @ 3-yard line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No Touchdown, the second extra period is over and the third extra period will begin with a 2-Point Try @ 3-yard line. After a change of team possession in extra periods, penalties by either team are declined by rule and scores by the fouling team are cancelled. Since the block was not a flagrant personal foul, it does not carry over. Starting with the 3rd extra period, tries must be attempted from the 2-point (3-yard line) distance.

question_text: 2/G @ B-8. Early in the 3rd quarter, A80 attempts to catch a pass near the pylon, but the ball hits the ground out of bounds and the officials rule incomplete. Replays show A80 gain control, get two feet down and reach the ball for the goal line pylon before it comes loose. It is not clear whether the ball broke the plane of the goal line or not before the ball came loose or where the ball went out of bounds in relation to the pylon.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/G, B 1/2, Ready. Whether the pass was complete and the ball's relation to the goal line/pylon are both reviewable. The receiver completes the process of the catch by reaching for the goal line, an act common to the game, so replay overturns to a completed pass. Without clear evidence on the goal line/pylon aspect, replay defaults to no score and places the ball short of the line.

tags: instant replay;illegal forward pass;line of scrimmage;replay-added foul;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v3;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 @ A-25. With 1:05 remaining in the 4th quarter, Team A trails 24-17 and has one timeout remaining. QB A1 scrambles near the LOS and throws a forward pass to A80 who catches it and runs out of bounds at the A-45 (0:58). There are no flags on the play. Replays show A1 was at the A-27 when he threw the pass.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/13, A-22, Snap - Clock at 0:58. Whether A1 was beyond the line of scrimmage when the pass was thrown is reviewable; the ball and his entire body must be beyond it at release for the pass to be illegal. Replay adds the foul, enforced from the spot of the pass with loss of down. There is no 10-second runoff since the pass was completed.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B21 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B21 catches the kick right at the goal line and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The "T signal" is an invalid fair catch signal, so the ball is dead when caught. The crew should shut the play down after B21 gains possession; the return doesn't count.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the second quarter and the clock running, Team A has no timeouts remaining. Ball carrier A12 throws a backward pass out of bounds from the B-32 to conserve time.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/7, B-37, 10-Second Runoff. The penalty is 5 yards from the spot of the foul and loss of down. Because this play occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, this foul comes under the 10-second runoff rule.

question_text: 2/20 @ B-40. A44 takes the handoff and gains 12 yards on the play. During the down B55's helmet comes off without a helmet foul by Team A. B55 immediately picks up his helmet, puts it back on and then chases the ball carrier and is almost in on the tackle.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. No foul, B55 legally re-entered by replacing his helmet.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, Ready. The action by B55 is a personal foul for continuing to participate after losing his helmet; the rule does not allow him to put the helmet back on and continue playing. The clock stops at the end of the down, and B55 must leave the game for the next down.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. Back A22 takes a backward pass from the quarterback, circles right end, and heads for the goal line. Guard A66, who had pulled out to lead the play, legally blocks B90 to the ground and then stands over him at the B-30 taunting and screaming obscenities. This draws a flag from the head line-judge, when A22 is at the B-10 before continuing into the end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-45, Snap. A66 commits a live-ball foul for unsportsmanlike conduct. The touchdown counts; the 15-yard penalty is enforced at the spot of the foul, the B-30. The clock starts on the snap after the score.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-30. B55 is lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. Back A22 takes the ball on a quick play up the middle, bursts into the open, and at the B-10 he turns around and prances backward into the end zone. The head line-judge and the line judge both have flags for the offside foul, and the back judge drops his flag for the action by A22.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/7, B-30, Snap. The offside by B55 and the unsportsmanlike conduct foul by A22 offset. The down is replayed at the previous spot; the clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/5 @ A-30. Snapper A55 lifts or moves the ball forward before moving it backward to start the snap. B52 bats the ball, causing it to roll loose with B73 recovering at the A-31.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-25, Ready. The action by A55 is an illegal snap and the ball remains dead; nothing that happens afterward is live. The penalty is enforced 5 yards from the succeeding spot.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-15. Team A's backward pass in flight is batted by B51 at the A-10, and the ball goes out of bounds behind Team A's goal line.
choice_b: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety. A, Free Kick, A-20, FK Rules. The bat by B51 is legal since a pass may be batted in any direction by a defensive player. The impetus is charged to Team A's pass, and the result of the play is a safety.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. After intercepting a legal forward pass at the B-20, B21 fumbles at the B-38. At the B-35, B44 intentionally bats the loose ball, which goes forward and out of bounds at the B-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. B44 is guilty of illegally batting the ball. The penalty is enforced 10 yards from the spot of the foul, and Team B keeps the ball. No loss of down is included since Team B is awarded a new series after the penalty enforcement.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter with the score tied at 14, receiver A80 catches a pass and runs to the B-28 where he is hit by B8 (0:40). There are no flags on the play. Replay stops the game to review the contact by B8 for targeting (0:34). Replays show during the tackle B8 lowered his head and attacked with the crown of the helmet, making forcible contact to A80's chest.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, snap/ready at Team A's option.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-14, reset clock to 0:40, snap/ready at Team A's option.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:34, snap/ready at Team A's option.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, 10-Second Runoff.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, snap/ready at Team A's option. Replay confirms crown-of-the-helmet forcible contact, so the targeting foul is added and B8 is disqualified. The clock is reset to the time of the foul, 0:40, and Team A has the snap/ready option.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter with the score tied at 14, receiver A80 catches a pass and runs to the B-28 where he is hit by B8 (0:40). There are no flags on the play. Replay stops the game to review the contact by B8 for targeting (0:34). Replays show after A80 was clearly on the ground, B8 drove his shoulder into the back of A80's head with force.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, snap/ready at Team A's option.
choice_b: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, potential 10-second runoff with Team A option, snap/ready depending on runoff.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-14, reset clock to 0:40, potential 10-second runoff with Team A option.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:34, potential 10-second runoff with Team A option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-14, DQ B8, reset clock to 0:40, potential 10-second runoff with Team A option, snap/ready depending on runoff. Replay confirms the late hit constitutes targeting, and B8 is disqualified. Since this is a dead-ball foul after the Two-Minute Timeout that causes the clock to stop immediately, a 10-second runoff option is added, with Team A choosing whether to exercise it.

tags: instant replay;out of bounds;10-second runoff;end of game;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v4;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter, Team A trails 27-24 and is out of timeouts. Receiver A80 catches a pass and is tackled at the B-32 as he attempts to get out of bounds (0:09). The Line Judge stops the clock ruling A80 did get out of bounds. Replays show A80's left knee hitting down inbounds first.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Game over, Team B wins. Replay can review whether a runner was in or out of bounds for clock purposes when 10 or fewer seconds remain and the runner is down. Since A80 was actually down inbounds, a 10-second runoff applies, and with no time left, the game is over.

tags: late substitution;2025 rule change;delay of game;no do-over;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/5 @ B-40. After the ball is ready for play with the clock running and the umpire (or center judge) in his regular position, Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second and snaps the ball. The umpire (or center judge) is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. The officials get the play shut down as A12 is handing off to A44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10, B-45, Ready. The play is shut down, the game clock is stopped, and the defense is allowed to substitute in response to Team A's late substitutions. Team A is penalized 5 yards for delay of game; there is no longer a "do over" under the 2025 rule. The play clock is set to 25 seconds and starts on the ready-for-play signal.

tags: field goal;substitution mechanics;end of half;defense reasonably expects;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-32. Late in the first half, Team A is out of timeouts. The 3rd down pass is complete and ends inbounds at the B-25 short of the line to gain with the game clock showing 0:10. Facing fourth down and three, Team A immediately hurries its field goal team onto the field. Team A gets set and kicks a successful FG as time expires.
choice_a: Score 3 points for Team A. Halftime.
choice_b: No score. Delay of game, Team A penalized.
choice_c: Score 3 points for Team A. Untimed down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score 3 points for Team A. Halftime. Team B should reasonably expect Team A to attempt a field goal in this situation and should have its field-goal defense ready. The Referee will not go into substitution mechanics and the Umpire will not stand over the ball, since there is no issue of the defense being uncertain about the next play.

tags: field goal;substitution mechanics;delay of game;10-second runoff;end of game;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/G @ B-10. Late in the game, Team A trails by 3 points and A12 completes a pass to A88 who is downed at the B-2 inbounds. The running clock is at 39 seconds when A88 is downed, and the Team A offense stays on the field to run a 4th down play. When the game clock gets to 15 seconds, the Team A coach decides to try a field goal and then rushes the field goal unit onto the field. The Referee goes into the "T" indicating substitution mechanics and before the Center Judge (or Umpire) can get to the ball, the center snaps the ball with 8 seconds remaining on the game clock. The Referee shuts the play down.
choice_a: Score 3 points for Team A. Game over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/G, B-7, 10-Second Runoff. Game over. Team A did not immediately substitute their kicking team, so the Referee properly initiates substitution mechanics. Since the ball is snapped before the Center Judge is in place during Team B's matching-up window, the play is shut down and Team A is penalized 5 yards for delay of game. Since the foul stopped the clock immediately, Team B selects a 10-second runoff, and with the clock at 8 seconds, the game is over.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-45. With the clock running, Team A hustles to the line and snapper A55 snaps the ball before the ball is made ready for play. A12 muffs the snap and B51 recovers the ball at the B-44.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/6, 50, Ready. This is a dead-ball foul, Team A delay of game, since the ball was snapped before it was ready for play. The ball never becomes alive, so B51's recovery does not count. The penalty is enforced 5 yards from the succeeding spot, and it remains Team A's ball.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-8. Runner A44 is hit at the B-5 and fumbles, and B54 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-3 back into his own end zone. A88 sees the loose ball and recovers while the ball is still in the end zone.
choice_b: No score. B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. 4th down fumble rule voids the recovery.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-20, FK Rules. The bat is legal since B54 batted the ball backward, but B54 adds a new impetus and is responsible for the ball's progress into Team B's own end zone. Although the general 4th down fumble rule would normally limit Team A's recovery, the safety rule's specific intent for this exact scenario supersedes it, and the result is a safety.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-8. Runner A44 is hit at the B-5 and fumbles, and B54 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-3 back into his own end zone. B21 sees the loose ball, dives and recovers the ball while grounded while the ball is still in the end zone.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-20, FK Rules.
choice_d: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-20, FK Rules. The bat is legal, but B54 adds a new impetus responsible for the ball's progress into Team B's own end zone. When B21 recovers while grounded, the ball is declared dead there, and since B54 is responsible for the ball being in the end zone, the result is a safety.

tags: batting;new impetus;advance out of end zone;no safety;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/Goal @ B-8. Runner A44 is hit at the B-5 and fumbles, and B54 bats the grounded loose ball at the B-3 back into his own end zone. B21 sees the loose ball, and recovers the ball while it is still in the end zone. B21 advances to the B-10 where he is tackled.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B, FK, B-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-10, Snap. The bat is legal, but B54 adds a new impetus into Team B's own end zone. Since B21 recovers and successfully advances the ball back into the field of play before being downed, there is no safety; the ball simply belongs to Team B at the dead-ball spot.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-7. A36 intends to punt from behind its goal line, but he muffs the ball. After A36 recovers the ball, he runs into the pylon at the intersection of the sideline and goal line. When A36's foot first makes contact with the pylon, the forward nose of the football is just breaking the plane of the goal line back in the field of play.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, FK, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, FK, A-20, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B. A, FK, A-20, FK Rules. The ball is dead at its most forward point when A36 contacts the pylon. Since only the nose of the ball has broken the plane, the entire ball is not beyond the goal line, so the ball remains in the end zone and the result is a safety.

tags: instant replay;out of bounds;coach's challenge;field goal range;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ B-43. Early in the 2nd quarter QB A1 scrambles to the B-35 where he is pushed out of bounds. Team A brings the FG team out, but before the snap the Team B head coach calls timeout to challenge the out of bounds spot. Replays show A1 stepped out of bounds at the B-39.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-39, Ready. The out of bounds spot is reviewable regardless of the line to gain in this situation. When on-field officials allow a runner to advance the ball after potentially stepping out of bounds, replay can always return to that out of bounds spot; with field goal range an issue here, replay stops the game before the coach's challenge.

tags: instant replay;illegal forward pass;quarterback out of bounds;replay-added foul;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v5;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 2nd quarter the score is tied. QB A1 scrambles to the B-28 near the sideline and throws a forward pass to receiver A80 who catches it and runs to the B-10 where he is tackled in bounds (1:23). The Referee rules that A1 stepped out of bounds before throwing the pass (1:28). Replays show A1 was in bounds when the ball was released.
choice_a: A, 3/13, B-33, 1:23 Ready or Snap at Team B's option.
choice_d: A, 3/13, B-33, 1:23 Ready or Snap at Team A's option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/13, B-33, 1:23 Ready or Snap at Team B's option. Whether A1 was out of bounds prior to the pass and where the pass was thrown in relation to the line of scrimmage are both reviewable. Replay creates a foul for an illegal forward pass instead. There is no 10-second runoff since the illegal pass was completed, and the defense gets the snap/ready option.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score late in the fourth quarter and after the Two-Minute Timeout. When ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play, the game clock reads 1:35. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-30. On the play, A22 gained three yards.
choice_a: A, 2/14, A-18, Snap or Ready option of Team B.
choice_b: A, 2/14, A-18, Snap or Ready option of Team B – or – A, 3/4, A-28, Snap or Ready option of Team B.
choice_c: A, 2/9, A-23, Snap or Ready option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/14, A-18, Snap or Ready option of Team B – or – A, 3/4, A-28, Snap or Ready option of Team B. Because the foul at the A-30 occurred beyond the end of A22's run (the A-28, the basic spot), the penalty is enforced from the end of the run. The clock starts on the snap or the referee's signal, at Team B's option.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-25. Team A is ahead in the score late in the fourth quarter and after the Two-Minute Timeout. When ball carrier A22 is tackled in the field of play, the game clock reads 1:35. The umpire reports to the referee that he has a flag for holding by snapper A55 at the A-30. On the play, A22 gained nine yards.
choice_a: A, 2/16, A-16, Snap or Ready option of Team B.
choice_c: A, 2/12, A-20, Snap or Ready option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/12, A-20, Snap or Ready option of Team B. Because the foul at the A-30 occurred behind the end of A22's run (which reached the A-34, beyond the basic spot), the penalty is instead enforced from the spot of the foul. The clock starts on the snap or the referee's signal, at Team B's option.

tags: false start;10-second runoff;end of game;dead ball foul;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz;2025-cfo-preseason-quiz-v6;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. Late in the 4th quarter, the game clock is running, Team A trails in the score, and both teams are out of timeouts. Guard A66 in a three-point stance misses the snap count and lurches forward, committing a false start. B77 then commits a dead-ball personal foul. The game clock is stopped with 8 seconds remaining in the game.
choice_a: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. The dead-ball personal foul also enforced.
choice_b: A, 2/15, B-35, Ready. The game is over.
choice_c: A, 2/10, B-30, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over.
choice_d: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over. The game is over because Team B accepts the 10-second runoff associated with the false start. The penalty for B77's dead-ball foul is not enforced since it occurred after the game had already ended.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. The game clock is running late in the 4th quarter. B77 is in the neutral zone at the snap but does not make contact. QB A12 rolls out to pass, runs to the B-17, and throws a forward pass which falls incomplete. The game clock reads 0:15.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/5, B-20, Snap. B77 commits a live-ball offside foul, and A12's forward pass from beyond the neutral zone is illegal. The fouls offset, and since there are offsetting fouls, there is no 10-second runoff. The game clock remains at 15 seconds and starts on the snap.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-35. Team A punts and at the snap has five players in the backfield. The kick is partially blocked behind the neutral zone and subsequently goes out of bounds at the A-45.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-40, Snap – or – A, 4/12, A-30, Snap. Team A's alignment is a foul for illegal formation. Team B may keep the ball, 1st and 10, after the 5-yard penalty is enforced at the dead-ball spot, or have the penalty enforced at the previous spot with 4th down repeated.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-5. Punter A88 from Team A's end zone kicks the ball. Tackle A77 is flagged for holding in the end zone. B21 catches the kick at the 50 and returns the ball to the A-30.
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team B with A, FK, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team B with A, FK, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, A-20, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team B with A, FK, A-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, A-20, Snap. Team B has the option of possession after the 10-yard holding penalty is enforced from the B-30, the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, or accepting the penalty for a safety instead.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Kicker A91 kicks a high, short kick and B44 is in position to catch the kick at the B-35. A21 covering the kick catches the free kick very near receiver B44, thus preventing him from making the catch. A21 is downed at the B-30.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, 50, Snap. The action by A21 is a foul for kick-catch interference. The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, the B-35.

question_text: 4/10 @ 50. B17 is at the B-20 and in position to catch Team A's high scrimmage kick. During the downward flight of the ball, A37 contacts B17 viciously and flagrantly before he touches the ball. A37 did not alter his speed or make any attempt to elude B17. The untouched kick rolls out of bounds at the B-10.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. Eject A37. A37 commits a foul for interference with the opportunity to catch the kick. Since the personal foul is flagrant, after the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, the B-20, A37 is ejected.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. Receiver A80 catches a pass and runs down the sideline to the B-3 where the ball is punched out by B40 (who was in bounds). The loose ball goes into the end zone where A80 jumps on it. The officials rule A80 stepped out of bounds at the B-4 before the ball came loose. Replays show A80 did not step out of bounds and recovered the loose ball in the immediate continuing action after the OB ruling.
choice_b: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No clock.
choice_c: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team A. A, Try, B-3, No clock. Whether A80 stepped out of bounds prior to the ball coming loose is reviewable, and since there is a clear recovery by A80 in the immediate continuing action, replay awards the ball to Team A at the spot of the recovery, in the end zone, for a touchdown.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. A80 attempts to catch a forward pass at the B-20, but is contacted by B3 just as the ball arrives and it falls to the ground incomplete. The Back Judge throws a flag, and the Referee announces targeting on B3. Replays show B3 leaving his feet to make a play on the ball and making forcible contact with his shoulder to the head of A80.
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-20, Snap. No foul for targeting.
choice_b: A, 4/10, B-40, Snap. Foul confirmed, DQ B3.
choice_c: A, 4/10, B-40, Snap. No foul for targeting.
choice_d: A, 4/10, B-40, Ready. No foul for targeting.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, B-40, Snap. No foul for targeting. Although A80 is defenseless and there is forcible contact to the head/neck area, B3 is legitimately playing the ball, so there is no foul for targeting.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A and Team B line up in position and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has multiple green dot helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. The kick by A91 is caught by B21 at the goal line and returned to the B-30.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap. There is no foul on the play. On free kick plays, the coach-to-player communications rule is not in effect, and there is no limit on green dot helmets for either team.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B44 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B44 catches the kick at the 5-yard line and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The "T signal" is an invalid fair catch signal, so the ball is dead when caught. The crew should shut the play down after B44 gains possession, and the return run doesn't count.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B44 gives the "T signal" as the kick is in flight. B44 muffs the kick at 5-yard line, recovers at the B-7, and returns the kick back to the B-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-7, Snap. The "T signal" is an invalid fair catch signal, so the ball is dead when recovered by B44. Since B44 muffed the initial touch, the ball is dead at the actual recovery spot, and the return run doesn't count.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40. Late in the game, A22 runs for 8 yards and is tackled short of the line to gain. The game clock reads 1:58 when A22 is downed. B54's helmet came off (not a direct result of a foul) during the play. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 3/2, B-32, 10-Second Runoff, Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_b: A, 3/2, B-32, Two-Minute Timeout, Play clock set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: A, 3/2, B-32, Two-Minute Timeout, Play clock set to 25 seconds.
choice_d: A, 3/2, B-32, Ready, Play clock set to 25 seconds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/2, B-32, Two-Minute Timeout, Play clock set to 25 seconds. The game clock is stopped at 1:58 for the Two-Minute Timeout and for B54's helmet being off. Even with a Team B helmet off, the play clock is set to 25 seconds following the Two-Minute Timeout, and the game clock starts on the snap. B54 may not participate in the next down since this was not a charged team timeout.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Early in the 2nd quarter, Team A has all 3 timeouts remaining. A12 throws to A88 but the pass is ruled incomplete. The Team A head coach calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to a catch at the B-31. After the ball is ready at the B-31, Team A breaks the huddle late in the play clock, and the Team A head coach attempts to call a timeout just before the play clock displays zero.
choice_b: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A charged a 2nd timeout.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/6, B-36, Ready. Team A commits a dead-ball delay of game foul as the play clock expires. Consecutive charged team timeouts are not allowed by the same team in any individual dead ball period, so the officials will not honor the second timeout request. Team A retains their challenge and their timeout.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. Team A snaps the ball with 12 seconds remaining on the game clock in the 4th quarter. QB A12 can find no receiver open, scrambles outside the tackle box and runs for 10 yards, and is downed inbounds and the clock is stopped with 6 seconds remaining. The defense participated with 12 players on the field. Clock status?
choice_a: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Reset the Game Clock to 0:12.
choice_c: A, 1/5, B-20, Snap. Reset the Game Clock to 0:12.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-15, Ready. Reset the Game Clock to 0:12.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-15, Snap. Reset the Game Clock to 0:12. Team B's action is a substitution infraction foul, and there is no requirement to accept the yardage penalty to have the clock reset. The offense may decline the 5-yard penalty and still keep the option to reset the game clock to 0:12, starting on the next snap.

tags: late substitution;2025 rule change;delay of game;no do-over;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week0;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/5 @ B-15. The ball is ready for play with the game clock running and the umpire (or center judge) is in his regular position. Team A quickly replaces some players with substitutes, gets set for the required one second, and snaps the ball. The umpire (or center judge) is attempting to get to the ball to allow the defense to match up, but he is unable to prevent the snap. The play gains 3 yards.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-20, Ready. The play is shut down, the game clock is stopped, and the defense is allowed to substitute in response to Team A's late substitutions. Team A is penalized 5 yards for delay of game; the play clock is set to 25 seconds, and both clocks start on the ready-for-play signal.

question_text: 3/2 @ B-45. Team A is in formation with the game clock running and the snap is imminent. Team A QB is in shotgun formation and is using a clap as a starting signal. The Team B defensive coordinator claps from his team area causing wideout A88 to false start.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. The action by the defensive coordinator is a dead-ball foul, delay of game for disconcerting signals. The rule's spirit includes squad members or coaches who clap when the snap is imminent and could cause the offense to false start, so the penalty is assessed against Team B.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A1's punt hits the ground and bounces near returner B8 at the B-30. A7 picks up the loose ball at the B-25 and runs the ball into B's end zone. The officials rule the ball did not touch B8. Replays show the ball hit B8's towel that was attached to his waistband.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Whether B8 touched the punt is reviewable. A uniform attachment such as a towel is considered part of the player's body for purposes of touching the ball. Since B8 did touch the ball beyond the neutral zone, Team A's recovery is legal.

question_text: 3/G @ B-10. A80 attempts to catch a pass near the goal line, but the ball comes loose and is immediately recovered by B44 in the end zone. The officials rule the pass incomplete. Replays show A80 gets firm control, takes a step, then reaches the ball for the goal line before it comes loose. It is not clear whether the ball broke the plane of the goal line before he lost possession.
choice_b: Touchdown. A, Try, B-3, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Whether the pass was incomplete and the ball's relation to the goal line are reviewable. Since A80 did complete the catch and Team B clearly recovered in the end zone, but there is no clear evidence the ball crossed the goal line, replay defaults to a touchback.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A91's kick is angled toward the pylon and bounces high at the B-5. After the ball has bounced, the return man, B21, gives a fair catch signal by extending one hand above his head and waving that hand from side to side more than once. B21 then receives the high bounce just inbounds at the B-1, and the officials properly kill the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-1, Snap. The signal by B21 is invalid because it was given after the free kick struck the ground. The ball is dead at the spot of the recovery, with no opportunity to advance.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A91's kick is angled toward the pylon and bounces high at the B-5. After the ball has bounced, the return man, B21, gives a fair catch signal by extending one hand above his head and waving that hand from side to side more than once. B21 then receives the high bounce with his right foot out of bounds and his left foot inbounds at the B-1. The officials properly kill the play.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-35, Ready – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Since B21 has a foot touching out of bounds, he is considered an out of bounds player. When he touches the kick, the ball is treated as out of bounds, giving Team B the standard free-kick-out-of-bounds penalty options.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Team A lines up with wideout A80 covering up Tight End A88 at the snap. Both receivers release downfield, and QB A12 throws to A88. Defensive back, B21, makes early contact and plays through the back of A88 at the A-45 before the catchable ball arrives and the BJ puts the marker down. The pass falls incomplete.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-45, Snap. DPI on B21.
choice_b: A, 4/10, A-25, Ready – or A, 3/15, A-20, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, A-25, Snap – or A, 3/15, A-20, Snap. Because A88 is covered up, he is not an eligible receiver, so pass interference rules don't apply to the contact on him. Instead, since A88 went more than 3 yards downfield as an originally ineligible receiver while the pass crossed the neutral zone, he committed a foul for ineligible receiver downfield, giving Team A a choice of enforcement.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A91's punt lands at the B-30 and is recovered by A80 at the B-25. After the ball landed at the B-30 and before the recovery by A80, the ball bounced forward to the B-28 and tipped receiver B21's towel.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-25, Snap. B21 is considered to have touched the ball at the B-28, since a player's equipment or attachments, including a towel, are considered part of that player. Since B21 touched the scrimmage kick beyond the neutral zone, any player may recover the ball, and A80's recovery is legal.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-40. Midway through the 2nd quarter, Team A is in position at the line with the game clock running. Before the snap, QB A12 yells "move" as a part of his offensive signals. B75 jumps into the neutral zone and contacts A67.
choice_a: B, 1/5, B-45, Ready. Encroachment on B75.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/6, B-45, Ready. New for 2025, the offense cannot use the terms "move" and "stem" as part of its starting cadence. A12's use of "move" is a dead ball foul, delay of game for disconcerting signals, penalized against Team A.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25. There are 15 seconds remaining in the 4th quarter and the score is A-17, B-20. No timeouts remaining for either team. Under pressure and while still in the tackle box at the B-35, A16 throws an incomplete pass which lands just beyond the neutral zone in an area where there are no eligible receivers with 0:10 remaining. B98 lost his helmet at the line of scrimmage during the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/20, B-35, 10-Second Runoff. Intentional grounding causes the clock to stop immediately, so a 10-second runoff applies regardless of the unrelated helmet-off on the same play. Team B elects the runoff, and the game is over.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A executes an on-side kick. A33 is running near the sideline and steps out of bounds at the A-45. At the A-47 he leaps from out of bounds and bats the ball forward, and it rolls out of bounds at the A-49.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_c: B, 1/10, A-42, Ready – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, Free Kick Rules.
choice_d: B, 1/10, A-42, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, Free Kick Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-42, Snap – or – A, Free Kick, A-30, Free Kick Rules. Airborne A33 is out of bounds when he touches the ball since he has not re-established inbounds. This is not a batting foul because the ball is dead as soon as A33, an out of bounds player, touches it; instead it is a free kick out of bounds, giving Team B the standard enforcement choices.

tags: dead ball foul;personal foul;half the distance;line to gain;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week1;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/4 @ B-30. Midway through the 3rd quarter, ball carrier A22 goes out of bounds at the B-18. B78 commits a personal foul immediately after the ball is out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-9, Ready. A22's run makes the line to gain, but B78's dead-ball foul occurs before the new line to gain is established, so it must be enforced first. The penalty is half the distance to the goal, creating a 1st and Goal.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-40. A80 attempts to catch a pass at the B-45 but the ball comes loose and hits the ground. Players from both teams attempt to jump on the loose ball at the B-43. The pass is ruled incomplete. Replays show A80 gets control with his left foot on the ground, then takes another step while tucking the ball away and turning upfield before the ball comes loose. There is no evidence of a clear recovery.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10, A-40, Snap. Whether the pass was complete or incomplete is reviewable, but there must be video evidence of a clear recovery to change the ruling to a catch and fumble. Without that evidence, the on-field incomplete ruling stands.

question_text: 4/3 @ B-33. QB A1 scrambles near the line to gain and begins to slide at the B-31 just before B4 hits him. The officials spot the ball at the B-30 and there are flags on the play. The Referee announces personal foul late hit with targeting on B4. Replays show B4 lead with his shoulder and make forcible contact to A1's chest.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-16, Snap. B4 will remain in the game.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-16, Ready. B4 will remain in the game.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-16, Snap. B4 will remain in the game. The line to gain and targeting are both reviewable. Since A1 began his slide short of the line to gain, the ball goes over to Team B on downs at that spot. The late hit personal foul is enforced after the change of possession, but since the contact was to the chest, not the head/neck, there is no targeting and B4 stays in the game.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. While the free kick is in the air, B21 gives a "T signal" thinking he will not return the kick. The ball is caught by B44 at the B-5.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-5, Snap. The signal by B21 is invalid, and the ball is dead when caught due to the invalid signal. Team B puts the ball in play at the dead ball spot.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. While the free kick is in the air, B21 gives a "T signal" thinking he will not return the kick. The ball is caught by B44 in the end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. The signal by B21 is invalid, and the ball is dead when caught due to the invalid signal. The result of the play is a touchback, and Team B puts the ball in play at the B-25.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-22. A44 runs for 6 yards and is downed inbounds. During the down A22's helmet comes off (no helmet foul by the defense) and B77's helmet comes off before the end of the play (no helmet foul by the offense). When the clock stops it reads 0:58 in the fourth quarter. Neither team has a timeout remaining. Play clock?
choice_a: A, 4/4, B-16, 10-Second Runoff. Play clock set to 40.
choice_b: A, 4/4, B-16, Ready. Play clock set to 25.
choice_c: A, 4/4, B-16, Ready. Play clock set to 40.
choice_d: A, 4/10, B-22, Ready. Play clock set to 40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/4, B-16, Ready. Play clock set to 40. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock stops for helmets off on opposing players. Both A22 and B77 must leave the game for one play. The play clock is set to 40 seconds, the treatment for a defensive helmet off, rather than 25 for an offensive helmet off.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. QB A12 takes the snap and rolls out to the right. Finding no receiver open, A12 turns upfield and runs. After making the line to gain, A12 breaks stride as if he is going to slide feet first at the B-27, stays upright and runs for a touchdown.
choice_a: Touchdown. A, Try, B-2, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-27, Running. The live ball becomes dead at the point where A12 simulates or fakes as if he will begin a feet-first slide. A fake slide is not reviewable.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Midway through the 3rd quarter, airborne A88 receives a legal forward pass at the A-35. As A88 receives the ball, he is contacted by B21 and first comes to the ground with the ball at the A-33, where the ball is declared dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-35, Running. The A-35 is the point of forward progress for A88, not where he lands. He has made the line to gain, and the clock does not stop for an awarded first down before the Two-Minute Timeout.

question_text: 3/4 @ 50. With the clock running after the ball is made ready for play and before the snap, B60 charges across the neutral zone and contacts snapper A50. A61 then commits a flagrant personal foul against B60.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. Eject A61. Team B is penalized 5 yards for B60's offside foul, resulting in a first down. Team A is then penalized 15 yards for A61's personal foul, resetting the chains to 1st and 10 from the A-40. A61 is ejected.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A91 kicks a squib kick downfield, and A21 has a foot down at the A-36 1/2 when the ball is kicked. B27 has his knee on the ground at the B-25 when he recovers the kick.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-30, Ready or A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap or A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap or A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-30, Snap or A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. A21 committed a foul for offside. The ball is dead at the spot of the recovery since B27 has his knee on the ground. Team B may choose a five-yard penalty from the previous spot with a re-kick, or five yards from the dead-ball recovery spot with a first down.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A91's punt travels five yards beyond the neutral zone where B33 touches the ball. The ball then rebounds behind the neutral zone, where A80 recovers it at the A-30 in the field of play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-30, Snap. The ball is dead when recovered by A80 and may not be advanced. Since B33 touched the ball beyond the neutral zone, the recovery is legal, resulting in a first down for Team A.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-20. Punter A1, under a heavy rush by multiple players, is forcibly contacted by B7 in the plant leg just after he kicks the ball. The ball goes out of bounds at the A-45. The Center Judge gives a tip signal and the Referee announces there is no foul for roughing the kicker as the ball was tipped. Replays show the ball was not touched by B7 but was touched by B44 before the contact with the punter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. The touching of the kick is reviewable, both as to whether it was touched and which player touched it. Since B7, the player who made the contact, did not touch the ball, the foul for roughing the kicker is confirmed and enforced.

question_text: 3/15 @ B-40. Early in the 3rd quarter, QB A1 scrambles out of the pocket and runs near the sideline toward the line to gain. He tiptoes down the sideline and is eventually pushed out of bounds at the B-24. The Line Judge rules A1 out of bounds at the B-30. Replays show that A1 did not step out of bounds until he was pushed out at the B-24.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5, B-30, Ready. If the on-field ruling is a ball carrier out of bounds, replay can only review the spot at that point in relation to the line to gain, or whether the ball carrier stepped out prior to that point. Replay cannot put the ball carrier back in bounds and award an advance.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. A12 passes to A22 who makes the catch at the A-40, runs down the sideline and steps out of bounds at the B-20. During A22's advance, the LJ moves down the sideline officiating the play and runs into the offensive team's Head Coach in the restricted area ("the white") and both go down at midfield.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-35, Ready. This physical interference with an official is a dead-ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul with no warning. Since the Head Coach is readily identifiable, he is charged with the UNS, enforced from the collision spot at midfield.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. A12 passes to A22 who makes the catch at the A-40, runs down the sideline and steps out of bounds at the B-45. During A22's advance, the LJ moves down the sideline officiating the play and runs into the offensive team's Head Coach in the restricted area ("the white") and both go down at the A-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Ready. This is a dead-ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul on the Head Coach. Since A22 made the line to gain, the foul is considered to have occurred after the series ended but before the new line to gain was established, so the penalty is enforced from the collision spot before that new line to gain locks in.

question_text: 4/12 @ A-35. Team A is in scrimmage kick formation at the snap, and just after the snap lineman B77 grabs guard A66 and pulls him to one side, allowing linebacker B43 to shoot the gap to try to block the kick. A91 gets the kick away and B44 catches the kick at the B-25 and returns to the B-40 where he is tackled.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-45, Snap. B77's action is a holding foul, and since it occurred before the kick, it is not governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement. The 10-yard penalty is enforced at the previous spot, and defensive holding includes an automatic first down.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-30. Nose guard B55 is lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. Back A22 takes the ball on a quick play up the middle, bursts into the open, and at the B-10 he turns around, shows the ball to the defense, and prances backward into the end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/7, B-30, Snap. B55's offside and A22's unsportsmanlike taunting are offsetting fouls with no choice for either team; the down is repeated. The clock starts on the snap since the result of the play was a touchdown.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 with extended hands. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-20, Snap. A blindside block delivered with extended hands is specifically not a foul. The interception and return by B44 are legal.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 by attacking with forcible contact with his shoulder into the chest of A88. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. The action by B21 is a personal foul for an illegal blindside block, enforced 15 yards from the spot of the foul at midfield.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. B44 intercepts the pass of A12 at the B-20 and turns back up-field on the return. During the return, B21 approaches A88 at midfield from the blind side and blocks A88 by attacking with forcible contact with his shoulder into the head of A88. B44 returns the pass to the A-20.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. B21 is disqualified.
choice_c: B, 1/10, A-20, Snap. B21 is disqualified.
choice_d: A, 1/10, B-35, Snap. B21 is disqualified.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-35, Snap. B21 is disqualified. This is a personal foul for a blindside block with targeting; A88 is a defenseless player, and B21 attacks with forcible contact to his head, so B21 is disqualified. The 15-yard penalty is still enforced from the spot of the foul, and Team B keeps the ball.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-40. Back A22 takes a backward pass from the quarterback at the B-45, circles right end, and heads for the goal line. Guard A66, who had pulled out to lead the play, legally blocks B90 to the ground and then stands over him at the B-30 taunting and screaming obscenities. This draws a flag from the head line judge when A22 is at the B-10 before continuing into the end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, B-45, Snap. A66's action is a live-ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul, and the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul, the B-30. The touchdown counts, and the clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-43. Late in the 4th quarter Team A is ahead 21-17 and Team B is out of timeouts. Back A2 runs to the A-45 where he is tripped up and goes to the ground (1:30). Clearly after A2 hits the ground, B54 goes down and drives his left forearm to the side of his head with force. There are no flags on the play.
choice_c: A, 1/10, B-40, 1:30, 10-Second Runoff option, Disqualify B54.
choice_d: A, 1/10, A-45, 1:30, 10-Second Runoff option, Disqualify B54.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, 1:30, 10-Second Runoff option, Disqualify B54. Whether there was targeting is reviewable. A2 is defenseless since he is clearly on the ground, and B54 leads with his forearm to attack the head/neck area, confirming targeting and disqualification. This dead ball foul after the Two-Minute Timeout creates a 10-second runoff option for Team A.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45. Late in the 4th quarter Team A trails 24-21 and is out of timeouts. A80 catches a pass and gives himself up by sliding feet first at the B-33, but time expires on the game clock. Replays show when A80 began the slide the game clock was at 0:03.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-33, 0:03, Ready. The game clock is reviewable in this situation. Since Team A is out of timeouts, there must be at least three seconds on the game clock when the play is over by rule, and replay looks at the moment the slide begins.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-48. From a scrimmage kick formation, A92 punts the ball to the B-7 where it hits B25 on the leg. As the ball rolls along the ground, B25 then kicks it at the B-4 to prevent Team A from recovering. The ball bounces into Team B's end zone and over the end line.
choice_a: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, FK, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap.
choice_c: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, FK, B-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, score 2 points for Team A with B, FK, B-20, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-2, Snap. B25's kicking the ball provides new impetus, resulting in a safety, and is also a foul for illegally kicking the ball. Team A may decline the penalty and take the safety, or accept the penalty, which is governed by postscrimmage kick rules and gives Team B the ball at the B-2.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A is prepared to kick off. The ball is on the tee, and the referee has signaled it ready for play. As the kicker approaches the ball it begins to fall off the tee just as he starts his kicking motion. The kicker follows through and kicks the ball as it continues to fall off the tee. The ball squibs down the field and rolls out of bounds at the B-45.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules. There is no foul, and the ball never becomes properly alive. Officials should stop play and have the teams line up for a new kickoff.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-35. Early in the 2nd quarter, Team A's formation at the snap includes only 6 players on the line with five players on the line of scrimmage numbered 50-79 and one player on the line wearing number 82. There are four players lined up legally in the backfield. A21 takes a handoff and runs to the A-47.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-47, Running. The formation is legal because Team A has no more than four players in the backfield and the required five players on the line wearing numbers 50-79.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-30. A91's punt travels downfield and B21 has a foot out of bounds at the B-30 when he gives a valid fair catch signal. B21 then catches the kick inbounds at the B-33.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-33, Snap. There is no rule against a receiver going out of bounds during a kick. The inbounds catch by B21 is legal, and the ball is dead where caught.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-35. A91 punts the ball, and at the snap Team A had five players in the backfield. A91's kick is partially blocked, rolls forward and goes out of bounds at the A-45.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, A-40, Snap – or – A, 4/12, A-30, Snap. Team A committed a foul for illegal formation. Team B may keep the ball with the penalty enforced from the dead-ball spot, or have the penalty enforced at the previous spot with fourth down repeated.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. In trying to gain yardage, ball carrier A44 is slowed by defensive players attempting to make the tackle at the B-12. Lineman A77 puts his hands on the buttocks of A44 and pushes him forward to the B-9 where A44 is downed.
choice_c: A, 3/8, B-12, Running. Illegal push, no gain.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-9, Running. It is not a foul to push the ball carrier from behind, so the action by A77 is legal and the extra yardage counts.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. In trying to gain yardage, ball carrier A44 is slowed by defensive players attempting to make the tackle at the B-12. Lineman A77 drives the pile of teammates who begin to surround A44 and A44 goes forward with the pile and is down at the B-9.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal, B-9, Running. It is not a foul to drive the pile of teammates around the ball carrier, so this action is legal and the extra yardage counts.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. In trying to gain yardage, ball carrier A44 is slowed by defensive players attempting to make the tackle at the B-12. Lineman A77 grabs the arm of A44 at the B-12 and pulls him forward for more yardage. A44 is down at the B-9.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/7, B-17, Ready. Unlike pushing or driving the pile, grabbing and pulling the runner's arm is a foul for assisting the runner. The 5-yard penalty is enforced using the three-and-one principle.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. Late in the 4th quarter, Team A is out of timeouts and trails 31-28. Receiver A80 catches a pass and goes to the ground at the B-28, and the game clock goes to 0:00. The officials immediately get together and the Referee announces that there was 0:02 on the game clock at the end of the play and reset it. Replays show that there was 0:02 on the clock when A80 was down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-28, 0:02 Ready. The on-field officials can reset the clock to less than three seconds themselves in this situation, and replay confirms the accuracy of that correction rather than overriding it.

question_text: 3/1 @ B-41. With 0:50 remaining in the game Team A leads 47-14 and Team B is out of timeouts. Back A2 takes a handoff and gets hit near the line to gain. The LOS officials come in and mark the ball at the B-40 signaling 1st down. From the replays available it is not clear as to whether A2 made the line to gain.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, B-40, Ready. Due to the lopsided score and time remaining, there is no significant competitive impact on this play, so replay should not stop the game despite the unclear video evidence.

question_text: Try @ B-3. During the second possession series of the 3rd extra period, the score is tied 21-21 and Team A lines up for their 2-point try. A12's screen pass is intercepted by B21 at the B-2, and B21 runs across the field, cuts back into his own endzone to avoid tacklers (no momentum) and is tackled with the nose of the football in the field of play and the back nose of the ball in the end zone.
choice_a: No score. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_b: Score a 2-point safety for Team A. Team A wins 23-21.
choice_c: Score a 1-point safety for Team A. Team A wins 22-21.
choice_d: Score a 1-point safety for Team A. Team A wins 22-21. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score a 1-point safety for Team A. Team A wins 22-21. The result of the play is a safety, since B21 runs the ball into his own end zone and is responsible for the ball being declared dead there. A safety on a try is valued as a 1-point safety, and the game ends since it decides the outcome.

question_text: Try @ B-3. During the second possession series of the 3rd extra period, the score is tied 21-21 and Team A lines up for their 2-point try. A12's screen pass is intercepted by B21 at the B-2, and B21 runs across the field, cuts back into his own endzone to avoid tacklers (no momentum). B21 then escapes and runs the length of the field for a Touchdown.
choice_a: Score a 1-point safety for Team A. Team A wins 22-21.
choice_b: No score. B, 1/10, 25-yard line, No Clock.
choice_c: Score a 6-point Touchdown for Team B. Team B wins 27-21.
choice_d: Score a 2-point Touchdown for Team B. Team B wins 23-21.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Score a 2-point Touchdown for Team B. Team B wins 23-21. Since B21 successfully carries the ball into his opponents' end zone and is responsible for it being declared dead there, this is a touchdown. A touchdown scored on a try is valued at 2 points, so Team B wins.

category: Series of Downs & Line to Gain
question_text: 1/10 @ B-25. During the first possession series of the first extra period, QB A12 throws downfield, and the ball is intercepted by B21 at the B-5. B21 returns out to the B-30 and fumbles, and A55 scoops up the ball at the B-25 and rumbles to the B-20 where he is downed.
choice_a: Team A's possession series is over. B, 1/10, 25, No clock.
choice_d: Team A's possession series is over. B, 1/10, 25, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team A's possession series is over. B, 1/10, 25, No clock. Since possession already changed on the interception, Team A's possession series is over regardless of the subsequent fumble recovery. Team B begins its own possession series at the 25.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-2. Team A comes to the line and QB A12 calls timeout. Team A huddles near the sideline and in front of their team area. The head coach for Team A brings one of the 18 authorized tablets for in-game video and reviews plays with Team A players on the field in front of their team area.
choice_a: A, 4/Goal, B-2, Snap. Unsportsmanlike conduct on the head coach.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/Goal, B-2, Snap. This is a legal use of an authorized tablet near the team area. It would only become an unsportsmanlike act if the coach took the tablet to the center of the field to review with the team.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Late in the game with the score tied, Team A lines up to free kick. Kicker A91 places the ball down at the right hash for an apparent onside kick. After the Referee's ready-for-play signal, A91 approaches the ball and then picks up the ball runs to the left hash at the A-35, places the ball down and quickly kicks the ball. The ball goes 10-yards and is recovered by A21.
choice_a: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_c: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules. Once the ball position between the hash marks has been selected and the ready-for-play signal given, the ball may be relocated only after a charged team timeout or before a re-kick. This is a dead-ball foul, penalized 5 yards from the succeeding spot.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A is offside and A91's kick goes out of bounds at the B-15 after it has been touched by B80 at the B-20.
choice_b: A, Free Kick, A-35, FK Rules.
choice_d: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-20, Snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick, A-30, FK Rules – or – B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. For the offside foul, Team B may elect to have the kick repeated after the 5-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot, or snap the ball at the inbounds spot five yards from where the ball went out of bounds.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. The kickoff is high and deep, and deep receiver B21 gives a fair catch signal as the kick is in flight. B44 catches the kick right in front of B21 at the 8-yard line. The LJ shuts the play down just after B44's catch.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-8, Snap. B21's fair catch signal is valid, and there can be no advance by any receiving-team player after a valid or invalid signal. The ball is dead when caught, and Team B puts it in play at the dead ball spot.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. A91's free kick bounces and breaks the plane of the A-45. While the ball is in the air beyond the A-45, A21, who is standing at the A-44, touches the ball, and A80 recovers the ball at the A-46.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-46, Snap. The touch by A21 is legal since the ball has broken the plane of and remained beyond Team B's restraining line, so the recovery by A80 is legal.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-40. A80 attempts to catch a forward pass at the B-30 but it deflects off his hands and B3 catches it. B3 is tackled at the B-35. The Back Judge has a flag down and the Referee announces targeting on B40 for hitting A80 at the B-30. Replays show B40 leading with his shoulder and attacking with forcible contact to the head of A80. The contact occurred clearly after B3 caught the football.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-15, DQ B40, Snap. The targeting foul is reviewable, and replay can examine the timing of the contact relative to the interception. Since it is clear the contact began after B3's interception, Team B retains possession and the penalty is enforced after the change of possession.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-40. QB A1 moves outside the pocket but can't find an open receiver so he tucks the ball and starts to run toward the line of scrimmage. B56 makes forcible contact with his shoulder to the head of A1 at the A-42 knocking him to the ground. The Line Judge throws a flag for targeting.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/8, A-42, Ready. Since A1 tucked the ball and is running, he is no longer in a passing posture and does not receive defenseless player protection. Only the crown-of-helmet standard applies, and the shoulder contact here is legal, so there is no foul.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Early in the 2nd quarter, QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air at the A-20 and A80 catches the ball as he crosses in front of A12 at the A-20 and A80 turns upfield and is out of bounds at the A-40.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-40, Running. The short flip of the ball by A12 is a legal forward pass unless clearly and obviously thrown backward. This is a completed forward pass to A80, and the run upfield results in a 1st down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-25. Early in the 2nd quarter, QB A12 is in shotgun formation and slot receiver A80, who is lined up to the wide side of the field, starts back in motion toward A12 before the snap. At the snap A80 heads toward A12 to run the jet sweep. A12 flips the ball up into the air at the A-20 and A80 muffs the ball at the A-20 and the ball drops to the ground.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10, A-25, Snap. The short flip is a legal forward pass unless clearly and obviously thrown backward. When the ball strikes the ground, it is an incomplete forward pass and the play is dead.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-40. After the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, A10 drops back in the tackle box to the B-47 and is unable to locate an open receiver. A10 throws a forward pass that is incomplete at the B-30 where there is no eligible Team A receiver. The game clock is stopped at 0:55.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/17, B-47, 10-Second Runoff. A10's pass is intentional grounding, an illegal forward pass, with the penalty being loss of down at the spot of the foul. Because this occurred after the Two-Minute Timeout, the 10-second runoff rule applies.

tags: illegal touching;out of bounds receiver;defensive touch;eligibility restored;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz;2025-cfo-weekly-quiz-week6;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 @ A-25. Early in the 3rd period, A12 throws an out route to A88 who went out of bounds on his own at the A-35. B21 tips the legal forward pass up in the air near the sideline at the A-33 while A88 is still out of bounds. A88 comes back and reestablishes inbounds and catches the pass at the A-35 where he is downed.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. Illegal Touching on A88.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10, A-35, Running. There is no foul for illegal touching by A88, since B21's touch made all players eligible for the remainder of the down.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30. A44 runs to the B-25 where he is downed inbounds. During the down A22's helmet comes off (no helmet foul by the defense) and B77 is down with an injury. When the clock is stopped it reads 0:58 in the 4th quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/5, B-25, Ready. There is no option for a 10-second runoff because the clock stops for both the helmet off and the injury on opposing players. The play clock is set to 40 seconds, the treatment for the defensive injury, rather than 25 for the offensive helmet off.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Team A lines up in position and the Referee signals the ball ready for play. Team A has A44 and A21 with green dots on their helmets on the field signifying that those players have radio receiving capability. A91's kick goes out the back of the end zone.
choice_a: A, 1/10, A-35, Snap. 5-yard equipment penalty on Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. There is no foul on the play. On free kick plays, the coach-to-player communications rule is not in effect, and there is no limit on green dot helmets for either team.

question_text: 4/10 @ A-35. A91's kick lands at the B-25, and rolls out of bounds at the B-15. A88 interferes with B21's opportunity to catch the kick at the B-25. During the down, time expires in the 1st quarter.
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-40, No Clock. Untimed down to end the 1st quarter.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap – start of the 2nd quarter.
choice_c: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap – start of the 2nd quarter.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-40, Ready – start of the 2nd quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-40, Snap – start of the 2nd quarter. A88 is guilty of kick-catch interference, enforced 15 yards from the spot of the foul. The period is not extended for this foul; it is enforced at the beginning of the second quarter instead.

question_text: 2/7 @ A-5 late in the 2nd quarter. Quarterback A12 drops back to pass and is scrambling in his end zone as he tries to find an open receiver. About to be tackled in the end zone, A12 throws the ball forward to the ground at the A-3 in an area where there are no eligible receivers. When the ball is dead the game clock shows 0:18. Team B accepts the penalty.
choice_a: Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, Ready.
choice_b: A, 1/10, A-20, Free Kick, No Clock.
choice_c: Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-35, 10-Second Runoff.
choice_d: Score 2 points for Team B. A, Free Kick, A-20, 10-Second Runoff.
