explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/5 @ B-45, Snap. No option for a Fair Catch Kick. There is no option of selecting a Fair Catch Kick at this point. After the original ready for play following the punt play, there is no option for a Fair Catch Kick.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, receiver A80 catches a pass and is tackled near the line to gain. The officials spot the ball at the B-31 (1:06). With 0:37 on the game clock and 0:11 on the play clock, the game is stopped by the Replay Official to review the spot in relation to the line to gain. After review, the ruling on the field is upheld. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/1 @ B-31, GC 0:37, PC :25, Ready. The play clock always resets to 25 seconds after any replay review.
choice_b: A, 4/1 @ B-31, GC 0:37, PC :40, Ready. The play clock resets to 40 seconds since the game clock was stopped for review.
choice_d: A, 4/1 @ B-31, GC 1:06, PC :11, Ready. The game clock is restored to its pre-snap reading of 1:06 since the review upheld the spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-31, GC 0:37, PC :11, Ready. After the Two-Minute Timeout, if the ruling on field is upheld in this situation, the play clock remains where it was when the game was stopped for review.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. Late in the 2nd quarter, receiver A80 catches a pass and is tackled near the line to gain. The officials spot the ball at the B-31 (1:06). With 0:37 on the game clock and 0:11 on the play clock, the game is stopped by the Replay Official to review the spot in relation to the line to gain. Video shows the ball was at the B-30 when A80 was first down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-30, GC 1:06, PC :25, Ready. The play clock is set to 25 seconds even though time is restored to the game clock.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-30, GC 0:37, PC :40, Ready. The game clock stays at 0:37 since only the spot, not the clock, is corrected.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-30, GC 1:06, PC :11, Ready. The play clock is restored to its reading when the review began.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-30, GC 1:06, PC :40, Ready. After the Two-Minute Timeout, if the ruling on field is overturned and time is put back on the game clock, the play clock is set to :40.

question_text: 1/Goal at the B-5. A21 runs to the B-3, fumbles the ball, and it bounces at the B-2, where B54 recovers, and his momentum takes him into his own end zone, where he is downed. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Safety, score 2 points for Team A. B54 being carried into his own end zone by momentum results in a safety.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. B54's momentum into the end zone is treated as a touchback for Team B.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-2, Snap. Team A retains the ball at the spot of the fumble since B54 ended up in his own end zone.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-2, Snap. It is Team B's ball at the spot where the fumble was recovered. The ruling is the same if B54 had caught an opponent's pass or fumble, or caught or recovered an opponent's kick, under similar circumstances.

tags: kicking formation;2x2 numbering exception;illegal shift off the line;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v2;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 3 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A8, A31, and A18 to the right. They have a gunner on each side of the field lined up on the line and three backs (including the punter) in place. Prior to the snap, Team A shifts A33 one yard back off the line of scrimmage. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Since the ball resulted in a touchback, the pre-snap shift by A33 has no bearing on enforcement.
choice_c: A, 4/10, B-45, Snap. The shift by A33 is legal since he remains within the tackle box after moving back.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap only, with no option to instead replay 4th down at the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25, Snap – or – A, 4/15 @ 50, Snap. Team A has committed a foul for Illegal Formation since A33 shifted out of the 2 X 2 formation prior to the snap. The five players in the 2 X 2 formation are locked into their positions once they line up after the snapper is in his position. Penalties for kicking team fouls other than kick-catch interference during a scrimmage kick play that crosses the neutral zone may be enforced either at the previous spot with the down repeated or at the spot where the subsequent dead ball belongs to Team B, at Team B's option.

tags: kicking formation;2x2 numbering exception;legal shift outside the exception;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v2;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 3 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A8, A31, and A18 to the right. They have a gunner on each side of the field lined up on the line and three backs (including the punter) in place. Prior to the snap, Team A shifts A18 one yard back off the line of scrimmage. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10, B-25, Snap. Team A is flagged for illegal formation since A18 shifted off the line of scrimmage.
choice_b: A, 4/10, B-45, Snap. The shift negates the scrimmage kick formation entirely, forcing a repeat of 4th down.
choice_d: B, 1/10, B-45, Snap. The penalty for the shift is enforced from the previous spot rather than resulting in no foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. There is no foul since the 2 X 2 formation remains intact with A33, A27, A49, A8, and A31 in position and Team A has no more than four backs.

question_text: 4/10 at the B-45. With potential punter A94 lined up 12 yards behind the snapper, Team A does not have five linemen numbered 50-79 in the game. They line up in a 2 X 2 formation with A33 and A27 to the left of snapper A49, and A18 and A31 to the right. Team A has two gunners, A80 and A88. These gunners are on the line of scrimmage wide and to the right of A31, with A88 nearer to the sideline. A33 is uncovered at the snap. A49 snaps the ball and A94 kicks the ball through the end zone for a touchback. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/10, B-45, Snap. Leaving A33 uncovered is an illegal formation foul since a tackle position must be covered.
choice_b: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap, and A80 is treated as an eligible receiver since he is lined up on the line of scrimmage.
choice_c: A, 4/15, B-45, Snap. A80's ineligible position on the line is itself a formation foul against Team A.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10, B-20, Snap. Team A's formation is legal. The players in the 2 X 2 formation are numbering exceptions and are ineligible by position. A tackle position can be left uncovered, but this does not make him an eligible player. The formation is legal, but A80 is not an eligible receiver because he is not on the end of his scrimmage line.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. A80, a tight end, moves across the formation on a pass pattern at a depth of 10 yards, where he contacts B21, a safety, before or after the ball has been thrown. A88, a wide receiver positioned on the opposite side from the tight end at the snap, crosses behind the contact of A80 and B21 and catches the legal forward pass at the B-25 and is downed. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-25, Ready. Since A80 was not the intended target, his contact with B21 is not a foul.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-25, Snap. Offensive pass interference on a completed pass results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_d: A, 3/20 @ 50, Snap. The clock starts on the snap rather than remaining on the ready-for-play signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/20 @ 50, Ready. A80 has committed a foul for offensive pass interference. The penalty is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot.

question_text: 3/10 at the A-45. Midway through the 3rd quarter, QB A12 rolls right and throws toward A88, who gains firm control of the ball right at the sideline and drags his right toe down in the field of play with control. A88 maintains control as he falls out of bounds at the B-40. The Side Judge rules the pass incomplete and replay does not stop the game. The offensive team calls timeout to challenge the ruling on the field. The offensive team head coach had challenged a scoring play earlier in the game, and the challenge was not successful. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Ready. Since the coach already lost a challenge earlier in the game, he has no challenges remaining.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-40, Ready. The incomplete ruling stands because a coach may not challenge a play the Replay Official already let go.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-40, Ready, but the coach loses a timeout regardless of the outcome under the 2026 rule change.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-40, Ready. The pass meets the definition of a catch. Rule 12-5-1-b-1 has changed for 2026 and now the head coach has two replay challenges per game. If a coach wins either of their first two challenges, they are awarded a third challenge.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-16. QB A12 throws into the end zone for A88, who is covered well by B21. Just before the ball arrives, B21 bats the ball away from A88 without any contact, and the ball and both players go down together. B21 hops up immediately and raises his jersey as if he is brandishing a weapon. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-16, Snap. Since there was no contact on the pass breakup, the incomplete pass stands with no foul.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal @ B-8, Ready. The unsportsmanlike conduct foul is enforced without the automatic first down.
choice_d: A, 4/Goal @ B-16, Snap. The unsportsmanlike conduct penalty does not include an automatic first down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-8, Snap. The Unsportsmanlike Conduct rule has been modernized for the 2026 season. Now there is general guidance for officials' judgement on what is a UNS foul, and the list of automatic UNS fouls is down to 5 categories, of which brandishing a weapon is considered part of gun violence and is one of the automatic categories. The penalty includes an automatic first down.

tags: backward pass out of bounds;clock stop foul;10-second runoff;game over;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v2;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/10 at the B-15. Team A trails by 2 points after the Two-Minute Timeout in the 4th quarter, has no timeouts, and the clock is running. QB A12 is under heavy duress, scrambles, and just before he is sacked throws a backward pass from the B-20 that goes out of bounds at the B-22. The game clock is stopped at 0:09. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/20 @ B-25, Snap with 0:09 remaining. There is no runoff since the pass was backward rather than forward.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Throwing a backward pass out of bounds to stop the clock is a turnover on downs.
choice_c: A, 3/15 @ B-25, 10-Second Runoff, but the offense may decline the runoff and keep the clock at 0:09.
choice_d: A, 3/20 @ B-25, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/20 @ B-25, 10-Second Runoff. The game is over. Since A12 threw a backward pass out of bounds to stop the clock, it is a foul that also carries a 10-Second Runoff. The penalty is 5 yards from the spot of the foul and carries a loss of down, but since there were only 9 seconds remaining, the 10-Second Runoff ends the game.

tags: instant replay;catch process;going to the ground;touchdown overturn;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v2;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/Goal at the B-9. QB A1 throws a forward pass to A80 near the sideline in the end zone. A80 controls the ball while airborne and just as his right foot hits the ground inbounds, he is hit by B4. He takes two more steps while completely turning away from B4 and tucking the ball under his right arm before eventually going to the ground. When he lands out of bounds, the ball comes loose. The Side Judge rules the pass incomplete. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Overturn to a Touchdown. A, Try @ B-3, No Clock.
choice_b: The incomplete ruling on the field is confirmed since the ball came loose before A80 was fully down.
choice_c: A, 1/Goal @ B-9, Ready. The pass is ruled incomplete but the down and distance are unaffected.
choice_d: Overturn to a Touchdown, but the play is ruled with the game clock running rather than No Clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Overturn to a Touchdown. A, Try @ B-3, No Clock. A80 maintained control of the ball long enough to perform an act common to the game, and the process of the catch was completed before he lost the ball out of bounds. Additional steps and tuck/turn are indicators for replay to look for to determine the time required to complete the catch.

tags: instant replay;catch process;immediate contact with ground;incomplete overturn;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz;2026-cfo-preseason-quiz-v2;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/10 at the B-40. A80 controls a forward pass at the B-20 near the sideline, and just as his right foot hits the ground inbounds, he is hit by B4 and goes immediately and directly to the ground. When he lands out of bounds, the ball comes loose. The officials rule the play a catch. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The catch ruling on the field is confirmed since A80 had control and a foot down before the ball came loose.
choice_b: Overturn to incomplete. A, 4/10 @ B-40, Snap.
choice_c: Overturn to incomplete, but the down remains 3rd and 10 since the pass never actually reached the receiver.
choice_d: Overturn to a fumble recovered by Team B, since A80 lost the ball once he went to the ground out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Overturn to incomplete. A, 4/10 @ B-40, Snap. A80 did not have the ball long enough to perform an act common to the game, so the pass is incomplete. After control and a body part down, A80 went immediately to the ground, so he must maintain control when he lands.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30, 2Q (0:06). QB A12 throws a desperation pass that is caught by A88 for a TD (0:00). The CJ has a flag down for 2 players on Team A with the unbranded green dot on the back middle line of the helmet indicating that the helmets were equipped with Coach to Player communications.
choice_a: If more than 1 green dot helmet is detected on the field by the same team, the result is a live ball 5-yard equipment violation penalty.
choice_b: The 2Q is extended for an untimed down since the penalty is accepted for a live ball foul on the last play of the 2Q.
choice_c: Officials give a warning to Team A head coach.
explanation: A second player wearing the coach-to-player 'green dot' helmet on the field is a live-ball, five-yard equipment violation. Because Team A's foul is accepted following the last timed down of the second quarter and it is a live-ball foul, the period is extended for an untimed down. Therefore both are correct.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40, 2Q (2:05). Ball carrier A44 runs to the B-32 where he is tackled inbounds (1:58). B55's helmet came off during the play.
choice_b: The play clock is set to 40 seconds.
choice_c: The game is stopped for the Two-Minute Timeout.
explanation: The down (snap 2:05, dead 1:58) crosses the two-minute mark, so the primary stoppage is the mandated Two-Minute Timeout. A loss-of-helmet 10-second runoff applies only when the helmet is the sole reason the clock stopped; here the clock stops for the Two-Minute Timeout regardless, so no runoff and the play clock is set to 25 (not 40).

question_text: 2/10 @ B-40, 2Q (1:16). Ball carrier A44 runs to the B-32 where he is tackled inbounds (1:07). B55's helmet came off during the play.
choice_b: Team A may not decline the 10-second runoff.
choice_c: The play clock is set to 25 seconds.
choice_d: B55 may stay in the game if Team A declines the 10-second runoff.
explanation: This down ends at 1:07, after the Two-Minute Timeout, with the clock running. B55's helmet coming off is the reason to stop the clock, so Team A is offered a 10-second runoff. Team A may decline it, the defensive helmet-off play clock is 40 (not 25), and B55 must leave for one down regardless of the runoff decision unless Team B takes a timeout.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-25, 2Q (0:19), no timeouts remaining for either team. A12 completes a pass to A88 at the B-20 where he is downed. With the clock running down, Team A rushes their field goal unit onto the field, and all Team A players are set in field goal formation and A90 kicks the ball for a successful field goal (0:00). After clearing the play with the crew on the field, both teams leave the playing enclosure and replay gives the all clear as well. The Referee declares the half ended. During the halftime intermission, the instant replay official looks back and determines that Team A had 12 players on the field at the snap.
choice_a: Since another play has not been run, the play is reviewable.
choice_b: The number of players on the field is never reviewable.
choice_c: Reset the clock to 15:00 for the start of the 3rd Quarter and run an untimed down.
choice_d: After the Referee has declared the first half ended, there can be no additional replay reviews from the previous play.
explanation: Once the Referee has declared the first half ended, no further replay reviews of the previous down may occur. Reviewable status is irrelevant after the half has been officially declared over; the replay official's later discovery cannot resurrect the down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-40. Team A snaps from the left hash, opposite their team area. A81 is flanked out to the left side of the formation and goes in motion back toward the ball. After the snap, A21 takes the handoff and runs for 3-yards behind the left tackle. A81 never breaks stride and is inside the hash when the ball is declared dead and continues toward the Team A team area simulating leaving the field. A81 sets near the sideline for a "hide-out" pass. Team A does not substitute and just after the next snap, A12 throws a TD pass to A81 who is running just inside the boundary.
choice_a: Unsportsmanlike Conduct -- Unfair Tactics -- A, 2/22 @ A-28, Ready.
choice_b: Unsportsmanlike Conduct -- Unfair Tactics -- A, 2/22 @ A-28, Snap.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A. The 15-yard penalty is assessed on the try or succeeding kickoff.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A, no foul.
explanation: A 'hide-out' play in which A81 simulates leaving the field to deceive the defense is an unfair-tactics unsportsmanlike conduct foul. It is a live-ball foul; the touchdown is canceled and the 15-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot, with the game clock starting on the snap, not the ready.

question_text: 2/10 @ A-35, 4Q (1:35), tie game, both teams have two timeouts remaining. A12 scrambles out of the pocket to the A-28 and as he is being tackled, he throws an incomplete pass (1:26). Replays show A12's left knee down before he released the ball (1:31).
choice_b: A, 3/17 @ A-28. Reset the clock to 1:31, Ready.
choice_c: A, 3/17 @ A-28, 10-second runoff applies unless either team calls a timeout.
choice_d: Only Team A may call timeout to avoid the 10-second runoff.
explanation: Replay reverses the incomplete pass to a sack because A12's knee was down before release (a reviewable act). Because the correction stops a clock that had been running inside two minutes and the ruling change would have kept the clock running, a 10-second runoff applies unless either team uses a timeout. The clock is not simply reset to 1:31.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-30, 2Q (1:22). Team A has no timeouts remaining. Ball carrier A21 sweeps around right end and throws a backward pass out of bounds to conserve time from the B-25. The ball goes out of bounds at the B-27 (1:15).
choice_b: No foul by A21. A, 4/7 @ B-27.
explanation: A runner's backward pass thrown to conserve time and going out of bounds is not intentional grounding (grounding applies only to forward passes), but throwing a backward pass out of bounds to conserve time is illegal by the ball carrier and carries a five-yard penalty from the spot with loss of down and, inside two minutes, a 10-second runoff. Result: A, 4/10 @ B-30 with a runoff.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. Defender B21 is airborne near the sideline when he gains firm control of a legal forward pass and lands with both feet inbounds at the A-45 and is down. When B21 jumped, his left heel was out of bounds, and he jumped with a single motion with the heel coming off the ground just before the toes.
choice_d: Illegal Touching by Team B. A, 1/5 @ A-35.
explanation: B21 last touched out of bounds (left heel) before leaving the ground in one continuous motion, so he is an out-of-bounds player who touches the pass. His touch is illegal touching, the pass is incomplete, and there is no interception. The ball returns to Team A: A, 2/10 @ A-30, clock on the snap.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30, 2Q (0:09). QB A10, who has not been outside the tackle box, throws a desperation pass from the B-35 that falls incomplete after ineligible A58 touches the ball in an attempt to catch it at the B-30 (0:00). There were no eligible receivers in the area.
choice_a: Foul for illegal touching by A58.
choice_b: The half is over, at the option of Team B.
choice_c: No option -- A, 3/15 @ B-35, extend the quarter with one untimed down.
choice_d: No option -- A, 3/20 @ B-40, extend the quarter with one untimed down.
explanation: Ineligible A58 touching the pass is illegal touching (five yards from the previous spot and loss of down). At the option of Team B the penalty may be declined and the half ended, or accepted; because the foul occurred on the last timed down of the half, Team B chooses to end the half rather than accept an untimed down that would benefit Team A only by yardage.

question_text: Targeting Video: Wyoming vs. Toledo, Arizona Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #31 of the defense against QB #6 of the offense.
choice_b: Targeting with the crown of the helmet.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation. Per the CFO key, the crown-of-the-helmet element of targeting is present (forcible contact with the top-of-helmet six-inch crown). Answer depends on the specific video; keyed as targeting with the crown of the helmet.

question_text: Targeting Video: Notre Dame vs. Oregon State, Sun Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #32 of the defense against #24 of the offense.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as No Targeting: the contact does not meet the forcible-contact-to-head/neck-of-a-defenseless-player or crown-of-helmet criteria with an indicator. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Syracuse vs. South Florida, Boca Raton Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #19 of the defense against the QB #10 of the offense.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as Roughing the Passer with Targeting: the passer is a defenseless player and the contact both roughs the passer and satisfies targeting. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Texas vs. Oklahoma State, Big 12 Championship -- Evaluate the contact by #2 of the receiving team against #22 of the kicking team.
choice_b: Illegal Blind Side Block with Targeting.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as Illegal Blindside Block with Targeting: a forcible blindside block delivered from outside the opponent's field of vision that also meets targeting criteria. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Utah vs. Northwestern, Las Vegas Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #81 of the defense against #4 of the offense.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as Targeting. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Georgia Southern vs. Ohio, Myrtle Beach Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #15 of the defense against QB #13 of the offense.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as No Targeting. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Howard vs. Florida A&M, Celebration Bowl -- Evaluate the contact by #13 of the kicking team against #27 of the receiving team.
choice_c: Dead Ball Personal Foul with Targeting.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as a Dead-Ball Personal Foul without the targeting element. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Targeting Video: Michigan vs. Washington, National Championship Game -- Evaluate the contact by #2 of the defense against #2 of the offense.
choice_b: Targeting with the crown of the helmet.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as No Targeting. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Game Video: UCLA vs. Boise State, LA Bowl -- Evaluate the coaching staff interaction near the sideline with the Head Line Judge.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as an Automatic Unsportsmanlike Conduct foul — e.g., contacting/confronting the official or an act carrying an automatic UNS. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: Game Video: Kentucky vs Missouri -- Evaluate the contact by defender #0 against the QB #12 of the offense.
choice_a: No foul for Horse Collar Tackle.
explanation: Video-clip evaluation keyed as a Horse-Collar Tackle foul: grabbing inside the back or side collar/nameplate area and abruptly pulling the runner down, now prohibited anywhere on the field including the tackle box. Answer is video-dependent.

question_text: 3/4 @ A-36. A81 catches a pass and runs towards Team B's goal line. A81 drops the ball straight down at the B-1 after assuming he had already crossed the goal line. The officials make no signal, and the loose ball bounces into the end zone where it comes to rest with no player making an attempt to possess it.
explanation: A runner who voluntarily releases the ball short of the goal line has fumbled. With no player possessing the loose ball that comes to rest in the end zone and the impetus provided by Team A, the ball is dead where it came to rest belonging to Team A; it is placed at the spot of the fumble by rule — A, 1/Goal @ B-1. It is not a touchdown because A never possessed the ball in the end zone.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, 4Q (0:13). A11 carries to the B-14 where he is injured when tackled inbounds (0:05). Team A has no time outs remaining. On this play:
choice_a: There is no option for a 10-second subtraction.
choice_b: There is a 10-second subtraction option, but not if A68 also held B53.
choice_c: There is a 10-second subtraction option, but not if B79 also lost his helmet.
explanation: An injured player stopping the clock inside two minutes offers the opponent a 10-second subtraction only when the injury is the sole reason the clock stopped and specific timing conditions are met. Here there is no 10-second subtraction option; the presence or absence of an accepted foul or a separate helmet-off would change the analysis but on these facts no subtraction applies.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. B31 catches A7's punt at the B-4 and his momentum carries him into B's end zone. He runs around in the end zone, then fumbles with B26 recovering the loose ball while grounded at the B-4. During B31's run, B18 blocked A81 in the back at the B-8.
explanation: B31's momentum from catching the punt carries him into his own end zone, so the momentum exception applies and no safety results when he is downed (or the ball becomes dead) there. The illegal block in the back by B18 during the return is a Team B foul behind the basic (momentum) spot and is enforced from the spot of the foul; the enforcement yields Team B's ball at its 20 by rule of this play — keyed Safety, but see enforcement. Keyed answer.

question_text: B55 commits a taunting foul in the 3rd quarter. This is B55's second unsportsmanlike conduct foul of the game.
choice_a: B55 is ejected from the game.
choice_b: B55 will have a first-half suspension for the next game but can participate in pre-game warm-up activities.
choice_c: B55 must remain out of view of the field of play during the first half of the next game.
explanation: A second unsportsmanlike conduct foul results in ejection (Rule 9-2-6). The 2026 rule change provides a next-game first-half suspension carryover ONLY for targeting and fighting, not for a two-UNS ejection; the next-game suspension statements are therefore not supported, and an 'All of the above' answer is incorrect.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-40. Team A is in formation with B97, positioned over A66, lined up offside. Before the snap, A66 reaches without simulating the snap and pushes B97's helmet out of the neutral zone.
choice_a: Dead-ball foul, False Start by A66.
choice_c: Dead-ball foul, delay of game on B97.
explanation: With B97 lined up in the neutral zone/offside, A66 reaching across and pushing B97 (without simulating the snap) is nonetheless an abrupt action by an offensive lineman that is a dead-ball False Start by A66. The offense is responsible for not initiating this contact even though the defender was offside.

question_text: 2/8 @ A-8. A17 takes the snap and immediately turns and throws a backward pass to A88 who is standing at the A-4. A88 catches the ball, takes a step, and is then hit hard by B22. The contact causes A88 to fumble the ball into the air after which B22 moves forward and bats the airborne ball back into team A's EZ. The grounded ball is muffed by B44 and goes OOB across the sideline in the EZ.
explanation: A88's backward-pass fumble is batted by B22 back into Team A's end zone — an illegal bat by Team B. The illegal batting penalty (10 yards) is enforced, and because the new impetus/ball status favors Team A, the result is A, 1/10 @ A-18 rather than a safety, since Team B's illegal bat, not Team A's action, sent the ball into the end zone.

question_text: FK @ A-35. While the kick is in flight, B21 gives a valid fair catch signal at the far hash while B34 gives a waving signal at the waist at the near hash. B34 catches the kick at the B-10 in the near side zone, and the Team B coach requests that the ball be placed on the far hash.
explanation: B34 gives an invalid waving signal, so his catch is not a valid fair catch; on a free-kick catch after any waving signal inside the 25, the ball is placed at the B-25 anywhere between the hashes. The receiving coach may select the far hash: B, 1/10 @ B-25, far hash.

question_text: FK @ A-35. B19 and B28 are aligned shoulder-to-shoulder within two yards of each other at the B-20 and move forward, but make no contact with any Team A player while in that formation. B43 fields the kick at the B-10 and carries to the 50 where he is tackled.
explanation: Two Team B players aligned shoulder-to-shoulder within two yards and moving forward to form a wedge is an illegal wedge (a 15-yard Team B foul) even without contact. Enforced from the end of the kick, the result is D, 1/10 @ B-5.

question_text: FK @ A-35. A3 places the ball on the tee with A42 lined up near the ball. After the ready-for-play, A3 moves forward as if to kick the ball, but A42 crosses in front of him and kicks the ball. When the ball is kicked, A3 is directly behind the ball with three teammates on one side of the ball. A42 plants his non-kicking foot on the same side of the ball as the three teammates. A85 recovers the untouched kick at the A-46.
explanation: Team A must have at least four players on each side of the kicker at the kick. With A3 the apparent kicker but A42 actually kicking and three teammates on one side (improper distribution), Team A has a live-ball free-kick formation foul. Enforced five yards from the previous spot with a rekick: A, FK @ A-30.

question_text: FK @ A-20 following a safety. A6's kick goes out of bounds untouched at the B-35. What is Team B's best option?
explanation: A free kick out of bounds untouched gives Team B options; the best is to take the ball 30 yards beyond the free-kick line, which from the A-20 free-kick line is the 50 — B, 1/10 @ 50.

question_text: FK @ 50. A10's onside kick is first touched by A99 at the B-41 then recovered by A82 while grounded at the B-39. A82 was offside.
explanation: On an onside kick, Team A may recover beyond Team B's restraining line only if Team B touches first. Here A99 touches first (illegal touching) and A82 recovers; being offside is a separate matter, but the controlling result is Team B's ball at the spot of first illegal touching — B, 1/10 @ B-41.

question_text: FK @ A-35. A4's untouched onside kick never breaks the plane of Team B's restraining line and is recovered by A24 at the A-44. A88 blocked B95 at the A-46. What is Team B's best option?
explanation: An onside kick that never crosses Team B's restraining line and is recovered by Team A is a foul (kicking-team player may not touch/recover until eligible); additionally A88's early block adds a penalty. Team B's best option gives it the ball with the penalty enforced — B, 1/10 @ A-39.

tags: roughing the kicker;scrimmage kick formation;punt;player safety;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/5 @ A-30. The snap goes over the head of punter A7, who is standing at the A-16. He recovers the loose ball while still in the tackle box at the A-10, and is able to punt the ball away. Attempting to block the kick, B99 forcibly contact A7's kicking leg and knocks him to the ground. The kick is downed at the B-45.
explanation: A7 recovers the errant snap while still in the tackle box and punts; he retains kicker protection. B99 forcibly contacting the kicking leg and knocking him down is roughing the kicker (15 yards, automatic first down). Result: A, 1/10 @ B-45.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-40. While A9's punt is above B's end zone, A87 bats it back into the field of play. B35 picks up the loose ball at the B-4 and carries it to the B-20 where he is tackled. During the kick, B46 blocked A82 below the waist at the B-12.
explanation: A87 batting the kick in Team B's end zone is illegal touching; Team B may take a touchback and, if the foul is accepted, the illegal-touching spot is ignored. B46's block below the waist during the kick is a Team B foul enforced under post-scrimmage-kick rules from the basic spot, producing B, 1/10 @ B-6.

question_text: 4/5 @ A-20. A5's punt is first touched by A17 at the B-40. B28 picks up the loose ball at the B-35 and returns it to the B-45 where he fumbles. A66 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the 50. During the kick, B26 held A84 at the 50.
explanation: A17's first touching of the punt is illegal touching, giving Team A the ball at the spot of first touching (B-40) at the option of Team B, unless a foul is accepted. B26's hold during the kick is a Team B foul; Team A takes the illegal-touching spot but the best result by rule is A, 1/10 @ A-30 after enforcement — Team A retains possession at its own 30.

question_text: 4/15 @ A-40. B31 muffs A13's punt at the B-15, and A82 recovers the loose ball while grounded at the B-20. Prior to the kick, A82 stepped on the sideline on his own, but immediately returned inbounds.
explanation: A82 stepped out of bounds on his own and returned, then recovered the kick — that recovery is illegal touching by a player who was out of bounds, carrying loss of down from the previous spot. B31's muff does not give Team A the ball; the result is A, 4/20 @ A-35 with the down replayed and penalty from the previous spot.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-20. A11's field goal attempt is blocked behind the line of scrimmage, striking the ground at the B-18 and taking a reverse bounce. A78 muffs the loose ball at the B-22 with B96 recovering while grounded at the B-18.
explanation: A field-goal attempt blocked behind the neutral zone remains a scrimmage kick behind the line; the ball may be recovered by either team. B96's recovery of the loose ball while grounded ends the down with Team B in possession at the recovery spot — B, 1/10 @ B-22.

question_text: 4/10 @ B-30. A2's field goal attempt is wide left. During the kick, B75 twists and turns A68's face mask at the B-28.
explanation: On an untouched, unsuccessful field goal snapped from outside the 20, the post-scrimmage-kick spot is the previous spot (B-30). B75's face-mask foul at B-28 is behind the basic spot, so it is enforced from the spot of the foul (per AR 10-2-3-V), giving Team B the ball with the 15-yard penalty. Keyed B, 1/10 @ B-14; note strict spot-of-foul arithmetic from B-28 yields B-13, so verify the exact spot.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A8's kick is good, but Team A was in an illegal formation at the snap. After the kick, B46 is flagged for taunting.
choice_a: A, Try @ B-3. The fouls offset.
choice_b: A, Try @ B-4. Penalize both fouls before the replay of the Try.
choice_c: A, Try @ B-8. Penalize A's foul before the replay of the Try, and B's foul on the succeeding kickoff.
explanation: Team A's illegal formation on a successful try and B46's post-kick taunting are handled as a live-ball A foul and a dead-ball B foul. The try counts unless canceled; the standard administration penalizes both before the try is replayed — A, Try @ B-4 — because the fouls do not simply offset.

question_text: Try @ B-3 from the far hash. A14's pass intended for A32 falls incomplete; however, B5 is flagged for DPI in the far corner of the end zone. The Team A coach requests that the ball be placed at the near hash mark for the replay of the Try.
explanation: Defensive pass interference on a try is a foul at the spot; the try is replayed with the ball half the distance to the goal (from B-3 to B-1½). The offended team may select the hash for the replay: A, Try @ B-1½, near hash.

question_text: Try @ B-3. A5's kick is blocked. The loose ball is recovered by B28 and returned across A's goal line. During the return, the Line Judge had to run around members of Team B who had come out onto the field.
choice_c: 2 points for B. A, FK @ A-35.
explanation: On a blocked try returned by Team B across A's goal line, Team B can score two points. Team B substitutes coming onto the field and interfering with the official is a Team B sideline/participation issue; the two points count and the resulting penalty is enforced on the succeeding kickoff — 2 points for B, A, FK @ 50.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-20. First possession of the first extra period. A13's pass is intercepted by B42 at the B-10. He returns it to the B-20 where he fumbles with A56 recovering while grounded at the B-25.
explanation: In the extra period, once Team B intercepts, Team B may advance; if B fumbles and Team A recovers, the ball remains dead by rule in most cases, but here the recovery ends Team A's possession series. With B's interception and the sequence given, the series result is B's ball to begin its possession — keyed B, 1/10 @ A-25.

question_text: 3/10 @ B-15. First possession of the second extra period. A42 carries to the B-10 where he fumbles. B31 recovers the loose ball and returns it to the B-30 where he is tackled. Prior to the fumble, A68 is flagged for holding at the line of scrimmage, and during the return, B97 is flagged for an illegal blind side block.
explanation: In overtime, Team A's holding before the fumble and Team B's illegal blindside block during the return are offsetting-type live-ball fouls handled per extra-period rules; Team B ends in possession and begins its series with penalties administered, yielding B, 1/10 @ A-25.

tags: overtime;extra period;try;roughing the passer;penalty on the try;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: Try @ B-3. First possession of the third extra period. A6's pass is complete to A24 in the end zone for two points. B75 is flagged for roughing the passer.
explanation: A successful two-point try in overtime with roughing the passer by B is scored; the dead-ball/live-ball personal foul is enforced on Team B's ensuing possession series. Because the try is good, B begins its series with the penalty from the succeeding spot — B, Try @ A-18.

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5. A42 goes airborne toward the left GL pylon and the Head Line Judge rules touchdown. Video shows that the ball in his left hand crossed the sideline at the B-2 and continued in his possession beyond the goal line extended. Video also shows that when he touched the pylon with his right hand, the ball was at the B-1.
explanation: A ball in a runner's hand that crosses the sideline (out of bounds) at the B-2 before reaching the goal-line extended means the runner is out of bounds at the B-2; touching the pylon does not create a touchdown when the ball was already out of bounds. Result on review: A, 4/Goal @ B-2.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-5. Ball carrier A31 is contacted by B98 near the line of scrimmage and the ball comes loose, but the Line Judge rules forward progress at the A-4. Video shows that A31 lost control of the ball simultaneous with the contact by B98, and that in the immediate continuing action, B76 picked up the ball at the A-2 and carried it into A's end zone.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team B. B, Try @ A-3.
explanation: Replay can rule the runner fumbled (simultaneous with contact) because there is clear evidence of the loose ball and recovery in the immediate continuing action. B76's recovery and advance stands to the point supported by video: B, 1/Goal @ A-2; a touchdown is not awarded because the recovery/advance into the end zone is not shown with the required clarity for replay to place it there.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10, 2Q. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A9 carries to near the goal line and the Head Line Judge rules touchdown (0:43). Video shows that A9 was down with the ball at the B-1 (0:45).
explanation: Replay corrects the ruling to down at the B-1 and resets the game clock to the time the runner was actually down (0:45), starting on the Referee's ready signal: A, 1/Goal @ B-1, Ready (0:45).

question_text: 3/Goal @ B-5, 4Q. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A12 carries to near the goal line and the Line Judge rules touchdown (0:26). Video shows that A9 was down with the ball at the B-1 (0:28).
explanation: Replay rules the runner down at the B-1; because the clock was running inside two minutes and neither team has a timeout, a 10-second runoff applies, moving the clock from 0:28 to 0:18: A, 4/Goal @ B-1, Ready (0:18).

question_text: 3/5 @ B-20, 4Q. Neither team has a timeout remaining. A41 fumbles near the line to gain and the loose ball rolls OOB. Officials spot the ball at the B-14. Video shows that A41 fumbled at the B-16 and the loose ball rolled OOB at the B-12 (1:37).
explanation: Replay corrects the fumble spot to B-16 and the out-of-bounds spot to B-12; the fumble went forward out of bounds, so the clock is reset to the time of the fumble and starts on the ready (1:37): A, 4/1 @ B-16, Ready (1:37).

question_text: 3/10 @ B-45, 3Q. A10's pass is complete to A82 at the B-35. He runs to the B-30 where B17 tackles him by the face mask. Video shows that the clock was at 0:00 prior to the ball being snapped.
choice_a: Not reviewable. 4th quarter will begin A, 1/10 @ B-15.
choice_b: Reviewable. 4th quarter will begin A, 1/10 @ B-30.
choice_c: Reviewable. 4th quarter will begin A, 3/10 @ B-45.
explanation: Whether the clock had expired before the snap is reviewable. Video shows 0:00 before the snap, so the third-quarter play is nullified and the fourth quarter begins with the situation as it existed — but the defensive face-mask foul is administered as the fourth quarter opens: A, 1/10 @ B-30, reviewable.

question_text: 2/5 @ B-10, 4Q. Score: A-24, B-28, and neither team has a timeout remaining. A3 carries to near the goal line, and the Side Judge rules him down at the B-1 (0:00). Replay pages immediately to determine if A3 might have scored. Video is inconclusive as to the location of the ball when A3 was down, but clearly shows that 0:02 remained at that time.
explanation: With the score A-24, B-28, video is inconclusive as to whether A3 scored but clearly shows 0:02 remained when he was down. Because a potential score is inconclusive and there is no clear evidence to place time back or award a touchdown that would end/continue the game favorably, the ruling on the field (down, time expiring) stands and the game is over.

question_text: 3/10 @ A-20. Runner A34 takes a handoff and runs to the A-27 where B44, intentionally and with force, strikes him in the helmet with his forearm bringing him to the ground.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-42. Personal foul with targeting.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-43. Personal foul with targeting.
explanation: B44 intentionally and forcibly striking the runner in the helmet with the forearm is a personal foul (15 yards, automatic first down): A, 1/10 @ A-42. Absent the specific targeting indicators (defenseless player and forcible contact to the head/neck or crown), it is a personal foul without targeting.

tags: chop block;blocking below the waist;player safety;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/12 @ A-35. At the snap, RG A66 fires out and contacts B98 just above his waist at the LOS. He maintains contact as he slides down to A66's thigh area. At this point right tackle A77 blocks B98 also in the thigh area. This effectively neutralizes B98 as back A33 runs past him to the A-45 where he is tackled.
explanation: A66's high block sliding to the thigh combined with A77's simultaneous low block on the same defender (B98) is a chop block — a 15-yard personal foul. Enforced from the previous spot: A, 2/27 @ A-20. The gain is negated by the live-ball foul.

tags: chop block;blocking below the waist;formation;player safety;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/7 @ B-37. Back A22 is lined up in the backfield and immediately behind RT A77. While shotgun QB A17 calls out signals, A22 legally shifts to a position next to A17. A22 stays in that position, but never stops his feet from chopping in place. At the snap, blitzing linebacker B57 is blocked from the front and below the waist at the B-40 by A22. While still in the pocket, A17 completes a pass to A88 at the B-25.
explanation: A22, who never stopped his feet (an illegal shift/motion element) blocks the blitzing linebacker from the front and below the waist outside the free-blocking zone/after the ball has left — an illegal block below the waist (15-yard personal foul). Enforced from the previous spot yields A, 2/22 @ A-48.

question_text: 4/16 @ B-26. Team A is lined up to attempt a FG. At the snap, B55 runs forward from the B-20 and leaps into the air in an attempt to block the kick. B55 lands on A95's shoulders as the kick passes through the goal posts.
explanation: B55 running from beyond the neutral zone and leaping into the plane above an opponent to block the field goal is leaping — a 15-yard personal foul with automatic first down; under 2026 rules it no longer matters whether he landed on a player. Team A may take the successful field goal and free kick from the 50, or decline the score and take A, 1/10 @ B-13.

question_text: 3/8 @ B-48. Runner A34 is hit hard at the B-42 and loses possession of the ball. There is a scramble by both teams to gain possession of the fumble, and a pile of players from both teams is formed around the ball. As the officials are digging through the bodies to determine which team has possession, A77 grabs B22 (who is in the pile) by the back of the jersey and yanks him aside, giving a clear view of A88 holding tightly to the ball at the B-37.
explanation: Using forcible contact to pull an opponent off the pile is a dead-ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul (15 yards). Team A recovered its own fumble and ends with possession, but the dead-ball UNS is enforced from the succeeding spot against Team A: A, 1/10 @ A-48.

question_text: 4/9 @ A-31. A5 punts the ball down to the B-45 where it hits the ground and takes a high bounce. As it is returning to the ground, gunner A88 strikes it with a fist at the B-40 and the ball bounds downfield to the B-30 where punt receiver B33 recovers the kick and advances to the B-35 before going OOB.
explanation: A88 striking the loose kicked ball with his fist is illegal kicking/batting of a loose ball (a Team A foul). Team B may take the result of the play (its ball where the action left it) or the penalty; the options are B, 1/10 @ B-45 or A, 4/19 @ A-21.

tags: punt;out of bounds;illegal use of hands;player safety;sideline interference;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 4/9 @ A-31. A5 punts the ball down to the B-35 where B33 catches it and falls to the ground. While the kick was in flight, gunner A88 went OOB at the A-45 to avoid B22 who was attempting to block him. B22 followed A88 into the bench area, crossing the sideline at the 50-yard line, and knocked him to the ground at that point.
explanation: B22 following A88 across the sideline into the bench area and knocking him down is a personal foul against Team A during the kick; the 15-yard penalty is enforced under post-scrimmage-kick principles from the basic spot, yielding B, 1/10 @ B-20.

question_text: 1/Goal @ B-8. A17 runs an option play to his right and pitches the ball from the B-10 to back A34 at the B- 12. A34 runs out of bounds at the B-2. Just as A17 pitched the ball B56 commits a personal foul against him.
explanation: B56's personal foul against A17 as A17 pitched the ball is a live-ball Team B personal foul; enforcement is from the basic spot with the half-the-distance limitation near the goal line, giving Team A options: A, 1/Goal @ B-4 or A, 2/Goal @ B-2 depending on whether the penalty is added to the end-of-run spot or the previous spot.

question_text: 4/7 @ A-43. The snap goes over the head of kicker A5 and is laying on his team's goal line when A5 kicks it across the endline.
choice_a: Safety, 2 pts for Team B. Team A, FK @ A-20.
choice_b: Safety, 2 pts for Team B. Team A, FK @ A-10.
explanation: A5's illegal kick of the loose ball from on/behind his own goal line sending it across the end line: the impetus is Team A's, the ball becomes dead behind A's goal line, and it is a safety — two points for Team B, Team A free kicks from its 20.

tags: illegal use of hands;loose helmet;face mask;player safety;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 2/8 @ A-12. RT A77 uses his hand and arm with extended contact to push B98's helmet off his head. B98 then picks up the helmet, puts it back on his head and pursues runner A34 who he helps bring down at the A-28.
explanation: A77 using his hand/arm with extended contact to push B98's helmet off is illegal use of hands/hands to the head (a Team A foul); the offsetting consideration is B98 continuing to participate after losing his helmet. With Team A's foul and B98's illegal continued participation, the fouls offset and the down is replayed: A, 2/8 @ A-12.

question_text: 3/8 @ A-30. A17's pass is intercepted by B22 at the A-38. B22 returns the interception up the sideline and at the A-3 jumps into the air and does a complete flip landing on his feet in possession of the ball in the EZ.
choice_a: TD. A, Try @ A-18 or A, FK @ B-20.
explanation: B22 leaps and completes a full flip, landing on his feet in possession in the end zone. Because he maintained possession and was not down before reaching the end zone (no part of his body touched the ground short of the goal line to make him down there), the return is a touchdown-in-reverse for B is not scored; the ball is spotted where he was down — keyed B, 1/10 @ A-18.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-25. Team A is lined up to attempt a FG. With all team A players set, kicker A5 turns and trots toward his sideline while audibly requesting a different kicking shoe. As he crosses over the top of the numbers, the ball is snapped to holder A11 at the B-32 who rises to catch the ball. At the same time A5 turns up field and is wide open when A11 throws him a pass that he catches at the B-15 and then proceeds into the EZ for a TD.
choice_a: TD team A. A, Try @ B-18 or A, FK @ A-20.
explanation: A5 trotting toward the sideline to feign leaving before catching a pass is an unfair-tactics/hideout deception — an unsportsmanlike conduct foul negating the touchdown; the 15-yard penalty from the previous spot yields A, 4/20 @ B-40.

question_text: 3/7 @ A-40. QB A17 throws a pass that is intercepted by B22 and returned to the A-10. Prior to the pass the Line Judge contacted a team B assistant coach who was beyond the coaching line but not on the field of play.
choice_a: B, 1/Goal @ A-10. Sideline warning issued to team B.
explanation: An assistant coach beyond the coaching line but not on the field is a sideline-interference matter (warning/DOG progression), not a 15-yard personal foul, and the incidental contact by the official does not create a foul on Team A. Team B keeps its interception return with no additional yardage foul: B, 1/10 @ A-25.

question_text: 3/5 @ B-15. Runner A34 is at the B-3 when teammate A22 reaches out and knocks the ball out of A34's possession. The loose ball strikes the ground at the B-1 and rolls into the EZ where A88 recovers it while grounded.
explanation: A22 batting the ball forward out of A34's possession is an illegal bat by Team A (a foul). The loose ball into the end zone recovered by A88 is not a touchdown; the illegal-bat penalty is enforced and the down replayed: A, 3/3 @ B-13.

tags: free kick;blocking below the waist;kickoff;enforcement spot;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: FK @ A-35. A5's kick is caught at the B-10 and returned to the B-30. While the kick was in-flight, B55 blocked A22 from the front in the thigh at the B-40.
explanation: B55's block in the thigh (below the waist) from the front during a free kick is prohibited on free kicks; however Team A may decline the receiving-team foul and instead take the result that benefits it. The best Team A option is to replay the free kick: A, FK @ 50.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. A17 drops back to pass and is still within the tackle box at the B-37 when he flips the ball forward to avoid being sacked. Back A44 catches the ball at the B-32 and advances to the B-22. Prior to A44's catch and advance, B98 pulls off his helmet and taunts A17.
explanation: B98 pulling off his own helmet and taunting is a dead-ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul by Team B (15 yards, automatic first down). It is enforced from the succeeding spot after A44's completion and advance: A, 1/10 @ B-11, UNS.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-32. A5's punt lands at the B-35 and bounces backwards towards team A's goal line when gunner A88 kicks the loose ball forward from the B-40 and OOB at the B-30.
explanation: A88 kicking the loose punted ball forward is illegal kicking by Team A. Team B may decline and take its ball where the ball went out of bounds, or accept the penalty against Team A; the options are A, 4/18 @ A-22 or B, 1/10 @ A-22.

question_text: 4/5 @ B-20. With team A in scrimmage kick formation, B99 is lined up directly across from guard A66. At the snap, B99 immediately moves diagonally forward and contacts snapper A55 aggressively in the shoulder, knocking him backwards and to the ground. A5's FG attempt is successful.
choice_c: Three points for team A; Team A, FK @ 50.
explanation: When Team A is in scrimmage-kick formation with the snapper not on the end of the line, a defender may not contact the snapper until one second after the snap; B99's immediate aggressive contact is a 15-yard personal foul with automatic first down. Team A may take the successful field goal and free kick, or decline and take A, 1/Goal @ B-10.

question_text: FK @ A-35. A5's kick is high and deep and lands untouched in the EZ. While the ball was in flight, B33 blocked A5 from the front and above the waist at the A-38.
explanation: B33 blocking A5 (the kicker) from the front above the waist while the free kick is in flight is a foul against the kicker; with the kick landing untouched in the end zone (touchback), Team A takes the penalty from the previous spot with a rekick: A, FK @ 50.

question_text: 4/15 @ B-20. Prior to the snap B22 positions himself onto the back of teammate B99 to gain additional height in order to block team A's kick. A5's FG attempt is successful.
choice_a: Three points for team A; Team A, FK @ 50.
choice_c: Team A will decline the 5-yard penalty and take the three points.
explanation: B22 climbing onto a teammate's back before the snap to gain height to block the kick is a leverage/leaping-preparation foul; it is treated as a dead-ball foul enforced before the snap: A, 1/Goal @ B-10.

question_text: 3/7 @ A-38. On the third play of the game, A34 is stopped one yard short of the LTG near the sideline by a hard and aggressive, but legal, tackle by B55. After the play is ruled dead, the team B linebackers coach goes out on the field to enthusiastically congratulate B55. One team B substitute joins the assistant coach in the celebration.
explanation: An assistant coach coming onto the field to celebrate plus a substitute joining is a first sideline-interference infraction — a warning, no yardage: A, 4/1 @ A-44; sideline warning issued.

question_text: 2/8 @ B-42. A17's pass is deflected by A88 at the B-20 near the sideline. As the ball is falling to the ground, B22 intentionally strikes it with his foot to prevent A88 from attempting to catch it. The ball hits the ground OOB.
explanation: B22 intentionally striking the airborne loose ball with his foot to prevent a catch is illegal kicking of a loose ball by Team B (10-yard penalty). Enforced from the previous spot with a replay: A, 1/10 @ B-32.

question_text: 2/7 @ B-37. A17 takes a hand-to-hand snap and moves to his right parallel to the NZ. He then pitches the ball back to A34. To prevent being hit by B95, A34 bats the in-flight ball forward from the B-43 where A88 recovers it at the B-35 and advances to the B-30.
explanation: A34 batting the in-flight backward pass forward is an illegal bat by Team A. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul/previous spot and the down replayed: A, 2/23 @ A-47.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-30. LG A66 and RG A55 pull to their right to block for a power sweep play. A55 blocks B92 and knocks him to the ground. Trailing A66 jumps with his left foot foremost over prone B92 at the B-32 and turns up field to block for ball carrier A30, who is tackled at the B-24.
explanation: A66 jumping feet-first over a prone opponent while blocking is legal (hurdling restrictions apply to the ball carrier and to defensive leap-to-block situations, not to this block over a down player). No foul: A, 1/10 @ B-24.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-44. A34 is tackled very near the LTG and the officials call a time-out to measure. As the chain is being stretched out, QB A17 moves to a position to see the front end of the ball and the forward stake. To help him have a clearer vision of the results, he removes his helmet. The referee signals the spot is a half- yard short.
choice_c: A17 must sit out for one play.
explanation: A player removing his helmet on the field during a measurement (dead ball, but on the field of play) is a violation, but with the spot a half-yard short the primary result is the failed conversion of the down: A, 4/½ @ B-40½. Voluntary helmet removal here does not require sitting a play under these circumstances.

question_text: 4/8 @ A-32. Team A lines up with eight players on the line of scrimmage, a punter 10 yards deep, and two shield blockers in the backfield. When snapper A20 puts his hands on the ball, the eight players on the line of scrimmage are (from left to right): A41, A39, A8, A55, A20, A61, A49, A80. A8 then shifts into the backfield, and after one second, he goes in motion to the right and is outside A80 when the ball is snapped. Punter A96's kick goes OOB at the B-22.
choice_a: Legal play, there are seven lineman and four backs at the snap.
choice_b: Legal play, only A39, A20, and A49 are numbering exceptions, and they are interior lineman.
choice_c: Foul for Illegal Formation, A8 is lined up as an interior lineman when the snapper places his hands on the ball, and he therefore cannot be a back at the snap.
explanation: NEW FOR 2026: In scrimmage-kick formation, numbering-exception players become ineligible interior linemen when the snapper takes his position and may not then shift or move to another position. A8, an interior lineman when the snapper set, cannot become a back — his shift is a foul for illegal formation.

question_text: 3/2 @ B-22. After the previous down, the game was stopped to allow injured B4 to be attended by sideline personnel. While B4 is being slowly walked off the field, Team B has substituted for B4 and has 11 players on the field. Team A did not substitute, has 11 players set, with the snapper's hands on the ball. While B4 is still being walked off the field and the Center Judge (or Umpire) over the ball before the Ready for Play signal, RG A66 suddenly comes up out of his three-point stance, simulating action at the snap.
choice_a: No foul since the ball is not yet Ready for Play. A, 3/2 @ B-22.
explanation: Because the ball is not yet ready for play (the Center Judge/Umpire is still over the ball awaiting the ready while an injured player is escorted off), A66 coming up out of his stance is not yet a false start — no restriction is in force pre-ready: no foul, A, 3/2 @ B-22.

question_text: 4/1 @ B-31. Team A is lined up with the intention of gaining a first down. A32 is a back whose lateral position is just outside the right TE. All Team A players are set for one second, with QB A16 under center. Before the snap, A32 goes slowly in motion towards his left. As he approaches the QB, he starts to move forward to a position directly behind A16. When the ball is snapped, A32 is still moving forward and A32 pushes runner A16 in the back, between the center/RG gap. A16 becomes down by rule with the ball at the B-29.
choice_c: Illegally Helping the Runner; A, 4/6 @ B-36.
explanation: A32 still moving forward toward the line at the snap is illegal motion (a back in motion may not move toward the line of scrimmage at the snap); pushing the runner is a separate consideration but the motion foul controls: Illegal Motion, A, 4/6 @ B-36.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. B99 crosses the NZ into the gap between interior linemen A67 and A71. B99 does not contact any Team A player. A32, legally in the backfield, moves forward and intentionally reaches to contact B99.
explanation: B99 in the neutral zone who does not contact an opponent has not fouled by himself; A32 legally in the backfield moving forward and intentionally contacting B99 draws the offense's reaction into a dead-ball False Start because the defensive player's presence did not force the contact.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-20. B99 crosses the NZ into the gap between interior linemen A67 and A71. B99 does not contact any Team A player. After B99 stops and begins retreating, A67 reaches forward without getting out of his stance to touch B99, who is still not completely out of the NZ.
explanation: With B99 retreating and not out of the neutral zone, A67 reaching forward (without leaving his stance) to touch B99 is a dead-ball False Start; the defender's mere non-forcing presence in the neutral zone does not make it offside on B.

question_text: When evaluating whether a passer is beyond the line of scrimmage during a forward pass play, the neutral zone is:
choice_a: A line; a legal forward passer must have a body part on the ground in or behind the neutral zone.
choice_b: A plane; a legal forward passer must have a body part behind the plane of Team B's side of the neutral zone.
explanation: For determining whether a passer is beyond the line on a forward pass, the neutral zone is a plane; a legal forward passer must have a body part behind the plane of Team B's side of the neutral zone — it is not merely a line requiring ground contact.

tags: illegal use of hands;pass interference;offensive pass interference;enforcement spot;2024-cfo-rules-test;NCAA;UIL
question_text: 3/14 @ A-26. A12 receives the snap and rolls out to his right. A32 runs into the side zone and turns towards A12 while standing still at the A-30. B52, sensing A12 wants to throw to A32, puts both hands into A32's back and pushes him face-first into the ground. A12 keeps the ball and scrambles to the A-29.
choice_b: Illegal block in the back. A, 3/4 @ A-36.
choice_c: Illegal use of hands. A, 1/10 @ A-36.
choice_d: Illegal use of hands. A, 1/10 @ A-39.
explanation: B52 pushing a stationary potential receiver face-first into the ground with both hands to the back, before any pass, is illegal use of hands by Team B (not a block in the back on a defender and not pass interference since no pass was thrown). The 10-yard penalty with automatic first down is enforced: A, 1/10 @ A-39.

question_text: 2/6 @ B-36. A10 receives the snap and throws to A4, who is running up the sideline. Defender B30 grabs A4's facemask at the B-23 while the ball is in the air, turning A4's helmet so that the catch cannot be made. The pass falls incomplete.
choice_c: Both (a) and (b) are correct; (b) is the obvious choice to Team A.
explanation: B30 grabbing A4's face mask while the ball is in the air, turning the helmet so the catch cannot be made, is both a face-mask personal foul and defensive pass interference. The offended team may choose the more advantageous enforcement; both options are correct and Team A takes the 15-yard spot: A, 1/10 @ B-21.

question_text: 2/12 @ A-38. Receiver A88 is held by B22 at the A-40 and then runs a slant pattern. A16's pass is tipped behind the line of scrimmage, completed to A88 and advanced to the A-44.
explanation: B22 holding A88 before the pass is defensive holding (10 yards, automatic first down). Enforced from the previous spot: A, 1/10 @ A-48. The completed tipped pass and advance are declined in favor of the first down and better spot.

question_text: 4/Goal @ B-3. A18 receives a deep snap and scrambles. B40 pursues A18 and strips him of the ball at the B-9. A44 picks up the fumble at the B-7 and runs out of bounds at the B-1.
explanation: A18's fumble recovered by A44 who runs out of bounds — because it is a Team A fumble recovered and advanced by Team A (not the fumbler) beyond the spot is limited by rule, but here A44 recovers behind the spot and is downed out of bounds at the recovery/advance spot: B, 1/10 @ B-9 on downs, since Team A failed to make the line to gain on fourth down.

question_text: 1/10 @ A-30. A33 takes the handoff and begins a long gain. He releases the ball at the B-1, thinking he has already scored a touchdown. The ball first lands in the end zone, then bounces to the B-4 where it becomes still. No player from either team moves towards the ball to possess it.
explanation: A33 voluntarily releasing the ball at the B-1 is a fumble; the loose ball lands in the end zone and bounces back to the B-4 where it comes to rest with no one recovering. With Team A's impetus and no recovery, the ball is dead belonging to Team A at the B-4 (the spot where it stopped): A, 1/Goal @ B-4.

question_text: When Team A intentionally covers up receiver A85 who is lined up on the line of scrimmage, which of the following must be true?
choice_a: A85's head must break the waist of the nearest interior lineman.
choice_b: A85's head must break the waist of the snapper.
choice_c: All Team A backs must be in the backfield enough to not break the waist of the nearest lineman.
explanation: For a legally covered end (intentional coverage of A85 on the line), the covering teammate's head must break the waist of the snapper, and all Team A backs must be positioned in the backfield so as not to break the waist of the nearest lineman. Both conditions apply.

question_text: 2/10 @ B-30. B16 intercepts a forward pass in the EZ. During B16's return, he fumbles while still in the EZ. The loose ball bounces forward to the B-4, where it is muffed by A66. A66's muff causes the loose ball to return to the EZ and over the end line.
explanation: B16's interception in the end zone gives Team B the momentum exception when he is subsequently forced back; A66's muff does not provide a new impetus (muffing a rolling loose ball does not change impetus). The ball going out of the end zone off Team A's muff, with Team B's momentum in effect, is a safety only if Team B's own action returned it — here the muff is Team A's, so it is a safety by rule: keyed Safety, B, FK @ B-20.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-24. B30 makes an acrobatic interception that has him going to the ground to complete the catch. B30 is airborne when he establishes firm control of the ball at the B-3, first touches the ground with his foot when the ball is at the B-1, and then falls to the ground with sustained firm control when the ball is fully in the end zone.
explanation: B30 establishes control at the B-3 (airborne), first touches the ground with the ball at the B-1, then falls with control fully in the end zone. Because he first touched inbounds at the B-1 while completing the catch, the interception is completed at the B-1; but continuing to the end zone with the impetus from the pass makes it a touchback: B, 1/10 @ B-20.

question_text: 2/3 @ B-23. B50 intercepts a pass in his EZ. A79 grabs B50's facemask and tackles him to the ground while still in the end zone.
explanation: B50 intercepts in his own end zone and is fouled (face mask) there by A79. Team B will take the ball out to the 20 (interception in the end zone, no return) and add the 15-yard personal-foul penalty from the succeeding spot: B, 1/10 @ B-35.

question_text: Free Kick @ A-35. Receiver B22 catches the kick in the EZ. As he begins his return, he fumbles from the EZ and the ball rolls out of bounds at the B-4.
explanation: B22 catches the free kick in the end zone and fumbles from the end zone with the ball going out of bounds at the B-4. The ball belongs to Team B; because the fumble originated in the end zone and the ball went out of bounds beyond the goal line, Team B is awarded the ball at the B-25 (touchback-type placement after a free-kick catch): B, 1/10 @ B-25.

question_text: 4/12 @ B-42. After Team A punts, deep returner B10 muffs the ball at the B-5. Seeing that A20 is about to recover the ball, B10 bats the ball from the B-4 into the end zone. B20 falls on the ball there.
explanation: B10 muffs the punt at the B-5 and then intentionally bats the loose ball from the B-4 into his own end zone where B20 recovers. Team B provided the impetus that put the ball into the end zone (the illegal bat/new force by B), so it is a safety: B, FK @ B-20.

question_text: 3/4 @ B-24. B22 intercepts a pass in the EZ and fumbles while still behind the goal line. The ball bounces to the B-4 where B90 is about to fall on it. A88 bats the ball towards his own goal line to prevent B90 from possessing. The ball goes out of bounds at the B-12.
explanation: B22 intercepts in the end zone and fumbles; A88 batting the loose ball back toward Team B's goal is an illegal bat by Team A, but the impetus for the ball entering the end zone remains Team B's original possession act. With Team B's ball and the impetus theirs, the result is B, 1/10 @ B-20.

question_text: 2/6 @ B-36. B2 intercepts a pass in the EZ and starts to return it. While B2 is still in the EZ, A78 grabs and twists his facemask, causing him to fumble. B5 falls on the ball at the B-8.
explanation: B2 intercepts in his end zone; A78's face-mask foul in the end zone causes the fumble recovered by B5 at the B-8. Team B keeps possession; the 15-yard personal foul is enforced from the succeeding spot (the 20 after the end-zone possession) giving B, 1/10 @ B-15 — a Team A personal foul in Team A's attacking end zone against a Team B player in Team B's end zone is enforced from the 20.

question_text: 1/10 @ 50, 4Q (0:14), tie game. A16 throws a pass to A80, who is tackled in bounds at the B-2 (0:02). B99 is flagged for DOF, and Team A wants to decline the penalty.
choice_a: Team A has the option of starting the game clock on the snap.
choice_b: No game clock option is available since the penalty was declined.
choice_c: No game clock option is available since the score is tied.
explanation: Even when Team A declines a defensive delay-of-game/dead-ball foul, if the foul stopped a clock that would otherwise be running, Team A has the option to start the game clock on the snap: Team A has the option of starting the game clock on the snap.

question_text: 2/8 @ B-28. A14 throws a pass that is caught by A88 for a TD. A81 was called for Illegal Motion since he was moving towards the line of scrimmage at the snap.
choice_a: A, 2/13 @ B-33, Ready due to the completed pass.
choice_b: A, 2/13 @ B-33, Snap since the previous down ended with a touchdown.
explanation: A81 moving toward the line at the snap is illegal motion, negating the touchdown; because the previous down would have ended in a score (clock stopped), after enforcement the clock starts on the snap: A, 2/13 @ B-33, Snap.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-20. During Team A charged timeout, both teams return to the field and the Referee and Umpire observe Team A having 12 players in formation for more than three seconds before A80 realizes he shouldn't be on the field and runs to the sideline. The Center Judge (or Umpire) is still over the ball, waiting for the Ready for Play signal for the 25-second play clock to start.
choice_c: No foul for Illegal Substitution during a charged team timeout.
explanation: During a charged team timeout there is no illegal-substitution foul for having 12 players in formation as long as the extra player departs before the ball is ready for play and the play clock has not started; here A80 leaves before the ready: no foul for illegal substitution during a charged team timeout.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-45. Team A is at an up-tempo speed and has 11 players in formation. Wideout A80 is quickly replaced by substitute A88, who gets set for one second. The Referee recognizes the substitution and gives the "T" signal. A66 snaps the ball before the Center Judge (or Umpire) can get to the snapper to communicate the start of substitution mechanics. Team A has no prior illegal actions related to offensive substitutions.
choice_a: Play is shut down, reset to A, 1/10 @ B-45. Any subsequent such action by Team A will result in Unsportsmanlike Conduct.
choice_b: Play is shut down, reset to A, 1/10 @ B-45. Any subsequent such action by Team A will result in Delay of Game.
explanation: On an up-tempo snap that beats the officials' substitution mechanics after a defensive substitution is recognized, the play is shut down and reset with no foul on the first occurrence; a warning is given that any subsequent such action will be unsportsmanlike conduct: A, 1/10 @ B-45, warning.

question_text: 1/10 @ B-30. Running back A32 runs the ball and advances to the B-24. A32 loses his helmet when he is tackled (no foul) by B55. The Umpire recognizes B55 is injured and calls an official's timeout so the Team B medical personnel can attend to B55.
explanation: Although A32 (offense) lost his helmet (which alone would set a 25-second play clock), the official's timeout is called for B55's injury (a Team B injury timeout), which sets the play clock to 40 seconds: 40-second play clock.

question_text: 2/4 @ B-24, 2Q (1:11). A16 throws a pass downfield and it is intercepted by B18, who runs OOB at the B- 30 (1:03). B99 was in the NZ at the snap.
explanation: B99 offside at the snap is a live-ball foul; Team A declines the interception result and accepts the five-yard penalty from the previous spot. Because the offside stopped/affected the clock before the Two-Minute Timeout, the clock starts on the ready: A, 1/10 @ B-19, Ready.

question_text: 3/8 at the A-35. QB A1, under a heavy rush, scrambles up the middle and to his left. As he is scrambling, he and the ball go completely across the A-37 and then he retreats backwards to the A-35, where he throws a forward pass to A2 at the A-45. A2 stepped out of bounds on his own, re-established inbounds prior to the pass being thrown, and then completed the catch at the A-45. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-45. The pass is a legal completion since A1 never had control of the ball while fully across the line.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-45. A2's illegal touching foul results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_d: A, 4/13 @ A-30, with A2 also flagged for illegal touching, penalties enforced together.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/13 @ A-30. A1 commits a foul for an illegal forward pass since he and the ball went completely beyond the line of scrimmage and then retreated behind it before he threw a forward pass. This foul is enforced from the spot of the foul, where the pass was thrown, and is a loss of down. A2 potentially commits a foul for illegal touching since he went out of bounds on his own and was the first to touch the pass after re-establishing inbounds; however, because the forward pass was already illegal, the illegal touching rule does not separately apply. The only foul on this play is the illegal forward pass.

question_text: 2/7 at the A-28. QB A15 completes a legal forward pass to A32 at the A-34. A32 runs to the A-38 where he is hit and fumbles the ball. The ball is rolling loose in the field of play at the A-40 when B7 touches the ball while his right foot is touching out of bounds. B7 then recovers the ball at the A-41 with his body completely inbounds. The on-field officials signal first down for Team B. Is this reviewable, and what is the clock status?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-41, Ready. B7's recovery counts since his body was completely inbounds when he gained possession.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-40, Ready. The ball is returned to the spot where B7 first touched it rather than the original spot of the fumble.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-41, Snap. This ruling is not reviewable since it is a judgment call on a fumble recovery.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-38, Ready. B7 is considered out of bounds since his right foot is touching out of bounds, and once he touches the ball while out of bounds, the ball is also considered to have gone out of bounds. The ball was fumbled at the A-38 and subsequently touched at the A-40, resulting in a fumble forward out of bounds — the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble. This play is reviewable, and replay will reverse the on-field ruling to Team A's ball, adjusting the clock as appropriate. The clock starts on the ready for play.

question_text: 4/7 at the B-39, 0:52 remaining in the 2nd quarter. Team A breaks their huddle and quickly lines up in a swinging gate scrimmage kick formation with 5 players on the line of scrimmage to the left of the formation, a snapper on the ball, another player to his right near the numbers, 3 shield protectors, and the punter. At the snap the punter holds the ball for a second and begins to run with it to his right, then stops and throws a high pass deep toward the end zone where it falls incomplete. At the snap, interior lineman A44 goes downfield more than 3 yards. End A80 is cut off from his route by a Team B player, clearly while the pass was in the air. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-39, Snap. Team B is flagged for defensive pass interference since A80 was clearly cut off from his route.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-39, Snap, :25, with Team A also penalized for A44's ineligible-downfield-pass foul, which cannot be declined.
choice_d: A, 4/12 @ B-39, Snap. The incomplete pass results in loss of down, but Team A retains possession due to the trick formation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-39, Snap, :25. A44 commits a foul for being ineligible downfield on a pass since he is more than 3 yards downfield, but this foul will be declined by Team B.
