There is no foul for defensive pass interference because the punter simulated a punt by throwing the ball high and deep. The clock starts on the snap following the incomplete pass and the turnover on downs.

question_text: 4/3 at the B-8. Team A is behind 17-14 late in the 4th quarter with 1:34 left in the game and the clock running. Team A breaks quickly to the line of scrimmage in a scrimmage kick formation. All players are set when the interior line goes from a 2-point stance to a 3-point stance in a simultaneous, abrupt movement causing the defense to react. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/3 @ B-8. The stance change is legal since all players were set before moving.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-8. Team B is awarded the ball due to Team A's deceptive formation.
choice_c: A, 4/8 @ B-13, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than following penalty enforcement.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/8 @ B-13. Team A commits a foul for a false start since their abrupt, simultaneous movement was designed to draw the defense offside and gain a first down. The clock starts on the ready for play following penalty enforcement.

question_text: 2Q, game clock :04. 4/8 at the B-28. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked by B98 behind the line of scrimmage and caroms high into the air. B32 jumps up and bats the ball forward at the B-27. The ball is subsequently recovered after a mad scramble by A85 at the B-35 with no time on the game clock. The Line Judge has a flag down for an illegal bat. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-35, No clock. Team A declines the penalty and simply takes the result of the play with no untimed down.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-27, Untimed down. The illegal bat awards Team B possession at the spot of the foul.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-18, No clock. Since time had already expired, there is no option for an untimed down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-18, Untimed down. The block of the kick by B98 is legal, but the bat by B32 is illegal since the ball was batted forward in the field of play. The result of the play is Team A's ball at the B-35. Team A accepts the penalty for the illegal bat, enforced from the previous spot, and because of Team A's acceptance the penalty extends the period for one untimed down.

question_text: 2Q, game clock :15. 3/2 at the A-42. Team B leads 35-14, and has 2 timeouts to Team A's 1 remaining. QB A1 throws a pass toward the sideline which WR A2 catches and is tackled inbounds at the A-39 with the game clock at :09. A68 and B10 continue to push after the play, and A67 rushes in and grabs B10's facemask, ripping his helmet off. Officials have a flag for a dead ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul on A67. The clock continued to run to :00. The crew discusses and decides there were :03 on the clock when the foul occurred. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/20 @ A-24, :00. The clock reads zero regardless of Team B's runoff decision since the half is effectively over.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-24, :03. The dead ball personal foul results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_d: A, 4/25 @ A-19, :03. The 15-yard penalty is enforced from the previous spot instead of the succeeding spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/20 @ A-24, :03. The foul stopped the clock, so the penalty includes a 10-second runoff option. If Team B wants the runoff, Team A would most likely not use a timeout to avoid it and the half would be over. If Team B does not want the runoff, it is 4th and 20 at the A-24 and the clock starts on the snap; B10 would be allowed to stay in the game since his helmet came off due to a foul.

question_text: 1st quarter, 5:27 on the clock. 2/10 at the A-17. Team A completes a long pass to the B-45 in the middle of the field, making it 1/10 at the B-45. The ball is made ready for play and the clock starts. Team A hurries to the line and does not substitute while Team B is substituting multiple players. QB A1 quickly looks over the defense, notices Team B is in the middle of a substitution, and quickly initiates his snap count. As center A62 double-clutches the snap, B21 is one step away from the Team B sideline. Both the Line Judge and Side Judge have flags down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-17. The illegal-participation foul for more than 11 players on defense is enforced instead as the more severe infraction.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. Both fouls offset since they occur on the same snap, and the down is replayed.
choice_d: A, 1/15 @ 50, Snap. The clock starts on the snap rather than the ready-for-play signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15 @ 50, Ready. There is a flag for an illegal snap, which correctly shuts the play down, and a flag for more than 11 players on defense at the snap. By rule, the snap infraction is a dead ball foul and stops the play and the clock. Because the snap infraction is a dead ball foul, there cannot also be a foul for more than 11 players on defense, since that is a live ball foul. The Referee's announcement should include the illegal snap and the reason there is no foul for too many players on defense.

question_text: 2nd quarter, 8:28 on the clock. 2/16 at the A-19. Team A has 3 receivers lined up on the right side of the formation; QB A2 is in shotgun with a back to his left. A receiver is on the A-19 to the left on the numbers. On the right side, a receiver is between the numbers and the 1-yard hash mark, another is lined up just outside the hash mark, and another is lined up just outside the tackle position on the A-16. The defenders are not in press coverage. QB A2 completes a pass inbounds to the middle receiver at the A-42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 2/16 @ A-19, Ready. The play is nullified due to one of the receivers lining up beyond the line of scrimmage.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-42, Snap. The illegal formation results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-42, Snap. The clock starts on the snap since no foul stopped play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-42, Ready. Officials should not be overly technical with a wide receiver or slot back in determining whether they are off the line of scrimmage — when in question, it is not a foul. Line of scrimmage officials should focus on keeping the formation and alignment legal without nitpicking close calls. The result of the play is a first down for Team A.

question_text: 3/5 at the B-9. Q4, 1:02 remaining. Team A trails 36-21 and is out of timeouts. QB A3 scrambles in the pocket and, while standing at the B-13, throws a screen pass back to A70, who catches it near the B-13 outside the numbers and runs for a touchdown. There are no flags, as the Line Judge signaled that it was a backward pass. The game clock reads 0:55 at the end of the play. Prior to the try, replay has indisputable video evidence that A70 was at the B-12 when he first touched the pass. What is the ruling, including the game clock?
choice_b: A, 4/15 @ B-9, Snap. Replay creates a foul for intentional grounding instead of illegal touching.
choice_c: A, 3/10 @ B-14, Ready. The game clock resets to the time of the snap due to a 10-second subtraction.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-13, Snap. The illegal touching results in loss of possession rather than a 5-yard penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10 @ B-14, Snap. A70 has committed a foul for illegal touching of a forward pass, a 5-yard penalty from the previous spot; illegal touching does not carry a loss of down. Replay cannot create a foul for intentional grounding, but it can rule on whether the pass was forward or backward and who touched it. This is not intentional grounding since there is no indication the passer was attempting to conserve yardage or time. The game clock remains at 0:55 with no adjustment or 10-second subtraction.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-35. Late in Q2, while scrambling, QB A1 fumbles the ball as he is being tackled at the B-43. B77 recovers it while on the ground at the B-45. The game clock is at 0:30 when the field officials award the ball to Team B at the end of the down. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A1's knee was down prior to the fumble. Additionally, B56's helmet came off during the down. What is the ruling, including the game clock?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-45, :30. Since B77 recovered the fumble, Team B is awarded a new series.
choice_c: A, 4/18 @ B-43, :20. A 10-second runoff applies due to B56's helmet coming off during the play.
choice_d: A, 4/18 @ B-43, :30, Snap, with B56 allowed to remain in the game since his helmet came off inadvertently.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/18 @ B-43, :30. Replay overturns the ruling since A1's knee was down before the fumble, giving Team A the ball back at the B-43. There is no adjustment to the game clock and no 10-second runoff due to the helmet coming off; the clock remains at 0:30 and starts on the ready for play. B56 must sit out for one play unless Team B opts to use a timeout to bring him back in.

question_text: 3/15 at the B-25. Score A-17, B-3. 0:42 remaining in the 2nd quarter. Team A has 1 timeout, Team B has none. A83 catches a pass at the B-24 and runs to the B-10 where he is tackled inbounds. Replay stops the game with 0:29 remaining to review the spot. Replay determines the runner was down at the B-11, inbounds, with 0:36 remaining on the clock. What are all the clock options?
choice_a: A, 4/1 @ B-11, :26, with the clock starting on the snap regardless of Team A's timeout status.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-11, :36. The reversal awards Team B the ball at the spot of the overturned ruling.
choice_d: A, 4/1 @ B-11, :29. The clock is reset to the time when replay stopped the game rather than the corrected time.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 @ B-11, :36. Team A has one timeout remaining and may use it to avoid the 10-second subtraction. This play is reviewable since the line to gain is involved and a reversal by replay would cause the clock to go from stopped to running. If Team A does not use their timeout, the reversal results in a 10-second subtraction and the clock is set to 0:26, starting on the ready, with the play clock set to 25. If Team A uses their timeout, the clock remains at 0:36 and starts on the snap.

question_text: 3/8 at the A-30. Score A-52, B-31, 1:40 remaining in the 4th quarter. Tight End A12 is positioned on the line of scrimmage 1 yard outside the tackle position (normal splits). At the snap, A12 blocks B19 from the side, below the waist, back towards the snap at the A-30. A21 takes the handoff from A10 and runs the ball to the A-42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/23 @ A-15, with the block ruled legal since A12 is a tight end regardless of alignment.
choice_b: A, 3/8 @ A-42, with the foul declined since the run gained positive yardage.
choice_c: A, 3/33 @ A-5. The penalty is enforced from the end of the run rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/23 @ A-15. A12 commits a foul for an illegal block below the waist. That foul occurred at the previous spot and is enforced for 15 yards. A12 is lined up in a position outside the tackle box at the snap and is restricted from blocking below the waist; his block occurred from the side and back towards the original position of the ball.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-20, 0:06 remaining in the 2nd quarter. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked and picked up on the run by B33. B33 slows down as he approaches the goal line, and long snapper A59, hustling full speed the entire way, strips the ball at the A-1 and the ball rolls into the end zone. A4 then intentionally bats the ball away from B33, who is trying to recover it, and the ball rolls over the end line. The clock reads 0:00. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Safety, 2 points for Team A. A4's illegal bat in the end zone results in a safety against Team B.
choice_c: B, 1/Goal @ A-1.5, No clock. There is no option for an untimed down since the foul is a loss-of-down penalty.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-1.5, Untimed down. Team A retains possession since B33 never crossed the goal line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/Goal @ A-1.5, Untimed down. This play is reviewable to determine if B33 crossed the goal line prior to the ball coming loose. Team B is responsible for putting the ball in Team A's end zone, so there is no safety since Team A is not responsible for the ball being in their own end zone. A4 commits a foul for an illegal bat in the end zone, enforced from the end of the related run (the A-1) since it is no longer a kick play — half the distance. Team B has the option to extend the period for one untimed down even though the illegal bat carries a loss of down.

question_text: 2/Goal at the B-8. QB A13 throws a pass to WR A80, who is about a yard deep in the end zone and closely guarded. A80 comes back for the ball, jumps, and controls it as the ball is over the goal line. The defender is also going for the ball and nudges A80 back into the field of play. A80 lands with the ball at the B-1 yard line with his chest to the goal line, then lunges forward and is stopped at the B-1/2 yard line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A. A80's lunge forward completes the process of the catch into the end zone.
choice_c: A, 3/Goal @ B-1. The spot reverts to where A80 first landed with the ball rather than where he was stopped.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-1. The defender's contact is ruled defensive pass interference, giving Team A a first down at the 1.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/Goal @ B-0.5. A80 completes the process of the catch and lands on his feet, then makes a second act by lunging forward, with his progress stopped at the B-1/2 yard line. Because of his second act, the ruling of forward progress and a potential score do not come into play.

question_text: 2/11 at the A-49. QB A8 drops straight back into the pocket and, while under pressure from the A-41, throws a pass downfield where the ball lands in the field of play at the B-21. There were no receivers in the area where the pass landed. As A8 dropped back into the pocket, A77 jerked B88 to the ground at the A-42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 2/21 @ A-39. Team B accepts the holding penalty instead of the intentional grounding foul.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-41. The intentional grounding foul results in loss of possession rather than loss of down.
choice_d: A, 3/19 @ A-41, with both fouls enforced together for a combined 20-yard penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/19 @ A-41. Team A commits multiple live ball fouls on this play. A77 commits a foul for offensive holding, which would be 10 yards from the previous spot, but that foul will most likely be declined, as A8 has committed a foul for intentional grounding since he is under duress, still in the tackle box, and throws a pass into an area without an eligible receiver. This foul is a spot foul and carries a loss of down, making it the more likely option for Team B.

question_text: 2/17 at the B-23. QB A7 drops straight back into the pocket and, from the B-31, throws a forward pass into the end zone where it is intercepted by B4. B4 advances from the Team B end zone into the field of play and is tackled at the B-20. Before the snap, left defensive tackle B52, who is lined up tight to the neutral zone, moves forward before the ball is snapped and attempts to move back to his original position on the line of scrimmage before the snap. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-18. Team B keeps the ball following the interception despite the pre-snap foul.
choice_b: A, 2/17 @ B-23. The offside foul is declined since the interception return gained significant yardage.
choice_c: A, 2/12 @ B-18, Snap, with the down not replayed since the foul occurred before the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/12 @ B-18. Team B commits a foul for encroachment/offside. That foul is enforced from the previous spot, 5 yards, and the down is replayed. Team B does not get to keep the ball despite the interception, since they fouled prior to the change of possession.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-13. A27 takes a handoff and runs to the B-4, where B10 grabs his facemask and pulls off his helmet. A27 stumbles, but regains his balance and runs to the B-2 before diving into the end zone. As A27 crosses the goal line, B10 delivers a blow to the side of his head with his forearm. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/Goal @ B-1, with A27 required to leave the game for one play since his helmet came off during the run.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-2. The dead ball unnecessary roughness foul results in a turnover on downs.
choice_d: A, 1/Goal @ B-1, with both fouls enforced from the same spot rather than separately.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-1. There is no foul for A27 participating without a helmet, since the play was over as soon as his helmet came off. The live ball facemask foul is enforced from the end of the related run (the B-4), and the dead ball unnecessary roughness foul is enforced from the succeeding spot. A27 may remain in the game because his helmet came off as a direct result of a foul. The clock starts on the ready for play.

question_text: 4/1 at the B-38. Team A's field goal attempt comes up short. B26 catches the kick five yards deep in the end zone and begins to run. During the return, B33 blocks a Team A player in the back at the B-15. As B26 continues the return, A41 blocks a Team B player in the back at the 50 in his attempt to get to B26. B26 continues and carries the ball across Team A's goal line. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. A41's block in the back is also a foul, offsetting B33's penalty.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. The illegal block in the back is enforced from the end of the return rather than the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B, 1/Goal @ B-1, Snap. The penalty is enforced from Team A's goal line as a safety-adjacent spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-7.5, Snap. B33 commits a foul for an illegal block in the back at the B-15, enforced from the spot of the foul since it occurred behind the end of the related run. A41 has not fouled, since he is legally able to block in the back in an attempt to get to the ball carrier. The foul is enforced half the distance and Team B keeps the ball. The clock starts on the snap following enforcement of Team B's penalty.

question_text: 3/15 at the A-40. QB A1 throws a deep pass that is intercepted by B5 at the B-20. B5 progresses upfield to the B-30 where a Team A player strips the ball from his control. The ball is then recovered and possessed by A10 at the B-35. A10 advances the ball to the B-25 where he is hit and fumbles. B1 recovers the fumble at the B-20 and runs downfield to Team A's end zone for an apparent touchdown. Before A1's pass attempt, offensive tackle A75 is flagged for illegal hands to the face by the Umpire. What is the ruling?
choice_a: TD for Team B, and the touchdown is nullified due to A75's personal foul during the down.
choice_b: TD for Team B. Try from the A-1.5 only, with no option to enforce on the succeeding kickoff.
choice_c: TD for Team B. Try from the A-1.5 or Team B kickoff from 50.
choice_d: No score. The touchdown is negated and the ball returns to the spot of A75's foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: TD for Team B. Try from the A-1.5 or Team B kickoff from 50. During the down, A75 is flagged for an illegal hands-to-the-face foul, which would normally be enforced from the previous spot if applicable. However, since the result of the play is a touchdown for Team B, the personal foul committed by A75, a member of the non-scoring team, can instead be enforced on either the Try or the succeeding kickoff, at Team B's option.

question_text: 4/8 at the 50. A9 punts, and the ball bounces at the B-5. A80 jumps in the air and controls the ball while airborne at the B-2. A80 gets a foot down at the B-1, falls to the ground where he hits the ground in the end zone, and the ball pops out of his control, not touching the ground in the end zone, and lands in the field of play where B45 picks up the ball at the B-1 and runs to the B-16 where he is tackled. At the snap, Team A had more than 4 players in the backfield. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/Goal @ B-1. A80's recovery of his own kick counts since he got a foot down before losing the ball.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-16, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the illegal touching rather than the dead ball spot.
choice_c: A, 4/13 @ A-45, Snap only, with no option to enforce at the dead ball spot instead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-21, Snap. When A80 controls the ball while airborne, that's only part of the catch process; getting a body part down is another part, but he must also perform an act common to the game or survive the ground, which he does not do here. Because the ball comes loose, the recovery process was not achieved — this is illegal touching of the kick at the B-1, in the end zone. Since the ball did not touch the ground in the end zone, it remains live with a legal recovery and advance by B45. Fouls by the kicking team are enforced from the previous spot with the down repeated, or at the subsequent dead ball spot belonging to Team B, at Team B's option — here, the 5-yard penalty from the B-16 to the B-21.

question_text: 4/4 at the A-28. On a short punt, B6 yells "Peter" while slightly waving his arms below the level of his shoulders. The ball first lands at the 50-yard line, takes a high bounce, and lands again at the B-45 where B48 is late to vacate the area; as he tries to turn away towards his bench, he runs into a member of the kicking team and they collide. B48 falls to the ground after the contact, where the punt bounces and hits his leg. The punt is recovered by A87 at the B-46. During the kick, B8 holds A12 at the A-43. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. B48's touching of the ball is ruled forced touching, giving Team B the ball.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-43, Snap. Team A accepts the holding penalty instead of taking the result of the play.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-46, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal since the play was blown dead early.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-46, Snap. B48 has touched the football when the ball hits him in the leg at the B-45; however, the contact created when he runs into the Team A player while trying to vacate the area does not constitute forced touching. The recovery by Team A is legal due to the touching. Team A will decline the offensive holding foul and take the result of the play. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-40. Punter A10 muffs the snap and the ball rolls past him towards the sideline. A10 picks up the ball on a sprint just outside the numbers and, within a couple of yards of the sideline, turns to punt the ball while at the A-30. As he extends the ball out to kick it, he is hit by a chasing defender and the ball drops to the ground as he completely misses it while attempting to kick it. A22, who was trailing this action, recovers the grounded ball at the A-30 and punts the ball downfield; the ball hits the ground and rolls out of bounds at the B-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-40, Snap. A22's kick and subsequent out-of-bounds result stands as a legal punt for Team A.
choice_c: A, 4/10 @ A-40, Snap. The muffed snap voids the play entirely and the down is replayed.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-40, Snap. Team B takes over at the spot where the punt went out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-30, Snap. A10 muffs the initial snap, but then recovers it near the sideline and attempts to kick the ball; he misses the ball and is therefore guilty of fumbling it. With it being 4th down, only A10 can legally recover the football and advance it. When A22 recovers the football, the play is dead by rule. Team A is in possession, but did not make the line to gain, so Team B puts the ball in play at the spot of recovery. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 4/6 at the B-46. A7 punts and the ball rolls out of bounds at the B-4. B44 recognizes there are only 10 Team B players on the field, so he runs onto the field right after the snap and blocks A88 at the B-25. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap only, with no option for Team A to decline the foul.
choice_b: A, 4/1 @ B-46, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the dead ball spot at the B-4 instead of the previous spot.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap or A, 4/1 @ B-41, Snap.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap. The foul awards Team A a first down rather than a 5-yard gain from the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap or A, 4/1 @ B-41, Snap. B44 commits a live ball foul for entering the field of play while the ball is in play. This foul is enforced 5 yards from the previous spot at Team A's choice, or Team A can decline the foul and take the result of the play. Regardless of which option is selected, the clock starts on the snap following the legal kick.

question_text: Try at the B-3. QB A8 muffs the shotgun snap. B97 picks the ball up at the B-8 and is immediately wrapped up. B97 then pitches the ball backward to B23, who muffs it at the B-5. A70 then picks up the ball and heads for the pylon but is stripped at the B-1. The ball rolls into the end zone where A65 dives on the ball. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No points. The try-fumble rule prevents Team A from scoring since possession changed multiple times.
choice_b: 2 points for Team A, with the try replayed instead of resulting in a game score.
choice_c: No score, Team B's ball at the spot of A65's recovery, since Team B had prior possession during the down.
choice_d: 2 points for Team A. Kickoff at the A-35.
explanation: The correct ruling is: 2 points for Team A. Kickoff at the A-35. Given that there was a change of possession, the try-fumble rule is no longer in effect, and the result of the play is a 2-point score for Team A.

question_text: 3/14 at the B-34. QB A2 drops back to pass and throws a forward pass towards the end zone where B20 jumps from the B-2, controls the ball while airborne, and his first foot touches the ground just inside the B-1. His second foot then lands directly on the goal line. B20 remains upright and runs towards the back of the end zone where he steps on the end line prior to jumping and dunking the football over the crossbar. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B20's actions in the end zone result in a safety against Team B.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-1. Team B takes over at the exact spot of interception with no penalty enforcement.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-20, with no penalty assessed since the dunking celebration occurred after the ball was already dead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-10. When in question between a safety or a touchback, award Team B a touchback for their interception. The ball continues to be live while in Team B's possession but becomes dead once the Team B player steps out of bounds. The dead ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul for dunking the football is enforced from the succeeding spot, the B-20, half the distance to the B-10. If it's clear Team B intercepted in the field of play, an alternative is to rule momentum and award Team B the ball at the spot of interception, the B-1, then penalize half the distance from there. Either way, B20 has at least one unsportsmanlike conduct foul attributed to him.

question_text: 4/4 at the B-24. QB A11 throws a quick pass to WR A88, who runs a quick out route and is near the sideline when he receives the ball while airborne. While still in the air, he is tackled by B4 at the B-20 and carried two yards towards the sideline where he lands out of bounds with the ball still in his possession before falling to the ground. The clock reads 1:59 in the 4th quarter. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-18, Ready. The ball is spotted where the receiver landed out of bounds rather than where he was held.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The clock starts on the snap since the play ended out of bounds.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Ready. The incomplete pass ruling stands since the receiver went out of bounds with the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-20, Ready. This is a completed pass. The ball is dead at the yard line where the receiver was held, his forward progress stopped, and carried in a manner that prevented him from immediately returning to the ground. The game clock is on the ready.

question_text: 3/Goal at the B-9. B10 intercepts the pass 2 yards in the end zone. B10 returns the ball to the B-4 and fumbles. A20 bats the loose ball forward at the B-2 and the ball hits the pylon before it is recovered by another player. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-2. A20's recovery attempt is legal since the ball was already loose before he touched it.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-4, Snap. The ball is placed at the end of B10's return rather than resulting in a touchback.
choice_d: A, 4/1 @ B-2, Snap, with the penalty accepted and enforced from the end of the related run.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. A20 commits a foul for batting a loose ball forward in the field of play. The bat also creates new impetus. The batted ball hits the pylon and is therefore dead, with the result of the play being a touchback since Team A is now responsible for the ball being in Team B's end zone. The illegal bat penalty is declined by rule, since it would be enforced 10 yards from the end of the related run (B-4), which is not advantageous to Team B. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/6 at the A-35, 0:50 remaining in the 4th quarter, score A-14, B-16. A1 runs to the A-40 and is tackled there and immediately calls timeout with 0:45 left. As the timeout signal is given, a Team B player is identified with an injury. Officials correctly rule the injury has stopped the clock, and Team A elects to decline the 10-second runoff and start on the snap. During the injury timeout, replay changes the ruling on the field, as A1 had actually reached the line to gain at the A-41. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-41, Snap only, with no option for the game clock to start on the ready-for-play signal.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-40, Ready. The spot reverts to the original tackle spot rather than the corrected line-to-gain spot.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-41, Ready, with a mandatory 10-second runoff applied regardless of Team A's choice.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-41, Ready or Snap. With replay reviewing the play and overturning the ruling on the field to a first down at the A-41, the 10-second subtraction option is eliminated, the play clock is set to 40 seconds, and the game clock starts on the ready for play. Team A's head coach needs to be made aware the clock will start on the ready for play, or they can use their timeout instead. The injured Team B player must sit out for one play.

question_text: 4/8 at the A-45. Team A's punt is caught at the B-26 and returned to the A-3 where the ball is fumbled. Prone A88 recovers the fumble at the A-5. During the kick, B66 holds. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap, with the holding foul enforced under postscrimmage kick rules from the dead ball spot.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-5. Team B is awarded the ball at the spot of the fumble recovery instead of Team A retaining it.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-26, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the catch rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. Team B fouls during the kick, but this foul does not qualify for postscrimmage kick enforcement since Team B is not in legal possession at the end of the play and will not next snap the ball. Fouls that occur during the kick play that are not governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement are enforced from the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 3/14 at the A-16. A32 takes the handoff at the A-11-yard line. B96 twists and turns A32's facemask, causing him to fumble at that spot. The ball is scooped up at the A-11-yard line by A86, who runs to the 50 where he is tackled inbounds. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ 50, Snap, with the facemask penalty tacked onto the end of A86's run.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ 50, Ready. The facemask foul against B96 results in loss of possession for Team A instead.
choice_d: A, 1/25 @ A-1, Ready. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ 50, Ready. Team A will decline the facemask foul by B96 since it occurred behind the line of scrimmage. The end of the related run would be the A-11, so the facemask foul would be enforced from the previous spot. This foul cannot be tacked onto the end of the run since it occurred behind the line of scrimmage on a running play, not a passing play.

question_text: 3/10 at the A-4. QB A11 takes the shotgun snap, sits in the pocket for a couple of seconds, and is flushed to his right. After rolling out, A11 completes a pass to A82 at the A-12, who goes out of bounds there. At the snap, LT A78 is slow to get off the ball and is beaten outside by DE B5. A78 immediately grabs B5 inside the jersey by the front collar at the A-2 and subsequently pulls B5 to the ground in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team B. A78's holding foul that ends in the end zone results in a safety.
choice_b: A, 3/12 @ A-2, Snap. The clock starts on the snap rather than the ready-for-play signal.
choice_d: A, 3/12 @ A-2, Ready, with Team B having no option to decline the penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/12 @ A-2, Ready. A78 commits a foul for offensive holding, enforced half the distance from the previous spot. The holding foul starts in the field of play, and even though the action ultimately ends up in the end zone, the foul occurred at the A-2, not in the end zone, so there is no safety. Team B could also choose to decline the foul and take the result of the play, which would be 4th and 2 at the A-12.

question_text: 4/5 at the 50-yard line. QB A16 takes the snap and rolls to his left, towards the line of scrimmage. A16 releases the ball at the B-48, which is subsequently caught by A88 for a touchdown. The Line Judge has a flag down for an illegal forward pass. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A16's right toe was on the 50 at the release. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/5 @ 50, Snap. The illegal forward pass ruling on the field is upheld since A16's toe was on the line.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, but the try is skipped since the pass was nearly beyond the line of scrimmage.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-48, Snap. Replay awards a first down instead of confirming the touchdown.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A, Try @ B-3.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, Try @ B-3. Replay can review the passer being beyond the line of scrimmage. For the passer to be beyond the neutral zone, his entire body and the ball must be beyond it when the ball is released. Because his right toe is on the 50-yard line, A16's entire body is not beyond the line at release. Replay overturns the illegal forward pass ruling on the field, and Team A is awarded a touchdown with the succeeding try at the B-3.

question_text: 2/5 at the B-20. First possession series of the second overtime period. Team A sets up in a trips formation with all three receivers off the line and in the backfield. QB A12 is in shotgun formation with RB A24 to his left. A12 receives the snap, rolls to his right, and throws the ball towards A9. B1 intercepts the pass at the B-10 and returns it for a touchdown. During B1's run, B55 forcibly contacts A78 outside of his field of vision, knocking him to the ground at the A-40. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the contact by B55 was to the head and neck area of A78. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score for Team B. B, 1/10 @ B-40.
choice_b: Touchdown stands for Team B, with B55 disqualified for targeting after the score is confirmed.
choice_c: No score for Team B, B, 1/10 @ A-40, with the foul enforced from the previous spot rather than the succeeding spot.
choice_d: No score for Team B, A, 1/10 @ B-40. The targeting foul results in loss of possession for Team B instead.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score for Team B. B, 1/10 @ B-40. The illegal forward pass by Team A and the blindside block by B55 are both declined by rule. However, the targeting foul that is created by replay is enforced from the succeeding spot, resulting in a first down for Team B at the B-40. B55 is disqualified by rule.

question_text: 3/5 at the B-35. A12 throws a forward pass to A88. A88 catches the pass at the B-25 and immediately goes out of bounds there. A88 was lined up as a tight end. A1 was lined up as a wide receiver on the same side of the formation and also on the line of scrimmage. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-25. The catch stands as a legal completion since A88 is a tight end.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-25. The illegal touching foul results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_d: A, 4/10 @ B-40, with only one of the two fouls enforced since they occur on the same play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/10 @ B-40. A88 commits two fouls by going downfield: he is covered up by A1 and goes downfield more than three yards, which is a foul for being ineligible downfield on a pass. A88 is also guilty of illegal touching, as he is an originally ineligible player who is the first to touch a legal forward pass. The illegal touching penalty is 5 yards from the previous spot and a loss of down.

question_text: A kickoff at the A-35. Team A just tied the game and executes a surprise onside kick, as they have the momentum. B1, who is standing at the 50, gives a valid fair catch signal and moves to catch the football. A2 moves directly in front of him at the 50, catches the football, and runs to the B-40 where he is tackled. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-40. A2's recovery of the onside kick stands since B1 never actually caught the ball.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ 50. The interference penalty is enforced from the spot of the kick rather than the spot of the foul.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-35, with A2 also assessed a personal foul penalty for the same act.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-35. B1, who gave a valid fair catch signal, must be given the unimpeded opportunity to catch the kick. The foul for interference is enforced from the spot of the foul.

question_text: 4/7 at the A-33. Team A's punt is touched by A22 at the B-30. B33 then picks up the ball at the B-25 and runs to the B-40, where he fumbles. A44 picks up the fumble and runs for a touchdown. During B33's run, B66 was flagged for holding at the B-35. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands since the illegal touching provision is unrelated to Team B's holding foul.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-30, with the holding foul enforced from the spot of illegal touching instead of the spot of the foul.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-30. Team A retains the ball at the spot of illegal touching regardless of Team B's foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-25. Team A's touchdown does not count. A22 illegally touches the football at the B-30, and B66 commits a foul for holding during B33's run. By rule, 10-yard fouls by the non-scoring team are declined unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick during the down. The illegal touching provision is cancelled with an accepted penalty. If Team A declines the holding foul, Team B would get the ball at the B-30 instead. Since the holding foul by B66 is enforced from the B-35, the result is Team B's ball at the B-25, clock on the snap.

question_text: 3/10 at the A-43. A15 is in motion at the snap and is flagged for illegal motion. QB A10 scrambles to his right and throws a low pass toward A1. B1 makes a diving attempt and intercepts the ball. Team B declines the foul for illegal motion. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball hit the ground prior to B1 intercepting the pass. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-43, Snap. The interception stands since replay cannot review this change-of-possession ruling.
choice_c: A, 4/10 @ A-43, Snap only, with no option to instead enforce the illegal motion foul.
choice_d: A, 3/15 @ A-38, Snap only, with the illegal motion foul mandatory rather than optional to decline.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/15 @ A-38 or A, 4/10 @ A-43. Replay can review any pass ruled complete, incomplete, or intercepted anywhere in the field of play. After review, the play is reversed to an incomplete pass. The foul for illegal motion can now either be enforced 5 yards from the previous spot with the down replayed, or it may be declined, bringing up 4th down.

question_text: 4/10 at the B-18. Team A's field goal attempt is blocked behind the line of scrimmage. The blocked kick floats downfield and hits the ground at the B-10, where it is recovered by B28 and advanced to the B-20. A88 holds B1 on the edge to prevent him from blocking the kick. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-20, with Team A retaining the ball since B28's recovery is negated by A88's holding foul.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-10. The holding foul is tacked onto the end of B28's return.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-20, with A88 disqualified for the holding foul on a kick attempt.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-20. The hold by A88 is declined by Team B, as it is not able to be tacked onto the end of the run since it occurred during a field goal attempt.

question_text: 4/17 at the B-43. A5's punt bounces untouched at the B-3 and breaks the plane of Team B's goal line, where A85 bats the ball back into the field of play. B44 muffs the ball at the B-2 and A68 recovers the ball at the B-6. The ball did not touch the ground in Team B's end zone. During the kick, B55 committed a holding foul at the B-26. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/Goal @ B-6, Snap. Team A takes the ball at the spot of A68's recovery rather than accepting the holding foul.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. Team B automatically gets the ball at the illegal touching spot regardless of Team A's choice.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-33, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-33, Snap. A85 illegally touches the ball when he bats the loose ball that is over Team B's goal line; the bat is not itself a foul, and the spot of the violation becomes the B-20, which is also the postscrimmage kick spot for potential enforcement. Team A is in legal possession at the end of the down and would likely decline that in favor of taking the ball at the recovery spot — however, Team A will instead elect to accept the holding foul by B55, enforced from the previous spot with an automatic first down, since Team B is not next to snap the ball and postscrimmage kick enforcement isn't an option. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/5 at the B-20. First possession series of the first overtime period, score tied 24-24. QB A1 throws a pass that is intercepted by B22. During B22's return for a touchdown, the Side Judge runs into the Team B head coach, who is on the field, and drops a flag for sideline interference. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The touchdown is nullified due to the Side Judge's interference with the Team B head coach, and the down is replayed.
choice_b: Team B wins, but the score is enforced with a half-the-distance penalty applied first.
choice_c: The game continues, since sideline interference by an official voids the entire play.
choice_d: Team B wins, game is over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B wins, game is over. Sideline interference by an official is a live ball foul enforced as a dead ball foul. By rule, the score will count, and the game is over, since Team B scored during the first possession series of overtime.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-20, 0:20 remaining in the second half. Team A is behind by 5 points and has one timeout remaining. A1 throws a forward pass to A2, who makes the catch on his knees at the B-10.5. The on-field officials award a first down and stop the clock with 0:14 remaining. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball was caught by A2 at the B-11. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 2/1 @ B-11, Snap only, with no option for a 10-second subtraction regardless of Team A's timeout status.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-11. The reversal results in a first down instead of a 2nd and 1.
choice_d: A, 2/1 @ B-11, :14, Ready, with the clock never able to start on the snap in this situation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/1 @ B-11. Team A has one timeout remaining and may opt to use it to avoid the 10-second subtraction. This play is reviewable since the line to gain is involved and a reversal by replay would cause the clock to go from stopped to running. If Team A opts not to use their final timeout, the reversal results in a 10-second subtraction and the clock is set to 0:04, starting on the ready. If Team A uses their timeout, the clock remains at 0:14 and starts on the snap.

question_text: 4/3 at the B-35. 5:00 left in the fourth quarter, game clock running, score tied. Team A sends in three substitutes including their place kicker, A99. A10 sets up in a shotgun formation at the B-43, with A99 directly to his right. Immediately after the snap, NG B99 charges directly at the snapper and forcibly pushes him backwards. A10 takes the snap, runs to his right, and throws a forward pass to A88 that falls incomplete. A99 holds B1 to prevent him from getting to A10. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-30, Snap. B99's contact with the snapper is a personal foul since all snappers are protected regardless of formation.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-35, Snap, with A99's holding foul also enforced alongside the turnover on downs.
choice_d: A, 4/8 @ B-35, Ready. The incomplete pass is negated due to Team A's illegal formation.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-35, Snap. Team A is not in a legal scrimmage kick formation, since A10 is 8 yards behind the snapper, there isn't a holder, and it's not obvious a kick will be made. Because it is not a legal scrimmage kick formation, B99's action against the snapper is not a foul, since the snapper does not have the usual personal-foul protection. The holding foul by A99 will be declined by rule, and the clock starts on the snap following the turnover on downs.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-20. Team A punts the ball. During the kick, A1 goes out of bounds on his own (no contact) at the A-30 and returns inbounds at the A-35. B1 catches the punt at the B-30 and returns it to the 50 where he is tackled by A1 and fumbles. A2 muffs the loose ball, and it goes out of bounds at the A-45. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. A1's return to the field of play negates the fumble ruling entirely.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ 50, Snap. Team B takes over at the spot of the fumble recovery attempt instead.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-45, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-45, Snap. The result of the play is a forward fumble out of bounds, so the ball is returned to the spot of the fumble. A1 commits a foul for returning inbounds after going out on his own during a scrimmage kick play. This foul is enforced 5 yards from the subsequent dead ball spot where the ball belongs to Team B, or it could be enforced from the previous spot, though that would be unlikely. The foul can't simply be declined, since otherwise Team B would next snap the ball at the 50 rather than where the ball is returned.

question_text: 1/15 on the B-30. QB A1 throws a pass towards A86, who is standing at the B-15. A86 makes a sliding catch at the B-15 with 10:00 in the first quarter showing on the game clock. The pass is ruled complete and the clock continues to run for 5 additional seconds. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball hit the ground before A86 gained firm control. What is the ruling, including clock status?
choice_a: A, 2/15 @ B-30, with the game clock rolled back 5 seconds to when the ball was ruled caught.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-30. The overturned incomplete pass results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_c: A, 2/15 @ B-30, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal instead of the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15 @ B-30. There is no adjustment to the game clock. The clock starts on the snap following the reversal by replay to an incomplete pass.

question_text: Try at the B-3. A11's pass is intercepted by B3 at Team B's goal line and returned to Team A's end zone for an apparent score. During the return, B44 is flagged for unsportsmanlike conduct at the A-20. At the snap, Team A was flagged for an illegal formation. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, Free Kick @ 50, No score for Team B.
choice_b: Touchdown stands for Team B, with the unsportsmanlike conduct foul enforced on the extra point attempt instead.
choice_c: A, Free Kick @ 50, with Team A's illegal formation foul also enforced on the kickoff alongside B44's foul.
choice_d: No score for Team B, A, Free Kick @ 45, enforced from a different spot than the kickoff line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, Free Kick @ 50, No score for Team B. Team A commits a foul for illegal formation, and B44 commits a foul for unsportsmanlike conduct. That foul occurred after the change of possession, so the score by Team B does not count. The illegal formation by Team A is declined, and the unsportsmanlike conduct foul is enforced on the succeeding kickoff.

question_text: 4/7 at the A-33. Team A's punt is first touched by A22 at the B-30. B33 then picks up the ball at the B-25 and runs to the B-40, where he fumbles. A44 picks up the fumble and runs for a touchdown. B1, in an attempt to make the tackle, blocks A1 below the waist. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score for Team A, B, 1/10 @ B-30, due to A22's illegal touching of the punt earlier in the down.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, enforce BBW on Try or kickoff.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A, with the below-the-waist block foul enforced only from the previous spot rather than on the Try or kickoff.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A, with both the illegal touching and blocking-below-the-waist fouls enforced together.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, enforce BBW on Try or kickoff. A22 commits an illegal touching violation at the B-30; however, Team A will choose to enforce the blocking-below-the-waist foul by B1 instead, which removes the illegal touching provisions, and the touchdown counts. The below-the-waist foul is enforced on either the Try or the succeeding kickoff.

question_text: 1/10 at the 50. QB A1, under a heavy rush, scrambles outside of the tackle box and turns upfield. At the B-42, A1 reaches out attempting to get the first down when he is contacted in the head by B1 and ruled down at the B-41. A1's helmet comes off as a direct result of the contact by B1. The Head Linesman and Referee both have flags down for targeting with the crown of the helmet on B1. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the contact by B1 was not targeting and there was no other indicator of targeting. What is the ruling, including player status?
choice_a: A, 2/1 @ B-41, with A1 allowed to remain in the game since the helmet coming off was ruled unintentional.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-41. The targeting penalty stands despite replay review since a flag was thrown on the field.
choice_d: A, 2/1 @ B-41, with B1 disqualified despite replay overturning the targeting call.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/1 @ B-41. B1's contact with the crown of the helmet was reviewed and overturned by replay. Following the review, QB A1 must sit out for one play since his helmet came off, but not as the direct result of a foul, unless Team A chooses to use a team timeout.

question_text: 4/7 at the A-40. Punter A15 kicks the ball towards B1. B1 signals for a fair catch, misjudges the punt, and lets it go over his head. B1 then proceeds to block A1 at the B-10, preventing A1 from downing the ball before it goes into the end zone. The ball rolls untouched into the end zone where it is blown dead. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The touchback stands since the ball rolled untouched into the end zone.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap. The foul is enforced from the postscrimmage kick spot rather than the closer spot of the foul.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. The penalty is enforced from the previous spot instead of half the distance.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-5, Snap. The block by B1 is illegal, since he gave a fair catch signal. This foul is governed by postscrimmage kick enforcement. The postscrimmage kick spot is the B-20 following the hanging touchback; however, the foul occurs at the B-10, which is behind the B-20, and is therefore enforced from that spot, half the distance. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 3/14 at the A-16. A32 takes the handoff at the A-11-yard line. B96 twists and turns A32's facemask, causing him to fumble at that spot. The ball is scooped up at the A-11-yard line by A86, who runs to the A-30 where he is tackled inbounds. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-30. Team A declines the facemask foul and keeps the ball at the spot of A86's tackle.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-30. The facemask foul results in loss of possession for Team A instead.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-30, with the foul enforced from the dead ball spot rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-31. The foul by Team B and the end of the related run both occurred behind the line of scrimmage, so the facemask foul is enforced from the previous spot. Team A will want to enforce this foul, since the ball is dead following A86's run at the A-30, and accepting the penalty gives Team A a 1-yard advantage.

question_text: 3/12 on the A-10. A1 drops back to pass and, under heavy duress, steps up into the pocket and throws the ball away. The Referee drops a flag for intentional grounding in the end zone. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the pass was thrown from the A-2. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team B, since the intentional grounding occurred while A1 was in the end zone.
choice_c: A, 4/20 @ A-2, with the safety upheld in addition to the loss-of-down penalty.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-2. The intentional grounding results in loss of possession instead of loss of down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/20 @ A-2. Replay can review this play since a safety resulted from the ruling of intentional grounding. It is the position of the ball in relation to the goal line when the pass is released that determines whether this is a safety. Since the pass was thrown from the A-2, not from within the end zone, the foul for intentional grounding is loss of down at the spot of the foul rather than a safety.

question_text: 3/5 at the B-27. QB A13 throws a pass towards A80, who catches the pass in the end zone. B24 grabs and restricts A80 prior to the pass arriving. B11 has a free rush and launches and hits A13 in the head and neck area. After the play is over, A13 taunts the opposing bench. After review, replay confirms the touchdown and the targeting action by B11. What is the ruling?
choice_a: No score for Team A, since B24's defensive pass interference in the end zone negates the touchdown.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, but B11 is not disqualified since the game had already resulted in a score.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A, with A13's unsportsmanlike conduct foul offsetting B11's targeting foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A. The live-ball roughing-the-passer-with-targeting foul can be enforced on the Try or the kickoff at Team A's option. The defensive pass interference by B24 will be declined, since it can't be carried over to the Try or kickoff. Replay confirms the targeting by B11, and B11 is disqualified. The dead ball unsportsmanlike conduct foul by A13 can also be enforced on the Try or kickoff, at Team B's option. A live ball foul cannot offset a dead ball foul, and both fouls must be enforced in order of occurrence.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-45. A1's punt bounces at the B-2 and crosses the goal line. A10, standing at the B-1 yard line, reaches across the plane of the goal line and bats the ball back into the field of play, and the ball rolls out of bounds at the B-6. During the kick, A50 commits a personal foul. Team B wants the ball. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-6. Team A retains possession at the spot where the ball went out of bounds.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap, with no option for Team B to instead accept the personal foul penalty.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-11, Snap. The personal foul is enforced from the previous spot with the down repeated only.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-21, Snap. The ball is batted by Team A in Team B's end zone; this is a special case of illegal touching and is not itself a foul. The spot of the violation becomes the B-20. Team B can either accept the personal foul penalty, enforced from either the previous spot with the down repeated or from the B-6, or, if Team B declines the personal foul, the illegal touching gives the ball to Team B at the B-20. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 3/9 at the B-19. A17's pass is intercepted in the end zone by B33. While attempting to return the ball, B33 is hit while still in the end zone and fumbles the ball into the field of play. With the ball rolling loose at the B-10, A55 kicks it forward where it contacts the goal line pylon. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-10. A55's kick is a legal continuation of the return, and Team A retains possession.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-10, Snap. The touchback is enforced from B33's fumble spot rather than the basic B-20 spot.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-30, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-30, Snap. The result of the play is a touchback, so the basic spot for enforcement of the 10-yard penalty for illegally kicking the ball is the B-20. The touchback results because of the new impetus given by A55 kicking the ball.

question_text: 3/8 at the A-32. RB A1 takes the handoff and runs outside toward the right sideline and is tackled out of bounds at the A-38. During the run, RT A78 holds B99 at the A-34. After the play, A88 forcibly blocks B12 from the side, in the middle of the field, at the A-40. The Field Judge flags A88 for a late hit away from the play. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/34 @ A-19, with both fouls automatically enforced together rather than Team B choosing between options.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-38. Team B is awarded the ball due to the combination of fouls by Team A.
choice_d: A, 4/17 @ A-23, with the late hit foul declined and only the holding foul enforced.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/17 @ A-23. Team B has options here. The best option is for Team B to decline the live ball holding foul by A78 and accept the dead ball unnecessary roughness/blindside block foul, since the down will count and bring up 4th down. Team B could also opt to accept both fouls, enforcing the holding foul from the spot of the foul and then the dead ball foul half the distance to the goal, repeating 3rd down.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-18. Team A is behind in the score 24-27 and is out of timeouts. On the previous play, Team A completed a pass for a first down and was tackled inbounds with the game clock at 0:18. After the Referee correctly whistles and then winds the clock, A7 muffs the snap. He immediately picks the ball up off the ground at the B-19 and spikes the ball. The game clock shows 0:15. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 2/11 @ B-19, :15. There is no 10-second subtraction since Team A intended the spike to stop the clock.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-19, :15. The intentional grounding results in a turnover on downs rather than loss of down.
choice_d: A, 2/11 @ B-19, :05, Snap only, with no option for the clock to start on the ready for play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/11 @ B-19, :05. The attempted spike to stop the clock was not legal, since A7 muffed the snap and the ball hit the ground, resulting in intentional grounding. The foul is a spot foul with a loss of down, and it also carries the potential for a 10-second subtraction. Team B will accept the 10-second subtraction since Team A is out of timeouts, and the clock is set to 0:05, starting on the ready for play.

question_text: 3/10 at the A-40. QB A7 throws a pass to A85 at the B-35. The Umpire rules that the ball was touched at the line of scrimmage by a Team B player and gives a tip signal. B24 makes early contact on A85 and the pass falls incomplete, without a flag being thrown. The Back Judge reports to the Referee that he didn't have a flag down because he saw the tip signal by the Umpire. The Referee announces there is no foul for defensive pass interference since the ball was tipped. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball was not tipped. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/10 @ A-40, Ready. The no-foul ruling on the field stands since replay cannot review a tip signal.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-35. B24's early contact results in loss of possession for Team A instead of a DPI penalty.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. The clock starts on the snap rather than remaining on the ready-for-play signal.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-45. The tip signal, or the Referee's announcement, allows the Replay Official to stop the game and review whether the ball was tipped. With video evidence that the ball was not tipped, the ruling is overturned, and a foul for defensive pass interference is created through replay.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-40. On a punt play, flyer A88 immediately runs out of bounds to avoid being blocked and sprints down the field in the "white" area to prevent his opponent from legally blocking him. The kicked ball lands at the B-5 and bounces toward Team B's goal line near the sideline. A88 leaps from out of bounds and, while airborne, bats the loose ball at the B-2, causing it to strike B99 at the B-1. A88 comes to the ground inbounds while the ball is loose, and teammate A90 recovers the loose ball at the B-1 and advances it into Team B's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-1. A90's recovery of the loose ball counts as a legal advance for Team A.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-1, with A88 flagged for an illegal bat since the ball was batted forward.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-2, with A88 also flagged for illegally returning inbounds during the down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-2. The loose ball is dead immediately when touched by A88, since A88 is an out of bounds player, and contact with an out-of-bounds player causes a loose ball to become dead. A kick that goes out of bounds belongs to the receiving team at that spot. Running out of bounds is not itself a foul; it only becomes a foul if a player returns inbounds during the down, which A88 never does, since the down ends the moment he touches the ball while still airborne. Although the bat is forward and would otherwise be a foul, the bat never happens by rule since the touch happens first, making any subsequent action against the ball irrelevant.

question_text: 2/16 at the B-39. Running back A25 runs around the right side. At the B-26, A25 dives towards the sideline and crosses it at the B-22. During the run, B99 grabs and restricts right tackle A77 at the B-37, which allows LB B56 to shoot the gap. After the play, the Team B head coach comes out to the numbers to protest the spot of the ball and throws his headset to the ground. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A25 stepped out of bounds at the B-32. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ B-11, with the coach's unsportsmanlike conduct foul declined since it occurred after the play.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-22. The out-of-bounds ruling stands since replay only reviews scoring plays near the sideline.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ B-16, with defensive holding enforced from the spot of the foul rather than the end of the run.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-11. This play is reviewable, since it involves the sideline and whether the player was in or out. Defensive holding is enforced from the end of the run, the B-32, since the related run ends beyond the neutral zone. Unsportsmanlike conduct by the Team B coach is enforced half the distance from the B-22 and counts toward one of his two allowed. The clock starts on the ready following enforcement of both Team B fouls.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-17. A12 takes the shotgun snap and throws a pass toward A1 at the Team B goal line. The pass falls incomplete as B8 plays through the back of A1 prior to the ball arriving. The Field Judge has a flag down for defensive pass interference, and the Referee has a flag down for roughing the passer with targeting by B96. Replay has indisputable video evidence to confirm the targeting by B96. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/Goal @ B-2, with B96 not disqualified since the yardage penalty for his foul was declined.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-48. Team A accepts the roughing-the-passer-with-targeting penalty instead of DPI.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-17. The defensive pass interference is declined in favor of an incomplete pass ruling.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/Goal @ B-2, DQ B96. Team A will decline the yardage penalty for roughing the passer with targeting, since it would be enforced half the distance to the goal from the previous spot, and instead accept the penalty for defensive pass interference, enforced from the B-17 to the B-2. B96 is disqualified for roughing the passer with targeting even though the yardage penalty for that foul was declined.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-35. A80 runs a go route up the sideline. As the ball is in flight, A80 steps out of bounds at the B-5 and jumps from out of bounds for the ball. B7 tips the ball in flight, which is caught by A80, who lands inbounds at the B-2 and dives for the pylon. He touches the pylon with his left hand as the ball crosses out of bounds over the half-yard line. The Side Judge rules touchdown. Replay confirms that A80 did step out at the B-5. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands since B7 touched the ball first, making A80 eligible again.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-2. A80's touch while out of bounds is ruled illegal touching, giving Team B the ball.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-2. The catch counts since A80 landed inbounds with control before reaching the pylon.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10 @ B-35. A80 goes out of bounds on his own and therefore becomes an ineligible player. A80 does not re-establish himself inbounds and is still airborne when he touches the ball. Because he did not re-establish himself inbounds, once he touches the ball the pass is ruled incomplete, not illegal touching. B7 does touch the ball; however, because A80 is still considered an out-of-bounds player, the ball is also considered out of bounds once he touches it. The clock starts on the snap following the incomplete pass.

question_text: 4/7 at the B-27. A5's field goal attempt is partially blocked at the line of scrimmage and rolls downfield. B33 recovers the rolling kick at the B-5 and returns it to the A-20 where he is brought down. At the snap, 5 Team A players were lined up in the backfield. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 4/12 @ B-32, Snap. Team B elects to accept the illegal formation penalty and repeat the down for Team A.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-5, Snap. The illegal formation foul is tacked onto the end of B33's return.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-20, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-20, Snap. Team B may advance a scrimmage kick, but tack-ons are not a penalty enforcement option on a field goal attempt. Team B has the option of declining the foul for illegal formation or enforcing the 5-yard foul from the previous spot; Team B will opt to decline the foul and take the result of the play. The clock starts on the snap following the change of possession.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-14. QB A9 throws a pass into the end zone which is intercepted by B18. B18 runs around in the end zone trying to find a way out, and B26 blocks A45 below the waist at the B-2 yard line before B18 is tackled in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-4. The blocking-below-the-waist foul is enforced from the spot of the foul rather than half the distance.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-2. Team A retains possession due to B26's foul during the return.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-1, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-1, Snap. The result of the play is a touchback, and the blocking-below-the-waist foul occurs behind the basic spot, so it is enforced half the distance from the B-2. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: 2/8 at the A-4. A11 receives the snap and is standing in the end zone when he is hit as he is throwing a forward pass. The pass floats upward and A70 tries to grab the ball to prevent B99 from intercepting it. A70 muffs the forward pass in the end zone and it falls incomplete at the A-1. A88 was right behind A70 when the pass was touched. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team B, since the muffed pass occurred entirely within Team A's own end zone.
choice_b: A, 3/10 @ A-2, with the foul ruled intentional grounding instead of illegal touching.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-2. The illegal touching results in loss of possession for Team A instead of loss of down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10 @ A-2. Illegal touching by A70 carries a loss of down, so the foul is enforced half the distance to the goal with a loss of down. With A88 right behind A70, there is an eligible receiver in the area of the pass, so this is not intentional grounding.

question_text: 1/10 at the B-20. A17 throws a forward pass toward A1. B22 jumps in front of A1 and intercepts the pass at the B-5. During the return, B22 takes the ball back into his own end zone to avoid being tackled. While in the end zone, A1 grabs and twists B22's facemask, causing B22 to fumble the ball. The ball is recovered by B22 while in the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, 2 points for Team A. B22's momentum into the end zone with a resulting fumble creates a safety.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-5. Team A is awarded the ball at the spot of the facemask foul.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-5, Snap. The facemask foul is enforced from the spot of the foul rather than the goal line.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-15, Snap. B22 takes the ball into the end zone on his own, creating a potential safety situation; however, A1 grabs B22's facemask, causing him to fumble, with the ball being recovered in the end zone by B22. The facemask foul is enforced from the goal line.

question_text: 4/Goal at the B-5. QB A11 muffs the snap at the B-6 and RB A23 picks up the loose ball at the B-10 and runs it in for a touchdown. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A11 clearly possessed the ball prior to it coming loose. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A stands since replay cannot review possession of a muffed snap.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-10, Snap. The 4th down fumble rule allows Team A to keep the ball at the recovery spot.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-6, Snap. The ball is placed at the original muff spot instead of A23's recovery spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-10, Snap. No score for Team A, as this play is reviewable, and after review the 4th down fumble rule applies since A11 clearly possessed the snap before it came loose. The ball is brought back to the B-10, where it is first down for Team B. The clock starts on the snap following the change of possession.

question_text: 2/10 at the A-25. A22 carries to the A-35 where B66 grasps his facemask, causing A22 to fumble and then lose his helmet. The ball is recovered by A88 at the A-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 @ 50, with A22 required to sit out one play since his helmet came off during the down.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-40. The facemask foul results in loss of possession for Team A instead.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-45. The penalty is enforced from the spot of the foul rather than the end of the run.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ 50. The facemask foul by B66 is enforced from the end of the related run, which is the A-35. A22 does not have to sit out one play, since his helmet came off due to the foul by B66.

question_text: 2/5 at the A-25. Team A leads 24-21, late in the fourth period. A44 runs for 10 yards before being tackled in the field of play. During the run, A55 held at the A-22. The game clock reads 1:30 at the end of the play. What is the ruling, including clock status?
choice_a: A, 2/15 @ A-15, Ready only, with no clock-start option available to Team B.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-22. The holding foul results in loss of possession for Team A instead.
choice_d: A, 2/15 @ A-15, Snap, at Team A's option rather than Team B's, since Team A committed the foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15 @ A-15. A55 holds behind the line of scrimmage. That foul is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot. The clock starts on the snap at the option of Team B, since they are behind in the score and Team A committed a foul.

question_text: 4/9 at the A-25. A1 punts the ball towards the sideline. The ball hits near the sideline at the B-27 and bounces back into the field of play, where B25 picks it up and returns it across Team A's goal line. The on-field officials rule touchdown. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball hit the sideline at the B-27. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B stands, since sideline contact on a kick is a judgment call not subject to review.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ B-27, Snap. The overturned ruling awards the ball to Team A instead of Team B.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-27, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal rather than the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-27, Snap. This play is reviewable as to whether or not the ball hit the sideline. The touchdown ruling on the field is overturned. The clock starts on the snap following the change of possession.

question_text: 3/8 at the A-35. QB A1, under a heavy rush, scrambles up the middle and to his left. As he is scrambling, he and the ball go completely across the A-37 and then he retreats backwards to the A-35, where he throws a forward pass to A2 at the A-45. A2 stepped out of bounds on his own, re-established inbounds prior to the pass being thrown, and then completed the catch at the A-45. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-45. The pass stands as a legal completion since A1 retreated behind the line before throwing.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-45. A2's illegal touching foul is enforced in place of the illegal forward pass.
choice_d: A, 4/13 @ A-30, with both the illegal forward pass and illegal touching fouls enforced together.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/13 @ A-30. A1 commits a foul for an illegal forward pass, since he and the ball went completely beyond the line of scrimmage and then retreated behind it before he threw a forward pass. This foul is enforced 5 yards from the spot of the foul and is a loss of down. A2 potentially commits a foul for illegal touching since he went out of bounds on his own and was the first to touch the pass after re-establishing inbounds; however, because the forward pass was already illegal, A2 has not separately committed illegal touching.

question_text: 2/6 at the B-34. WR A81 is lined up to the right side of the formation. At the snap, QB A1 pitches the ball to the right on a sweep play to back A24. B36, in pursuit, loses sight of the receiver. A81 moves back toward the original position of the ball and launches, leading with his forearm from the side into the head and neck area of B36 at the B-32. A24 runs off the block for a 14-yard gain. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 2/19 @ B-47, Snap, with A81 not disqualified since the targeting portion of the combination foul was declined.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ B-32. The blindside block with targeting foul results in loss of possession for Team A.
choice_d: A, 2/33 @ B-33, Ready, DQ A81. The foul is enforced from the previous spot instead of the spot of the foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/19 @ B-47, Ready, :25, DQ A81. A81 commits a combination foul: a blindside block with targeting. The foul is enforced from the spot of the foul, the B-32, since this is not a previous-spot enforcement (the end of the related run ends beyond the neutral zone). This foul is reviewable by replay; since replay confirms the targeting call, A81 is disqualified. The clock starts on the ready for play.

question_text: A kickoff at the A-35. When the ball is kicked, A88 is offside. The receiver muffs the kick at the B-3, recovers the ball, and returns it to the B-17. After the runner is tackled, A17 piles on and is flagged for unnecessary roughness/late hit. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-17, Snap. Only the offside foul is enforced, with the piling-on foul declined entirely.
choice_b: A, Free Kick @ A-15 only, with no option to instead tack both fouls onto the end of the run.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ B-42, Snap. Both fouls are enforced together from the B-17 for a combined 20-yard penalty.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ B-37, Snap. A88 is offside on the kick; this foul can be tacked onto the end of the run, the B-17. After the play was over, A17 commits an unnecessary roughness foul by piling on. This foul is then enforced from the B-22, resulting in a first down for Team B at the B-37. Team B also has the option to instead enforce both fouls from the previous spot and replay the down, resulting in a kickoff for Team A from the A-15.

question_text: A kickoff at the A-35. 0:09 left in the game, Team A leads 14-7. B1 fields the ball at the B-15 and runs to the A-31 where he is contacted by A1. In a desperation attempt, B1 pitches the ball to B2, who is standing at the A-30. B2 muffs the ball and it hits the ground with 0:02 on the clock. There is a scramble for the loose ball, and B2 recovers the ball at the A-35 while grounded as time expires. After the play, A1 spears B2 with the crown of his helmet. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-21, :00. The clock expires entirely since the ball hit the ground before the recovery.
choice_b: A, 1/10 @ A-30. The illegal forward pass results in loss of possession for Team B instead.
choice_c: B, 1/10 @ A-26, :02. The spearing foul is enforced 5 yards instead of 15 yards.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 @ A-21, :02. B1's pitch forward is a foul for an illegal forward pass. This foul does not carry a 10-second subtraction due to the change of team possession. The foul is enforced 5 yards from the A-31. A1's spearing foul is then enforced 15 yards, and A1 is disqualified following replay confirmation of the crown-of-the-helmet hit on B2.
