question_text: 4/7 at the A-45. A12's punt is blocked by B89. The ball is rolling at the A-43 when B78 bats the ball to the B-49, where it is recovered by B99. B99 advances the ball to the A-10 where he is forced out of bounds. Team A had 5 players in the backfield at the snap. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 4/12 @ A-33. Team A's illegal formation is declined and the muffed recovery stands for Team A.
choice_c: B, 1/Goal @ A-5, with B78's bat also ruled an illegal forward bat since it moved the ball backward illegally.
choice_d: B, 1/10 @ A-10, Snap, with the formation foul enforced only from the previous spot as the sole option.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/Goal @ A-5. Team A commits a foul for illegal formation. That foul can either be enforced at the previous spot or the subsequent dead ball spot where Team B will next put the ball in play, since the scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone. The bat by B78 is legal, since it is backwards; while it does create new impetus, it does not change the status of the ball, and it remains a kick.

question_text: 1/10 at the A-20. B1 intercepts the pass at the A-35. During the return, B4 blocks A1 below the waist at the A-25. B1 is hit and fumbles the ball, which is recovered by A10, who advances the ball to the B-40 where he is tackled. During A10's run, A88 illegally blindside-blocked B15 at the A-45. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ A-25. Team B retains the ball since they had prior possession from the interception.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ A-30, with both fouls enforced together for a combined penalty from different spots.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-20. The blindside block foul is enforced from the previous spot instead of the spot of the foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ A-30. There are fouls by both teams on this play. B4 commits a foul for blocking below the waist following the interception; this foul will be declined. A88 commits a foul for an illegal blindside block, enforced from the A-45. Because Team A has clean hands, they can elect to decline the below-the-waist foul by B4 and enforce the penalty for their own blindside block foul.

question_text: 3/10 at the B-20. 0:25 left in the second quarter, no timeouts for either team. Shotgun QB A1 takes the snap and looks to find an open receiver. Under heavy pressure, he rolls out to his left. As he is scrambling, A78 hits B99 from the side with forcible contact in the shoulder at the B-30. A1 continues rolling to his left towards the sideline. B1 tackles A1 at the B-25 with 0:18 left on the clock. Just prior to the tackle, A1 throws the ball out of bounds and it lands at the B-22. The on-field officials rule A1 down prior to the pass being thrown. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A1 was not down prior to the pass being thrown. What are all possible answers, including clock status?
choice_a: A, 4/15 @ B-25, :18, Ready, with intentional grounding also enforced since replay shows A1 was not down.
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ B-25. The out-of-bounds pass is ruled a turnover since A1 was not actually down.
choice_d: A, 3/25 @ B-35, :08, Ready, with a 10-second subtraction applied to the blindside block foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/25 @ B-35, :18, Ready. There is only one potential foul on this play: a blindside block by A78. That foul, if accepted, is enforced from the previous spot and does not qualify for a 10-second subtraction, resulting in 3rd and 25 at the B-35, clock remaining at 0:18, ready. Since the on-field officials ruled A1 down prior to throwing the pass away, this ruling is not reviewable — even with indisputable video evidence, replay cannot get involved in a runner-down judgment call, so there is no foul for intentional grounding. Team B could also opt to take the result of the play, resulting in 4th and 15 at the B-25, clock remaining at 0:18, ready.

question_text: 4/17 at the A-45. A90's punt bounces untouched at the B-3 and breaks the plane of Team B's goal line, where A2 bats the ball back into the field of play. B44 attempts to grab the football but muffs the ball at the B-2. A68 is in perfect position and recovers the ball at the B-6. During the kick, B55 committed a holding foul at the B-25. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 @ B-20, Snap only, with Team A having no option to instead accept the holding foul.
choice_b: A, 1/Goal @ B-6, Snap. Team A takes the ball at A68's recovery spot instead of accepting the holding penalty.
choice_c: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Ready. The clock starts on the ready-for-play signal instead of the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 @ B-45, Snap. A68 is in legal possession of the ball at the end of the down. Postscrimmage kick rules do not apply, since Team B will not next put the ball in play. B55 commits a holding foul during the kick; since that foul does not meet the postscrimmage kick requirements, it is enforced from the previous spot and results in an automatic first down. If Team A were to decline the holding foul, Team B would instead take the ball at the illegal touching spot, the B-20.

question_text: 4/10 at the A-6. B66 blocks the punt and the ball bounces into Team A's end zone. A20 bats the ball from the end zone into the field of play, and it goes out of bounds at the A-5. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 @ A-5, Snap, with no safety since Team A never had impetus into their own end zone.
choice_c: Safety, 2 points for Team B, with no option for Team B to instead decline and take the ball at the A-5.
choice_d: A, 1/10 @ A-6, Snap. The result of the play is a touchback for Team A instead of a safety.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, 2 points for Team B. Team B blocked the punt, giving the ball impetus into Team A's end zone, and A20's illegal bat is a foul in the end zone. The result is a safety, 2 points for Team B, with Team A then kicking off from the A-20 — or Team B can instead decline the foul and take the result of the play, a first down at the A-5, with the down replayed.

question_text: Team A scores a touchdown on the first possession series of overtime. On the try, B5 lines up two yards beyond the line of scrimmage and attempts to jump through the A-gap, making contact with the center and left guard as they rise up after the snap. B5 falls over the top of them and into the backfield. The try is successful. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the 40, or Team A can decline the score and re-attempt the try, half the distance, after enforcement.
choice_b: The try is confirmed as successful with no foul assessed, since B5's contact occurred after the snap.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the 40. The leaping foul is enforced as a spot foul with an automatic first down for Team A.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the 40, with no option for Team A to decline the score and re-attempt the try.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the 40, or Team A can decline the score and re-attempt the try, half the distance, after enforcement. B5 commits a foul for leaping. This personal foul on the try may be enforced from the succeeding spot in extra periods, or Team A can decline the score and repeat the try, half the distance, after enforcement.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the B-44 in the second quarter with the game clock at 0:07 and Team A out of timeouts. Team A completes a pass to the B-28 and the game clock expires. Video shows that when the receiver completed the catch with his knee on the ground, the game clock showed 0:03. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 at the B-28. The game clock reading stands at 0:00 since this is not a reviewable play.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the B-28, reset the game clock to 0:03, with the play clock starting on the ready for play.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-28, reset the game clock to 0:03 minus a mandatory 10-second runoff for the clock stoppage.
choice_d: B ball at the B-28. The play is blown dead with the clock expiring, ending the half before review can occur.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 at the B-28, reset the game clock to 0:03, with the play clock starting on the ready for play. This play is reviewable to determine whether time expired before the ball became dead. There isn't a 10-second subtraction on this play since the result of the play is a first down. If time expires in a half and the game clock would start on the Referee's signal after review, there must be at least 3 seconds remaining when the ball should have been declared dead in order to restore time.

question_text: Team A attempts a field goal on fourth down from the B-13 with 0:10 remaining in the second quarter. Team B blocks the kick at the line of scrimmage and B0 recovers the ball at the B-6. During the kick, B99 removes his helmet at the B-10 while celebrating the block, then puts it back on and continues to play, joining B4 to block A1 at the B-15 and springing B0 for an open path to the end zone. B2 also blocks A3 into the sideline, causing the head linesman to fall. B0 crosses the goal line after time has expired. Following the score, B99 removes his helmet again and taunts Team A. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown stands for Team B, with B99 assessed a single unsportsmanlike foul enforced on the try.
choice_b: No score for Team B, first down at the B-1.5, but B99 is not ejected since the two fouls occur on different sides of the change of possession.
choice_c: No score for Team B. The period is extended, Team B has a first down at the B-1.5, and B99 is ejected for two unsportsmanlike fouls.
choice_d: No score for Team B, first down at the B-1.5, with the period not extended since the foul occurs after the change of possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score for Team B. The period is extended, Team B has a first down at the B-1.5, and B99 is ejected for two unsportsmanlike fouls. B99 commits a live-ball unsportsmanlike foul for removing his helmet during the play, then a live-ball unnecessary roughness foul for putting his helmet back on and continuing to participate as he blocks A1. Only one of these fouls can be enforced, and the unsportsmanlike foul is enforced as a post-scrimmage kick foul from the B-6, half the distance. Because Team B fouled following the change of possession, the score does not count. Following the score, B99 commits a second unsportsmanlike foul and is ejected from the game. This foul is enforced half the distance following enforcement of the live-ball unsportsmanlike foul. The period will be extended due to the acceptance of the live-ball foul.

question_text: Team A leads 24-21 late in the fourth quarter with a second-and-5 at the A-25. A44 runs for ten yards before being tackled in the field of play. During the run, A55 holds B1 at the A-22. The game clock reads 1:30 at the end of the play. What is the ruling, including clock status?
choice_a: A, 2/15 at the A-15, with the clock required to start on the ready for play regardless of the score.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the A-35. The holding foul is declined since the run gained more yardage than the penalty would cost.
choice_c: B ball at the A-22, with loss of down assessed against Team A for the holding foul.
choice_d: A, 2/15 at the A-15, with the clock starting on the snap at the option of Team B.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/15 at the A-15, with the clock starting on the snap at the option of Team B. A55 commits a holding foul behind the line of scrimmage. This foul by rule is enforced from the previous spot. Team B has the option, since they are behind in the score, to start the clock on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the A-40. QB A12 takes the snap and runs an RPO to his right, with A66 pulling and leading the play. B55 blocks A66 in the knees at the A-42, and A12 is driven out of bounds at the 50. What is the ruling?
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the 45. The block below the waist is enforced from the previous spot rather than the succeeding spot.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the 50. The block below the waist foul is offset by an illegal formation foul on Team A.
choice_d: B ball at the 50. B55's block below the waist results in a change of possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 at the B-35. B55 commits a foul for blocking below the waist. This foul is enforced 15 yards from the succeeding spot at the 50.

question_text: Team A kicks off from the A-35 with 0:05 remaining in the fourth quarter. A99 kicks the ball toward the right sideline. The ball bounces at the B-10 and goes through the legs of B15 and out of bounds at the B-5. The on-field officials rule that the ball touched B15 and wind the clock, which runs to 0:00. Replay has video evidence that the ball did not touch B15. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-5, snap, with the game clock reset to 0:05 since the ball is dead where it left the field of play.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-35, snap, with the game clock reset to 0:05, or Team A can re-kick from the A-30 with the clock reset to 0:05.
choice_c: The ruling on the field stands, and the game ends with the clock at 0:00, since a kick going out of bounds is not reviewable.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-35, snap, with the clock remaining at 0:00 since the game has already ended.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-35, snap, with the game clock reset to 0:05, or Team A can re-kick from the A-30 with the clock reset to 0:05. This play is reviewable to determine if the ball was touched. With video evidence that the ball was not touched, this is a kick out of bounds, and Team B has the option to enforce the penalty from the previous spot or 30 yards beyond Team A's restraining line. The game clock will be reset to 0:05 following the replay reversal.

question_text: Team A has a second-and-5 at the A-30 with 0:10 remaining in the first quarter. QB A12 hands the ball off to A6 on a dive play up the middle. A6 is tackled by the facemask and brought down at the A-34, with time expiring during the run. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 at the A-49, with an untimed down awarded to Team A since a live-ball foul occurred as time expired.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the A-49, snap, with the first quarter extended by one down before changing ends of the field.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the A-49, snap, with the game clock reset to 15:00, and teams change ends of the field to start the second quarter.
choice_d: The period ends with the facemask foul carried over and enforced on the first play of the second quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 at the A-49, snap, with the game clock reset to 15:00, and teams change ends of the field to start the second quarter. By rule, a period shall be extended for an untimed down only if a penalty is accepted for a live-ball foul during a down in which time expires in the second or fourth quarter. Because this is the first quarter, the period is not extended. The foul is enforced prior to moving the chains, and then teams switch ends to start the second quarter.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the B-25 in the fourth quarter with 0:50 on the clock, the clock running, Team A out of timeouts and behind by four points. B55, lined up in the gap between the right tackle and the tight end, makes an abrupt lunge into the neutral zone directly into that gap. Right guard A60 comes up out of his stance and points at B55. The clock shows 0:43 when the play is blown dead. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/15 at the B-30, with a 10-second runoff automatically applied since Team A is out of timeouts.
choice_b: No foul on the play, since B55's encroachment did not directly threaten right guard A60.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-30, with the false start declined since it was caused by the defense.
choice_d: A, 1/15 at the B-30, with the clock at 0:33 and the ball ready for play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/15 at the B-30, with the clock at 0:33 and the ball ready for play. B55 jumps into the neutral zone, but he is in the gap between the right tackle and the tight end. Right guard A60 false starts due to B55 jumping into the neutral zone; however, A60 is not "protected" since B55 went into the gap between the right tackle and the tight end. Because there is a false start by A60, the clock stops and is subject to a potential 10-second subtraction at the option of Team B.

question_text: Team A has second-and-10 at the A-25. Shotgun QB A10 is under heavy duress and is contacted by B99 while attempting to throw a forward pass. The contact causes the ball to come loose before A10's hand starts forward. The ball hits the ground at the A-18, where B77 recovers it and carries it across Team A's goal line. The Referee and covering official both rule an incomplete pass. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Reviewable. Replay overturns the ruling to a fumble, and Team B has a first down at the A-18, ball ready on the snap.
choice_b: Not reviewable, since intentional grounding rulings and pass timing cannot be overturned by replay.
choice_c: Reviewable. Replay overturns the ruling to a fumble, and the touchdown by B77 stands since he legally advanced the loose ball.
choice_d: Reviewable. Replay overturns the ruling to a fumble, but the ball reverts to Team A at the spot of the fumble.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Reviewable. Replay overturns the ruling to a fumble, and Team B has a first down at the A-18, ball ready on the snap. Replay will overturn the ruling on the field of an incomplete pass. Because the ruling was overturned from an incomplete pass to a fumble, Team B is awarded the ball for the immediate recovery, but the advance and score are not allowed.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the A-20. Shotgun QB A1 throws a swing pass from the A-13 to WR A2, who catches the ball at the A-13. A2 looks upfield and throws a pass to A3, who catches the ball at the A-30 and runs for a touchdown. An official throws a flag for an illegal forward pass. Replay has video evidence that the pass from A1 was caught by A2 at the A-12. What is the ruling?
choice_a: The flag for the illegal forward pass stands, and Team A loses the down at the spot of A2's second throw.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-3.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A, but the try is moved back to the B-15 as a penalty for the original flag.
choice_d: Not reviewable, since the direction of the first pass cannot be determined by video review.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-3. Because this is a potential scoring play involving a ruling of a forward pass on the field, replay can review whether the first pass was forward or backward. After determining that the first pass was backward, the flag for an illegal forward pass is picked up, and the result of the play is a touchdown for Team A.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth-and-7 from the A-30. Punter A15 kicks the ball toward B1, who signals for a fair catch, misjudges the punt, and lets it go over his head. B1 then blocks A1 at the B-20, preventing A1 from downing the football. The ball bounces out of bounds at the B-6. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-6, at the spot where the ball went out of bounds, with no foul assessed.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the B-20, at the spot of B1's illegal block, with a change of possession.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-3, ball ready on the snap.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-3, but the penalty is enforced from the previous spot instead of the end of the kick.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-3, ball ready on the snap. B1 commits a foul for an illegal block following his valid fair catch signal. This foul is enforced as a post-scrimmage kick foul, half the distance from the end of the kick, which is the B-6.

question_text: Team A punts on fourth-and-8 from the A-30. The punt is caught at the B-30 and returned to the A-10, where the ball is fumbled. A88, already down, recovers the fumble at the A-8. During the kick, B66 holds at the B-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1/10 at the A-40, but the foul is enforced as a post-scrimmage kick foul from the end of the return.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the A-8, since the holding foul is declined because it occurred during the kick.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the A-8, with no adjustment for the holding foul since Team B never gained possession.
choice_d: A, 1/10 at the A-40, ball ready on the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 at the A-40, ball ready on the snap. B66 commits a foul for holding during the kick; however, this foul does not qualify for post-scrimmage kick enforcement since Team B is not in legal possession at the end of the play and will not next snap the ball. Fouls that occur during the kick play that are not governed by PSK enforcement are enforced from the previous spot, resulting in a first down for Team A.

question_text: Team A kicks off from the A-35. B45 gives a valid fair catch signal at the B-30, misjudges the kick, and the ball goes over his head, bounces at the B-5, and rolls into the end zone where B1 covers it. Prior to the ball entering the end zone, B45 blocks A80 below the waist at the B-24. Team A does not want to re-kick. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-12, ball ready on the snap.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-10, since the personal foul is enforced from the touchback spot at the B-20, half the distance.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-24, at the spot of the foul, with no adjustment for the touchback.
choice_d: A, 1/10 at the B-24, since the illegal block below the waist results in a change of possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-12, ball ready on the snap. B45 commits a foul for an illegal block following a valid fair catch signal. The foul is a block below the waist, which is a personal foul and will be enforced half the distance from the spot of the foul rather than the customary 10 yards. That foul is enforced from the B-24, since the result of the play is a touchback on a free kick, which places the ball at the B-25, not the B-20.

question_text: Following a safety, Team A free-kicks from the A-20. A99 punts the ball toward the right sideline. B1 moves up to catch the ball but loses his bearings and is standing with his left foot out of bounds at the B-30 and his right foot in the field of play when he catches the ball. An official whistles the play dead as soon as B1 catches the ball. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-30, ball ready on the snap, since the ball is dead at the spot where B1 caught it.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the 50 on the right hash, ball ready on the snap, or Team A can re-kick from the A-15, with the clock starting when the ball is legally touched in the field of play.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-30, since a player standing out of bounds who touches a kick forfeits possession to the kicking team.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the 50, but Team A has no option to re-kick since the ball was legally caught.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the 50 on the right hash, ball ready on the snap, or Team A can re-kick from the A-15, with the clock starting when the ball is legally touched in the field of play. B1 is standing out of bounds when he touches the ball, and because he is out of bounds and touches the ball, this is a kick out of bounds. The enforcement options are five yards from the previous spot and re-kick, 30 yards from the previous spot with the ball at the same lateral position, or five yards from the dead ball spot.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-10 at the A-20. A22 runs to the A-27, where he is hit by B1 and fumbles. B77, who was chasing A22 from behind, dives on the loose ball and recovers it at the A-30 with his right foot out of bounds. The on-field officials award the ball to Team B at the A-30. Replay has indisputable video evidence that B77 was out of bounds with his right foot when he recovered the football. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/3 at the A-27, ball ready on the snap, since the clock had been stopped for the change of possession.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the A-30 stands, since a fumble recovery spot near the sideline is not reviewable.
choice_c: A, 4/3 at the A-27, ball ready for play, with the clock starting on the ready.
choice_d: A, 4/13 at the A-20, with the ball returned to the spot of the fumble rather than where B77 stepped out.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/3 at the A-27, ball ready for play, with the clock starting on the ready. B77 is out of bounds when his right foot touches the sideline. Replay can review this play as it relates to a fumble recovery near the sideline. Replay overturns the call on the field, and this becomes a forward fumble out of bounds, brought back to the spot of the fumble, giving Team A a fourth down at the A-27. The clock starts on the ready for play.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-1 at the B-18, late in the fourth quarter, trailing 22-14, with neither team holding a timeout. QB A7 drops back and quickly throws a forward pass that is knocked down at the line of scrimmage by B95. A7 catches the ball before it hits the ground and rolls to his right. Seeing no open receivers, A7 throws a second pass from the B-25 that lands out of bounds at the B-5. After A7 releases the pass, B1 takes two steps and delivers a hit into A7's shoulder, knocking him to the ground. When the ball hits the ground, the clock reads 0:22. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/1 at the B-18, with a 10-second runoff applied for the incomplete illegal forward pass.
choice_b: B ball at the B-25, with Team A losing the down for the illegal forward pass and no roughing the passer foul recognized.
choice_c: A, 3/6 at the B-13, with only the roughing the passer foul enforced, from the previous spot.
choice_d: A, 3/1 at the B-18, ball ready on the snap, with the clock at 0:22.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/1 at the B-18, ball ready on the snap, with the clock at 0:22. A7 commits a foul for an illegal forward pass when he throws the second forward pass. B1 commits a foul for roughing the passer when he takes two steps and hits A7 in the shoulder with a punishing hit. There isn't a rule stating that a passer of an illegal forward pass is not protected from being roughed. While the incomplete illegal forward pass would potentially carry a 10-second subtraction, the addition of the roughing the passer foul negates that possibility, and both fouls offset with the clock starting on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the A-20. QB A12 throws a pass that is intercepted at the A-45 by B2, who runs down the right sideline and is tackled by the facemask at the A-31. During B2's return, B99 illegally blindside blocks A7 at the A-15. The Referee announces that B99's block is a combination foul for an illegal blindside block with targeting, and B99 is disqualified while the play is under further review. Replay has indisputable video evidence that B99's contact was to the shoulder of A7. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the A-46, ball ready on the snap, with B99 not disqualified.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the A-31, with B99 remaining disqualified since the blindside block foul still stands.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the A-46, with B99 disqualified since targeting was confirmed by the Referee's initial announcement.
choice_d: A, 1/10 at the A-31, since the facemask foul on B2's return offsets the blindside block.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the A-46, ball ready on the snap, with B99 not disqualified. There are two fouls on this play, one by each team, both after the change of possession: a Team A facemask at the A-31, and a blindside block with targeting by B99. The targeting component of a combination foul is reviewable. With replay overturning the targeting component, the blindside block still applies and is enforced from the A-31. The facemask foul is declined, since Team B wants to keep the ball. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: Team A free-kicks from the A-35. Team A sets up for an onside kick with 0:50 on the clock in the fourth quarter, trailing by four points. A99 squibs the ball toward the sideline, and B41 recovers the kick and drops immediately to the ground at the A-46. An official has a flag down for Team B having two players wearing #3 during the play. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, kickoff from the A-45, with the clock remaining at 0:50 since the ball never became loose.
choice_b: A, kickoff from the A-40, with the clock set to 0:49.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the A-46, with the duplicate-number foul declined since Team B recovered the kick.
choice_d: A, kickoff from the A-40, with the clock reset to 0:50 since the foul stops the clock.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, kickoff from the A-40, with the clock set to 0:49. The clock is set to 0:49, since B41 recovered the ball and then dropped to the ground. Team B's foul for having two players participate wearing the same number is enforced five yards from the previous spot, as outlined in the penalty statement.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-goal at the B-7. Shotgun QB A7 receives the snap, and in his attempt to hand the ball off to RB A1, fumbles the ball. The ball is picked up by A1 at the B-10, who runs to his right and throws a forward pass to A2 in the end zone. The on-field officials signal touchdown. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A7 controlled the snap and fumbled the ball prior to the recovery by A1. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands, since the fumble recovery by a teammate on fourth down is not reviewable.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-7, at the spot of the fumble, since the ball must return to where possession was lost.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-10, ball ready on the snap.
choice_d: A, 4/1 at the B-1, since the fumble recovery and subsequent pass are treated as a continuation of the same down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-10, ball ready on the snap. The result of the play is a potential touchdown, which is reviewable in all aspects. The recovery of a fumble on fourth down is also reviewable to determine if this is a fumble versus a muff. After determining that this is indeed a fumble, the score does not count, since the ball is dead as soon as A1 recovers, and it is brought back to the spot of recovery. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: First possession series of the third overtime period, second-and-8 at the B-15. Team A lines up with trips receivers off the line in the backfield. QB A14, in the shotgun with RB A21 to his left, takes the snap, rolls right, and throws toward A6. B4 intercepts the pass at the B-8 and returns it for a touchdown. During the return, B62 forcibly contacts A81 outside his field of vision, knocking him down at the A-35. Replay has indisputable video evidence that B62's contact was to the head and neck area of A81. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown stands for Team B, with B62 disqualified for targeting after the score is confirmed.
choice_b: No score for Team B, Team B has a first down at the B-8, with the foul enforced from the previous spot.
choice_c: No score for Team B, Team A retains the ball at the A-35 due to the targeting foul.
choice_d: No score for Team B. Team B has a first down at the A-35, and B62 is disqualified.
explanation: The correct ruling is: No score for Team B. Team B has a first down at the A-35, and B62 is disqualified. Any illegal forward pass and blindside block components on this play are declined by rule. The targeting foul created by replay is enforced from the succeeding spot, giving Team B a first down at the spot where the contact occurred. B62 is disqualified by rule.

question_text: Team A's fourth-and-10 field goal attempt from the B-18 is blocked behind the line of scrimmage. The blocked kick floats downfield and hits the ground at the B-10, where B28 recovers and advances to the B-20. During the kick, A88 holds B1 on the edge to prevent him from blocking the kick. What is the ruling?
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-28, with the holding foul tacked on to the end of the return.
choice_c: A retains possession at the B-18, since the holding foul negates the blocked kick recovery.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-10, at the spot of recovery, with the holding foul enforced from there.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-20. The hold by A88 is declined by Team B, since it cannot be tacked on to the end of the return — it occurred during a field goal attempt.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-8 at the A-35. QB A1, under a heavy rush, scrambles up the middle and to his left. As he scrambles, he and the ball go completely across the A-37, and he then retreats backward to the A-35, where he throws a forward pass to A2 at the A-45. A2 had stepped out of bounds on his own, re-established himself inbounds prior to the pass being thrown, and completed the catch at the A-45. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 4/13 at the A-30, with A2 also flagged for illegal touching for going out of bounds on his own.
choice_c: B ball at the A-45, since A1's retreat behind the line of scrimmage makes the pass a backward pass, and A2 fumbled after the catch.
choice_d: A, 3/3 at the A-45, since the catch is legal and no foul is recognized for A1's retreat.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/13 at the A-30. A1 commits a foul for an illegal forward pass, since he and the ball went completely beyond the line of scrimmage and then retreated behind it before he threw a forward pass. This foul is enforced from the spot of the foul and is a loss of down. A2 does not commit a foul for illegal touching, even though he went out of bounds on his own, since that rule specifically applies only to a legal forward pass, and this pass was illegal to begin with.

question_text: Late in the second quarter, Team A has a third-and-10 at the B-35. While scrambling, QB A1 fumbles the ball as he is being tackled at the B-43. B77 recovers it while on the ground at the B-45. The game clock is at 0:30 when the on-field officials award the ball to Team B at the end of the down. Replay has indisputable video evidence that A1's knee was down prior to the fumble. Additionally, B56's helmet came off during the down. What is the ruling, including the game clock?
choice_a: A, 4/18 at the B-43, with a mandatory 10-second runoff applied because a player's helmet came off during the down.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-45 stands, since a knee-down ruling on a fumble is not reviewable.
choice_c: A, 4/18 at the B-43, with no adjustment to the game clock and no 10-second runoff for B56's helmet coming off; the clock remains at 0:30 and starts on the ready for play.
choice_d: A, 4/18 at the B-43, with the game clock reset to 0:40 to account for the review stoppage.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/18 at the B-43, with no adjustment to the game clock and no 10-second runoff for B56's helmet coming off; the clock remains at 0:30 and starts on the ready for play. Replay overturns the ruling to a fumble recovery negated by the runner being down by contact, giving Team A a fourth down at the B-43. There is no adjustment to the game clock and no 10-second runoff, since a helmet coming off does not trigger a runoff. B56 must sit out for one play unless Team B uses a timeout to bring him back in.

question_text: Team A has a second-and-11 at the A-49. QB A8 drops straight back into the pocket and, while under pressure from the A-41, throws a pass downfield that lands in the field of play at the B-21, with no eligible receivers in the area. As A8 dropped back into the pocket, A77 jerked B88 to the ground at the A-42. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 2/21 at the A-39, with only the offensive holding foul enforced.
choice_b: A, 3/19 at the A-41, with both fouls offsetting and the down replayed.
choice_c: B ball at the A-41, with intentional grounding resulting in a turnover on downs rather than a loss of down.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/19 at the A-41. Team A commits multiple live-ball fouls on this play. A77 commits a holding foul, enforced 10 yards from the previous spot. However, that foul will most likely be declined, since A8 has committed intentional grounding, being under duress, still in the tackle box, and throwing a pass into an area without an eligible receiver. This foul is a spot foul that carries a loss of down and is the more favorable option for Team B.

question_text: Team A has a second-and-8 at the A-37 with 1:59 remaining in the second quarter. The clock is stopped, the score is tied, and both teams have three timeouts remaining. QB A2 takes the shotgun snap and steps up into the pocket. Under a heavy rush by B99, A2 turns and runs toward his own end zone without ever leaving the tackle box. B99 grabs A2 in the nameplate area and abruptly pulls him to the ground at the A-31. At the snap, B99 is flagged for lining up in the neutral zone. The clock reads 1:52 at the end of the play. What is the ruling, including clock status?
choice_a: A, 2/3 at the A-42, with the clock starting on the snap at the option of Team A.
choice_b: A, 2/3 at the A-42, with a horse collar tackle foul also assessed against B99 for grabbing A2 in the nameplate area.
choice_c: A, 2/3 at the A-42, with the clock required to start on the ready for play regardless of the score.
choice_d: A, 1/10 at the A-42, with an automatic first down awarded for a horse collar tackle foul.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/3 at the A-42, with the clock starting on the snap at the option of Team A. B99 commits a foul for being lined up in the neutral zone at the snap. B99 does grab A2 in the nameplate area; however, A2 is still in the tackle box when tackled, so this is not a foul for a horse collar tackle under the rule as it currently stands. Because the score is tied, Team A has the option to start the clock on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-10 at the A-5, with 1:50 remaining in the fourth quarter and the score A 30 – B 28. Punter A1, standing very close to his own end line, receives the snap and punts the ball. B88 catches the kick at the 50 and is tackled at the A-45. A79 is flagged for holding in Team A's end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety is the only available result, since the foul occurred in Team A's own end zone.
choice_b: Team B has the option to enforce the holding foul at the succeeding spot or take a safety.
choice_c: The succeeding spot enforcement is the only option, since a safety cannot be awarded on a foul that occurs during a kick.
choice_d: Team B must enforce the foul from the previous spot inside Team A's end zone, resulting in a safety by rule.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B has the option to enforce the holding foul at the succeeding spot or take a safety. Team B has two options. A79's holding foul can be enforced either at the succeeding spot or it will result in a safety. Both options need to be presented to Team B's head coach given the time and score. If safety is selected, it's two points for Team B and a safety kickoff from the A-20.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-15 at the B-25, trailing 17-3, with 0:42 remaining in the second quarter. Team A has one timeout, and Team B has none. A83 catches a pass at the B-24 and runs to the B-10, where he is tackled inbounds. Replay stops the game with 0:29 remaining to review the spot and determines that the runner was down at the B-11, inbounds, with 0:36 remaining on the clock. What is the ruling, including all clock options?
choice_a: A, 4/1 at the B-11, with the clock reset to 0:36 and no runoff option available since the reversal favors the offense.
choice_b: A, 4/1 at the B-11, with the clock automatically reset to 0:26 and no option for Team A to use a timeout to avoid the runoff.
choice_c: A, 4/1 at the B-11, with the clock reset to 0:36; if Team A does not use a timeout, the clock is set to 0:26 and starts on the ready for play, or Team A can use its last timeout to keep the clock at 0:36, starting on the snap.
choice_d: A, 4/1 at the B-11, with the clock remaining at 0:29, since the review stopped the game, regardless of timeout usage.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 4/1 at the B-11, with the clock reset to 0:36; if Team A does not use a timeout, the clock is set to 0:26 and starts on the ready for play, or Team A can use its last timeout to keep the clock at 0:36, starting on the snap. If Team A does not take a timeout to avoid the runoff, the clock is set to 0:26 and starts on the ready for play with the play clock at 0:25. If Team A uses its last timeout to avoid the 10-second runoff, the clock stays at 0:36 and starts on the snap with the play clock set to 0:25.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-15 at the A-20. Team A's punt goes beyond the neutral zone and is first touched by A80 at the B-30, then picked up by B88 at the B-20. B88 advances five yards to the B-25, where he fumbles. B99 holds at the B-35 during B88's run. A98 recovers the fumble and advances for a touchdown. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands, since the illegal touching by A80 does not affect a later fumble recovery by Team A.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-30, at the spot of the illegal touch, with the holding foul declined by rule.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-25, with the touchdown negated but the ball awarded to Team A at the spot of the fumble.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-15. A80 commits an illegal touch, being the first to touch the football beyond the neutral zone. B88 legally recovers and advances the ball; during his run, B99 commits a holding foul. B88 then fumbles, and the ball is legally recovered by A98, who advances for a touchdown, but the score does not count given the illegal touch. Because of the illegal touching by Team A, the penalty for Team B's holding foul may be enforced; Team A has the option to decline the penalty or enforce it from the B-25, the end of the related run. Enforcing the foul is the better option for Team A.

question_text: Team A has a first down at the B-35. A80 runs a go route up the sideline. As the ball is in flight, A80 steps out of bounds at the B-5 and jumps from out of bounds for the ball. B7 tips the ball in flight; A80 catches it, lands inbounds at the B-2, and dives for the pylon, touching it with his left hand as the ball crosses out of bounds over the half-yard line. What is the ruling?
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, since A80 touched the pylon with possession of the football.
choice_c: B ball at the B-5, since A80 commits illegal touching by going out of bounds on his own and touching the pass.
choice_d: A, 2/10 at the B-35, with A80 also flagged for offensive pass interference for jumping from out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 2/10 at the B-35. A80 goes out of bounds on his own and therefore becomes an ineligible receiver. He does not re-establish himself inbounds and is still airborne when he touches the ball. Because he did not re-establish himself inbounds, the pass is ruled incomplete once he touches it, rather than illegal touching. B7 does touch the ball, but because A80 is still considered an out-of-bounds player, the ball is also considered out of bounds once he touches it. The clock starts on the snap following the incomplete pass.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-5 at the B-18. QB A15 throws a pass toward Team B's end zone. B38 jumps and secures firm control of the pass near the goal line, lands inside the B-1, and in one motion falls to the ground in the end zone. Just after A15 releases the pass, B99, rushing unabated, launches and hits A15 in the head with his forearm, knocking him to the ground. The Referee announces roughing the passer with targeting, and A15 is injured on the play. What is the ruling, including reviewability and clock status?
choice_a: Touchback for Team B stands, with B99's roughing the passer with targeting foul carried over to Team B's ensuing possession.
choice_b: A, 1st and goal at the B-9, ball ready on the snap, with B99 disqualified.
choice_c: A, 1st and goal at the B-9, with the clock starting on the ready for play instead of the snap.
choice_d: Interception stands at the B-1, with B99's foul enforced from the dead-ball spot rather than the previous spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1st and goal at the B-9, ball ready on the snap, with B99 disqualified. For interceptions inside the B-1, when in question of where possession was gained, the play is made a touchback. The result of the play is a touchback; however, B99 commits a foul for roughing the passer with targeting, since the contact to A15 is to the head and neck area. This play is reviewable, and B99 is disqualified. The foul is enforced from the previous spot, half the distance to the goal, with an automatic first down for Team A. Because we have one incident causing the clock to start on the ready and one on the snap, the clock starts on the snap. A15 must sit out for one play due to his injury.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-10 at the A-40. Punter A10 muffs the snap, and the ball rolls past him toward the sideline. A10 picks up the ball on a sprint just outside the numbers and, a couple of yards from the sideline, turns to punt the ball at the A-30. As he extends the ball to kick it, he is hit by a chasing defender, and the ball drops to the ground as he completely misses it. A22, trailing the action, recovers the grounded ball at the A-30 and punts it downfield; the ball hits the ground and rolls out of bounds at the B-40. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-40, at the spot where the punt by A22 went out of bounds.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the A-30, since A22's recovery and successful kick advance Team A's possession.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the A-30, ball ready on the snap.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the A-8, at the spot of the original muffed snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the A-30, ball ready on the snap. A10 muffs the initial snap but recovers it near the sideline and attempts to kick the ball. He misses the ball and is therefore guilty of fumbling it. Since it is fourth down, only A10 can legally recover and advance the football. When A22 recovers the football, the play is dead by rule. Team A is in possession but did not make the line to gain, and Team B will put the ball in play at the spot of recovery. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-8 at the B-38. A7 completes a legal forward pass to A9, who advances to the B-4, where he is hit and fumbles near the sideline. B37 recovers the fumble in the end zone and is tackled immediately. The Referee flags B99 for roughing the passer. Officials discuss whether the ball hit the pylon prior to B37's recovery. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball did hit the pylon prior to B37's recovery. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 1st and goal at the B-4, with the roughing the passer foul enforced from the spot of the fumble.
choice_b: Touchback for Team B stands, since the ball hitting the pylon after Team A's fumble results in a change of possession.
choice_c: A, 1st and goal at the B-2, with the roughing the passer foul instead carried over to the extra point attempt.
choice_d: A, 1st and goal at the B-2.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1st and goal at the B-2. The ball hitting the pylon results in a touchback but does not result in a change of possession during the down, since the ball was still loose after Team A's fumble. Therefore, by rule, the roughing the passer foul is enforced from the end of the last run, which is the B-4.

question_text: Team A has second-and-goal at the B-7. A22 takes the handoff and runs around the right end. B99 tackles A22 at the B-3, causing him to fumble the ball. The airborne ball goes out of bounds inside the pylon and through the end zone. The on-field officials rule that the ball went out of bounds in the field of play and bring it back to the B-3. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball was inside the pylon before going out of bounds. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-20, ball ready on the snap.
choice_b: The ruling on the field stands, since a fumble's location relative to the pylon is not reviewable.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-3 stands, since the ball remains Team A's possession regardless of where it crossed the goal line.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-3, at the spot of the fumble, since the ball is dead there once ruled out of bounds.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-20, ball ready on the snap. This play is reviewable regarding where the ball went out of bounds in relation to the goal line. With indisputable video evidence, replay overturns the ruling on the field from a fumble forward out of bounds to a touchback, resulting in a first down for Team B at the B-20 with the clock on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-7 at the A-45. A12's punt is blocked by B89. The ball is rolling at the A-43 when B78 bats it to the B-49, where B99 recovers it and advances to the A-10 before being forced out of bounds. Team A had five players in the backfield at the snap. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1st and goal at the A-10, at the spot where B99 went out of bounds, since the illegal formation is declined.
choice_b: B, 1st and goal at the A-5.
choice_c: A retains the ball at the A-45, since B78's bat of the loose ball is an illegal bat that returns possession to Team A.
choice_d: B, 1st and goal at the A-5, but the illegal formation foul can only be enforced from the previous spot, not the succeeding spot.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1st and goal at the A-5. Team A commits a foul for an illegal formation. That foul can be enforced either at the previous spot or the subsequent dead-ball spot where Team B will next put the ball in play, since the scrimmage kick crossed the neutral zone. The bat by B78 is legal, since it is backward, and while it creates a new impetus, it does not change the status of the ball and therefore remains a kick.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-10 at the A-9, with 2:00 remaining in the second quarter and the score tied 14-14. B58 blocks the punt, and the ball bounces into Team A's end zone. A33 bats the ball from the end zone into the field of play, and it goes out of bounds at the A-8. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Safety, two points for Team B, with no option for Team B to decline the foul and take the result of the play instead.
choice_b: First and goal for Team B at the A-8, ball ready on the snap, with no safety recognized since the ball never had impetus into the end zone from a kick.
choice_c: Safety, two points for Team B, with a safety kickoff from the A-20, or Team B can decline the foul and take the result of the play, first and goal at the A-8, ball ready on the snap.
choice_d: Safety, two points for Team B, with the foul tacked on from the A-8 since the ball crossed the neutral zone before the block.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Safety, two points for Team B, with a safety kickoff from the A-20, or Team B can decline the foul and take the result of the play, first and goal at the A-8, ball ready on the snap. Team B blocked the punt, giving the ball impetus into Team A's end zone, and A33's illegal bat is a foul in the end zone. The result is a safety, with Team A then kicking off from the A-20, or Team B can instead decline the foul and take the result of the play, a first down at the A-8. The foul cannot be tacked on from the A-8, since the ball never crossed the neutral zone.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-9 at the A-25. A1 punts the ball toward the left side of the field. The ball hits the ground near the sideline at the B-27. B1 picks up the bouncing ball at the B-25 and advances for a touchdown. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball hit the sideline at the B-27, with 1:10 remaining on the clock in the second quarter when the ball hit the ground. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team B stands, since a punt hitting the ground near the sideline is not reviewable.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-25 stands, at the spot where B1 legally picked up the loose ball.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-27, with the clock remaining at 1:10 and starting on the ready for play instead of the snap.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-27, with the clock reset to 1:10 following the review, since this is under two minutes in the second quarter.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-27, with the clock reset to 1:10 following the review, since this is under two minutes in the second quarter. This play is reviewable, since the ball hit the sideline and is, by rule, out of bounds. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-7 at the B-12. B23 intercepts a forward pass, returns the ball to the B-20, and fumbles. A42 recovers the fumble and advances to the B-15, where he fumbles. A35 recovers the ball and advances it into the end zone. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-3.
choice_b: B ball at the B-15, since the fourth-down fumble rule prevents a teammate from recovering and advancing after the second fumble.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A stands, but the try is instead moved to the B-15 as a penalty for the multiple fumbles.
choice_d: A ball at the B-15, first down, since Team A must be given a fresh set of downs rather than a score after multiple fumbles.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-3. While it is fourth down, the fourth-down fumble rule does not apply after a change of team possession, so A35's recovery and advance for a touchdown stands.

question_text: Team A trails 28-21 and throws a desperation pass on the last timed down of the game, from a third-and-12 at the B-37. The ball is caught by A88 in the end zone for a touchdown. After releasing the pass, QB A8 was roughed by B55. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A, with the roughing the passer foul automatically enforced on the try and no option to carry it into overtime.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A, with the roughing the passer foul enforced either on the try, at the B-1.5, or carried over to the first possession series of overtime, at Team A's choice.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team A, with the roughing the passer foul declined by rule since the game-ending down cannot carry a penalty forward.
choice_d: No score for Team A, since the roughing the passer foul negates a touchdown scored after the passer released the ball.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, with the roughing the passer foul enforced either on the try, at the B-1.5, or carried over to the first possession series of overtime, at Team A's choice. B55 commits a foul for roughing the passer, which by rule can be enforced on the try, resulting in the try at the B-1.5, or it can be carried over to the first possession series of overtime. Both choices need to be presented to Team A's head coach.

question_text: Team A has a second-and-4 at the B-38 in the fourth quarter with 0:52 remaining, the clock running, and both teams out of timeouts. Team A trails 24-21. QB A1 receives the shotgun snap and throws a quick pass to A81 at 0:48, who catches the ball at the B-35, turns upfield, and is pushed out of bounds at the B-30 with 0:43 showing on the clock. Replay stops the game to review the catch and has indisputable video evidence that A81 caught the ball with his left knee on the ground at the B-35. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/1 at the B-35, with the clock reset to 0:43 and no 10-second subtraction, since the review reversal favors the offense.
choice_b: A, 3/1 at the B-30 stands, since the ruling of a completed catch with forward progress is not reviewable.
choice_c: A, 3/1 at the B-35, with the clock reset to 0:48, a 10-second subtraction applied, putting the clock at 0:38, starting on the ready for play.
choice_d: A, 3/1 at the B-35, with the clock reset to 0:48 and starting on the snap rather than the ready for play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/1 at the B-35, with the clock reset to 0:48, a 10-second subtraction applied, putting the clock at 0:38, starting on the ready for play. This play is reviewable regarding the completion of the catch. Replay overturns the ruling on the field to down by rule. Because both teams are out of timeouts and the replay reversal changed the clock from stopped to running, a 10-second subtraction is included. The game clock is reset to 0:48, 10 seconds are subtracted, putting the clock at 0:38, and it starts on the ready for play.

question_text: Team A has a third-and-10 at the 50. A88 is blocking downfield when A82 catches a pass at the B-48. Two officials throw flags for offensive pass interference. Before the snap, B22 was confused and attempting to leave the field, but is still two steps from the sideline at the snap. No flags are thrown for that from the other officials. Replay has indisputable video evidence that B22 was the 12th player for Team B. What is the ruling?
choice_a: A, 3/5 at the 50, with only the illegal substitution foul enforced and the OPI declined.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-48, with the offensive pass interference foul enforced and the illegal substitution declined.
choice_c: A, 3/10 at the 50, with the illegal substitution foul not recognized, since replay cannot be used to count players on the field.
choice_d: A, 3/10 at the 50, with the down replayed after the two fouls offset.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 3/10 at the 50, with the down replayed after the two fouls offset. A88 commits a foul for offensive pass interference. Team B commits a foul for illegal substitution, having more than 11 players on the field. The number of players on the field is reviewable. These two fouls offset, and the down is replayed.

question_text: On the first possession series of the first overtime period, Team A has second-and-goal at the B-5. A32 runs to the goal line, where he is tackled by the facemask by B55. The officials spot the ball at the B-1 and signal first down. Replay has indisputable video evidence that the ball broke the plane of the goal line before A32 was down. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-1.5, or the 15-yard facemask foul can be carried over to enforce on Team B's ensuing possession series.
choice_b: Touchdown for Team A stands, but the facemask foul cannot be enforced anywhere since the down ended in a score.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-1 stands, since the spot of a runner relative to the goal line is not reviewable.
choice_d: Touchdown for Team A, with the facemask foul mandatorily enforced on the try and no option to carry it over.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Touchdown for Team A, with the try at the B-1.5, or the 15-yard facemask foul can be carried over to enforce on Team B's ensuing possession series. This play is reviewable, since it is a potential scoring play. Replay confirms the ball broke the plane of the goal line before A32 was down, resulting in a touchdown for Team A. The 15-yard facemask foul can be enforced on the try or carried over to Team B's possession series.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-7 at the B-43. A5's punt hits the ground at the B-2 and is across the goal line and over the end zone when A88 bats the ball back into the field of play. The kick is recovered by B33 at the B-5 and returned to the B-25, where he is hit and fumbles. A44 recovers the loose ball and runs across Team B's goal line. During the kick, B44 held A66 at the B-28. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Touchdown for Team A stands, since B44's holding foul during the kick negates Team B's later fumble recovery rights.
choice_b: A, 1/10 at the B-33, with no score for Team A.
choice_c: A, 1/10 at the B-28, at the spot of B44's holding foul, with the touchdown negated.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the B-5, with the illegal touching by A88 resulting in a change of possession at the spot of the bat.
explanation: The correct ruling is: A, 1/10 at the B-33, with no score for Team A. No score for Team A. Five- and 10-yard fouls are declined by rule unless enforcement is made possible by illegal touching of a kick. B44 commits a foul for holding during the kick. Fouls that occur during the kick and are not governed by PSK rules are enforced from the previous spot. The illegal touching goes away with an accepted penalty, and the holding foul is enforced 10 yards from the previous spot with an automatic first down.

question_text: Team A kicks off from the A-35. A1 kicks the ball high and short. B1 gives a valid fair catch signal and then muffs the ball at the B-23. The ball bounces off B1's shoulder pad and is caught in the air by B1 at the B-26. A80 was offside on the kickoff. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the B-26, at the spot where B1 completed the catch, with the offside foul declined.
choice_b: B, 1/10 at the B-23, at the spot of the initial muff, with no option for Team A to re-kick.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the B-30, ball ready on the snap, or Team A can kick off again from the A-30.
choice_d: A, 1/10 at the B-30, since the offside foul by Team A results in a loss of possession.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the B-30, ball ready on the snap, or Team A can kick off again from the A-30. B1 gave a valid fair catch signal and is afforded the opportunity to complete the catch prior to the ball touching the ground. The process of the catch was completed at the B-26. Because this is a free kick, the ball is relocated to the B-25, and the offside foul by A80 is enforced from there, with the clock on the snap. The offside foul can also be enforced from the previous spot at the option of Team B.

question_text: Team A has a fourth-and-10 at the 50. A99 sets up in a legal scrimmage kick formation at the A-36. The snap goes high and over his head. A99 retreats backward, recovers the ball at the A-30, and as he is kicking it, gets hit and knocked down by B99. The contact causes the ball to flutter downfield and bounce out of bounds at the A-45. An official has a flag down for an illegal formation by Team A. What is the ruling?
choice_a: B, 1/10 at the A-45, with B99 also flagged for roughing the kicker since contact occurred during the kicking motion.
choice_b: A retains the ball at the A-30, since the illegal formation foul is tacked on to the spot of the botched snap.
choice_c: B, 1/10 at the A-36, with the illegal formation foul tacked on to the succeeding spot since the kick crossed the neutral zone.
choice_d: B, 1/10 at the A-45, ball ready on the snap.
explanation: The correct ruling is: B, 1/10 at the A-45, ball ready on the snap. B99 does not commit a foul for roughing the kicker, since A99 recovers and possesses the ball more than five yards behind his original starting position. Team A commits a foul for illegal formation; however, this foul cannot be tacked on to the succeeding spot, since the kick did not cross the neutral zone. The clock starts on the snap.

question_text: First possession series of the first overtime period, score tied 24-24, Team A has second-and-5 at the B-20. QB A1 throws a pass that is intercepted by B22. During B22's return for a touchdown, a covering official runs into the Team B head coach, who is on the field, and drops a flag for sideline interference. What is the ruling?
choice_a: Team B wins, and the game is over; the touchdown counts, since sideline interference is enforced as a dead-ball foul.
choice_b: No score for Team B, since the sideline interference foul during the return negates the touchdown.
choice_c: Touchdown for Team B, but the game continues into a second overtime period since a foul occurred on the scoring return.
choice_d: Team B wins, and the game is over, but the head coach involved is also ejected for being on the field during the play.
explanation: The correct ruling is: Team B wins, and the game is over; the touchdown counts, since sideline interference is enforced as a dead-ball foul. Sideline interference is a live-ball foul that is enforced as a dead-ball foul, so by rule the score counts, and the game is over, since Team B scored during the first possession series of overtime.
